#### Unit 42.0 — Phase 42 prep (first cross-surface expansion of a framework consumer: wikilinks → all 4 surfaces; constructor-arg-only change; closes Phase-38 APPEND-D-L item 1; 9th anticipated ADR-0018 D-G APPEND)

- Phase 42 kickoff per `MASTER_PROMPT.md` §12 cardinal rule. Phase 41 closed at HEAD `939dcc6` (Unit 41.4 gate; thirty-second NON-§13 phase; 5 units; third concrete framework consumer realized + 3-of-3 slot demonstration complete + first 3-way composition end-to-end).
- §12 sign-off recorded **via "Continue" override** (**thirty-seventh invocation** of this pattern).
- §13 ledger status: still CLOSED. Phase 42 is the **thirty-third NON-§13 phase**.
- D-1 first-thread recommendation: **wikilinks cross-surface expansion to all 4 markdown surfaces via constructor-arg change in `PHASE_38_DEFAULT_ENABLED_SURFACES`**. Closes ADR-0018 APPEND-D-L item 1 (Phase 38 explicitly flagged "Cross-surface wikilink expansion — bio + reviewNotes + rationale wikilinks; demand-signal-first; constructor-arg change with zero plugin or registry rework"). **4-phase gap** from Phase-38 intent to Phase-42 realization; second prep-/APPEND-doc-level deferral closed by later phase (first was Phase-40 closing Phase-38-prep D-11).
- Why wikilinks over tables or arxiv (the three candidate consumers for cross-surface expansion): wikilinks has the strongest historical motivation (Phase-29 D-G REALIZED block + Phase-38 APPEND-D-L item 1 both specifically flag it by name); most navigational pattern (curators may reference problem slugs in bio + reviewNotes + rationale + actionRationale all four naturally); closes the oldest of the three Phase-N+1+ deferrals (D-L Phase 38 vs D-Q Phase 39 vs D-Y Phase 41).
- D-2: 0 new migrations anticipated. Pure constant-value change in a TypeScript module; no schema change.
- D-3 expansion shape pinned in prep doc: `PHASE_38_DEFAULT_ENABLED_SURFACES` value changes from `Set(["actionRationale"])` to `Set(["bio", "reviewNotes", "rationale", "actionRationale"])` (`MarkdownSurface` type-union order); `WikilinkExtensionRegistry` class UNCHANGED; factory dispatch arm UNCHANGED; constant name preserved (Phase 38 = introduction phase encoded in name; value evolves Phase 42; APPEND documents the audit trail).
- D-4: APPEND letter sequence — Phase 42 anticipates APPEND-D-Z (last single-letter slot before wrap to two-letter D-AA at Phase 43+); audit-trail-friendly single-letter sequence preserved through Phase 42.
- D-G composition matrix expanded: wikilinks-on-all-4 + tables-on-reviewNotes = SAME-surface different-slot composition on reviewNotes (wikilinks's rehypePlugins + tables's schemaOverrides) — **first canonical same-surface different-slot composition case under default dispatch** (Phase 41's 3-way example was disjoint-surface); wikilinks + arxiv on rationale = analogous same-surface different-slot conflict-free case; 3-way `wikilinks,tables,arxiv` remains conflict-free under APPEND-D-R rules.
- D-G Phase-43+ deferrals: tables cross-surface expansion (analogous Phase-43 candidate); arxiv cross-surface expansion (analogous Phase-44 candidate); multi-anchor wikilink alias `[[slug|display]]` (Phase-38 APPEND-D-L item 2); cross-entity wikilinks `[[paper-id]]` / `[[author-slug]]` (item 3); `<a class="wikilink">` styling (item 4); 404 handling for unresolved wikilinks (item 5); plugin parameterization for wikilink-href-builder (item 6 / D-L Phase 39+).

- **12 end-to-end `rationale`-surface tests** in `lib/markdown/index.test.ts` `describe("Phase-41 arxiv consumer ...")` block: single-arxiv-ref rendering with surrounding text; version-suffix preservation through full pipeline; case-insensitive prefix with source casing preserved; multi-ref-per-paragraph; **absolute-URL passing `rehypeStripUnsafeHrefs` naturally** (the architectural delta from Phase-38 wikilinks's relative-URL emission documented in ADR-0018 APPEND-D-V); bio/reviewNotes/actionRationale surfaces unaffected (registry default-deny on non-enabled per Phase-37 framework's per-surface differentiation property); XSS-defense survival (javascript: stripped; arxiv still resolves); nested-inside-emphasis preservation; non-matching-3-digit-suffix literal-text fallthrough; non-matching-older-category-prefixed literal-text fallthrough.
- **8 3-way composition end-to-end tests** in `lib/markdown/index.test.ts` `describe("Phase-41 3-way composite consumer ...")` block: wikilinks resolve on actionRationale under 3-way; tables render on reviewNotes under 3-way; arxiv autolinks resolve on rationale under 3-way; arxiv does NOT resolve on actionRationale (scope respected); wikilinks do NOT resolve on rationale (scope respected); tables do NOT render on rationale (scope respected); bio surface unaffected by any of the three consumers; XSS defense survives 3-way composition. **First 3-way composition feasibility realized end-to-end** under the Phase-40 `CompositeExtensionRegistry` infrastructure (which previously had a 2-way real-consumer case Phase 40 + 3-way structural-test coverage; Phase 41 adds the 3-way real-consumer + end-to-end pipeline coverage).

Curator prose like "see arxiv:1909.03004 for the methodology" in challenge resolution rationales will render as `<a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/1909.03004">arxiv:1909.03004</a>` end-to-end. #### Unit 41.1 — `lib/markdown/extensions/arxiv.ts` (`remarkLinkArxivIds` plugin + `ArxivExtensionRegistry` class) + 22 tests + ADR-0018 D-G APPEND arxiv-consumer block + new `@types/mdast` types-only dep (855/71; 69th consecutive 103 kB unit; zero behavioral change Day 1)

- Second Phase-41 unit; ships the arxiv plugin + registry class + 22 tests + ADR-0018 D-G APPEND. Factory dispatch wiring deferred to Unit 41.2.
- **2 new files** under `lib/markdown/extensions/`: `arxiv.ts` (remark plugin + `ArxivExtensionRegistry` class + `PHASE_41_DEFAULT_ENABLED_SURFACES` const) + `arxiv.test.ts` (22 tests: 17 plugin-level + 5 registry-level).
- **`remarkLinkArxivIds` plugin** walks the mdast tree via `unist-util-visit`, finds text-node substrings matching `ARXIV_PATTERN = /\barxiv:(\d{4}\.\d{4,5})(v\d+)?\b/gi`, and splice-replaces each match with an mdast `link` node emitting `<a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/<id>[<version>]">arxiv:<id>[<version>]</a>` after the standard mdast→hast bridge. 17 plugin-level tests verify: single match resolution; version suffix preservation (both href and display); case-insensitive prefix (`arxiv:` / `ArXiv:` / `ARXIV:`) with source casing preserved in display; surrounding text preserved; multiple matches per paragraph; adjacent matches separated by punctuation; nested inside bold; 6-digit suffix rejection (regex caps at 5); 3-digit suffix rejection (regex requires 4+); 3-digit prefix rejection; trailing-letter version typo rejection (`arxiv:1909.03004v` without digits); mid-word match rejection (`\b` anchor prevents `Xarxiv:NNNN.NNNNN`); older-style category-prefixed IDs rejection (out of scope Phase 41); bare-ID-without-prefix rejection; whole-paragraph match; trailing-punctuation preservation; 3-match-per-paragraph composition.
- **`ArxivExtensionRegistry` class** takes a `ReadonlySet<MarkdownSurface>` constructor arg; `getExtensions(surface)` returns `{ remarkPlugins: [remarkLinkArxivIds] }` for enabled surfaces, `{}` otherwise. Phase-41 default = `PHASE_41_DEFAULT_ENABLED_SURFACES = new Set(["rationale"])`. 5 registry tests verify enabled-surface dispatch + non-enabled-default + empty-set + multi-surface (Phase 42+ expansion shape) + Phase-41-default constants.
- **First mdast-level synthesis-emitting remark plugin** in project history. `remark-gfm` (Phase 17) is a syntax-parser hook; arxiv autolinks splice new `link` nodes from inside pre-existing `text` nodes via regex pattern match. Different shape from `remark-gfm`'s parser-hook style.
- **First absolute-URL emission by a framework extension** — wikilinks (Phase 38) emit relative `/problems/<slug>`; tables (Phase 39) emit no URLs; arxiv emits absolute `https://arxiv.org/abs/<id>`. The absolute URL passes the existing Phase-17 base allow-list verbatim (`<a>` tag + `href` attribute + `https:` URL scheme all already allow-listed) — **NO `schemaOverrides` required**. First framework consumer requiring zero sanitize-schema cooperation: wikilinks needed plugin-order discipline; tables needed `schemaOverrides`.
- **Plugin declaration shape**: function-declaration `export function remarkLinkArxivIds()` returning a transformer closure, rather than `Plugin<[], Root>` type-alias form. The function-declaration shape mirrors `remark-gfm` / `remark-parse` / `remark-rehype` idiom and chains cleanly under `unified().use(remarkParse).use(remarkLinkArxivIds)` — TypeScript's overload resolution under `Plugin<>` typing runs into a known union-type narrowing issue with chained mdast.Root processors that the function-declaration form avoids. Documented inline in `arxiv.ts` plugin-style block-comment.
- **XSS-safety contract documented** (ADR-0018 D-G APPEND-D-V): three layers combine. (1) Regex IS validation per Phase-38 APPEND-D-I discipline — `\d{4}\.\d{4,5}(v\d+)?` rejects path traversal, query strings, fragments, scheme injection. (2) Absolute `https:` URL constructed from regex captures (never arbitrary input) passes `rehypeStripUnsafeHrefs` allow-list naturally. (3) Emitted `<a>` + `href` + `https:` already allow-listed in Phase-17 base schema — no `schemaOverrides` audit-surface expansion.
- **ADR-0018 D-G APPEND**: new `EXTENDED Phase 41 Unit 41.1` block with 5 sub-decisions: APPEND-D-U arxiv plugin shape (regex + transform + post-default fold + mdast→hast bridge); APPEND-D-V XSS-safety boundary (3-layer combination); APPEND-D-W URL-emission convention (absolute https; version preserved; source casing preserved in display); APPEND-D-X `ArxivExtensionRegistry` per-surface enabling + 3-way composition conflict matrix; APPEND-D-Y Phase 42+ deferrals (cross-surface arxiv; older-style category IDs; bare IDs; DOI sibling consumer; display-text alias syntax; paper-card hover-preview). **Eighth APPEND on ADR-0018 D-G** (Phase 18 + 27 + 29 + 37 + 38 + 39 + 40 + **41**); extends the **first-ADR-D-clause-with-most-APPENDs record** from 7 → 8.
- **Path A** executed: no new ADR; ADR-0018 D-G APPEND only. **24 ADRs UNCHANGED**. **ADR-0025 candidate slot stays open** for Phase 42+ concrete-moderation-provider commitment.
- **`@types/mdast@^4.0.4` added to `dependencies`** (mirrors `@types/hast` Phase-17 convention; types-only dep; **zero bundle impact**). First new `@types/*` package addition since Phase 17 base. The prep doc anticipated "0 new runtime deps" — `@types/mdast` is a types-only devDep-flavored dependency listed in `dependencies` per the project's `@types/hast` precedent; runtime bundle UNCHANGED. - D-G composition matrix pinned: arxiv + wikilinks (rationale vs actionRationale; remark vs rehype) + tables (rationale vs reviewNotes; remark vs schema) = **all pairs/triples conflict-free** under APPEND-D-R composition rules. `MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS=wikilinks,tables,arxiv` becomes a valid env-var value Phase 41 — **first 3-way composition feasibility**.

#### Unit 40.1 — `lib/markdown/extensions/composite.ts` (`CompositeExtensionRegistry`) + 13 tests + ADR-0018 D-G APPEND Phase-40 composition block (818/70; 65th consecutive 103 kB unit; first multi-consumer composition infrastructure; first explicit conflict-error inside a registry class)

- Second Phase-40 unit; ships the composition infrastructure + tests + ADR-0018 D-G APPEND. Factory wiring deferred to Unit 40.2.
- **2 new files** under `lib/markdown/extensions/`: `composite.ts` (`CompositeExtensionRegistry` class) + `composite.test.ts` (13 tests covering composition rules + Phase 38+39 conflict-free case + conflict-error semantics).
- **`CompositeExtensionRegistry` class** wraps a `ReadonlyArray<MarkdownExtensionRegistry>` of components; `getExtensions(surface)` walks all components and merges per-surface extension sets per APPEND-D-R rules:
  - `remarkPlugins` CONCATENATED across components (registration order preserved).
  - `rehypePlugins` CONCATENATED across components (registration order preserved).
  - `schemaOverrides` **AT-MOST-ONE-PER-SURFACE**; throws on conflict with clear message naming both component indices + the surface + cross-link to APPEND-D-C override-replace semantics.
- **First explicit conflict-error within a registry class** in project history. The throw is the loud-failure analog to `getExtensionRegistry()` throw-on-unknown (Phase-37 APPEND-D-E); under APPEND-D-C override-replace semantics, two components both providing schemaOverrides cannot be safely merged — explicit throw surfaces misconfiguration loudly at first call.
- **Phase 38+39 composition is conflict-free** (canonical Phase-40 case): wikilinks uses `rehypePlugins` only on `actionRationale`; tables uses `schemaOverrides` only on `reviewNotes`; the two consumers' enabled surfaces are disjoint AND each consumer uses a distinct slot. `CompositeExtensionRegistry([wikilinks, tables])` produces: `actionRationale` gets wikilinks; `reviewNotes` gets tables; `bio` + `rationale` get nothing. **Composition order does not affect the outcome** — verified by test.
- **13 tests** verify composition end-to-end: empty components → empty for all surfaces; single-component mirror; remarkPlugins concat in order; rehypePlugins concat in order; single-component schemaOverrides pass-through; **conflict throws with surface + both component indices + APPEND-D-C reference**; per-surface conflict isolation (surfaces where only one component provides schemaOverrides don't throw); combined plugin types + schemaOverrides from different components; empty-set components contribute nothing; Phase 38+39 canonical composition case end-to-end; composition order independence for disjoint-surface case.
- **ADR-0018 D-G APPEND**: new `EXTENDED Phase 40 Unit 40.1` block with 4 sub-decisions: APPEND-D-R composition rules table; APPEND-D-S Phase 38+39 conflict-free composition example; APPEND-D-T Phase 41+ deferrals (resolvable conflicts; order-dependent composition; weighted composition; composition introspection API). **7th APPEND on ADR-0018 D-G** — extends the **first-ADR-D-clause-with-most-APPENDs record** from 6 → 7 (Phase 18 + 27 + 29 + 37 + 38 + 39 + **40**).
- **Path A** executed: no new ADR; ADR-0018 D-G APPEND only. **24 ADRs UNCHANGED**.
- **Tests**: 805 → **818** (+13 tests); test files 69 → 70 (+1 file `composite.test.ts`).
- **Zero behavioral change Day 1**: factory dispatch arms remain single-value at Unit 40.1 ship (`MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS=wikilinks` OR `tables` OR default; comma-separated values still hit the `default` throw arm). Multi-value parsing + composition wiring is Unit 40.2 scope. Existing tests pass verbatim.
- **No client-bundle delta**: server-only; First Load JS shared chunk **UNCHANGED at 103 kB** (**65th consecutive 103 kB unit**).
- **Zero new runtime dependencies**: composite.ts uses only types from already-imported `rehype-sanitize` + `unified` + `./types`.
- **Zero new env vars / migrations / i18n keys** at Unit 40.1; multi-value env-var parsing is Unit 40.2.

Composite is a peer of the consumer registries, not a wrapper around them.

#### Unit 40.0 — Phase 40 prep (multi-value `MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS` composition + `CompositeExtensionRegistry`; closes Phase-38-prep D-11 deferral; 35th "Continue" override; **thirty-first NON-§13 phase**)

- First Phase-40 unit; docs-only. Opens Phase 40 (**thirty-first NON-§13 phase**). **§13 ledger CLOSED** at Unit 9.9 (carried unchanged through Phases 10-39).
- **Phase 40 sign-off granted via "Continue" override** (**thirty-fifth invocation** of this pattern).
- **Recommended thread: multi-value composition via new `CompositeExtensionRegistry`** (D-1). Closes **Phase-38-prep D-11 deferral** ("single-value Phase 38; Phase-39+ extension to multi-value lean") + **Phase-39 mutual-exclusivity wrinkle** (Phase 38 + 39 dispatch arms are alternatives, not composable) + **Phase-39-gate Phase-40-candidate rank 2** ("Multi-value `MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS=wikilinks,tables` composition").
- **Why over Phase-40-candidate rank 1 (third concrete consumer)**: composition closes a CONCRETE deferral with documented deployment value (a deploy that wants BOTH wikilinks AND tables active currently cannot); third-consumer thread (rank 1) has zero content evidence and would extend the Phase-39 demand-signal-first trade-off into a third phase. Composition addresses a REAL multi-consumer-coexistence requirement that emerges the moment 2 consumers exist (Phase 39); it's the GENERALIZED multi-consumer mechanism. **Sets up rank 1 (third consumer) Phase 41+** — post-composition any new consumer adds without dispatch rework.
- **Reuses Phase-37/38/39 surfaces verbatim**: `MarkdownExtensionRegistry` interface + 2 existing consumer classes + `getExtensionRegistry()` factory + 3 test hooks. Phase 40 adds a third concrete `MarkdownExtensionRegistry` implementation (`CompositeExtensionRegistry`) + multi-value env-var parser. **Validates the framework's "zero-rework consumption" claim for the third consumer too** — no edits to `default.ts` / `types.ts` / `buildProcessor()` / 4 lazy markdown helpers / `wikilinks.ts` / `tables.ts`.
- **Path A** (no new ADR) chosen per Phase-37/38/39 precedent. ADR-0018 D-G APPEND adds **7th APPEND** — extends the first-ADR-D-clause-with-most-APPENDs record from 6 → 7.
- **Tradeoffs flagged**: passing on 8 thread alternatives: **Third concrete consumer (`@mention` via remarkPlugins)** — premature absent composition (would require another mutual-exclusion deferral); demand-signal-first weak. **ADR-0025 concrete moderation** — not autonomous-tractable. **Pre-commit typecheck hook** — tiny; better as Class B follow-on. **Q78 / Q79 Profile / v2 methodology / safeAuth / test backfill / account-deletion** — each deferred per prior rationale.
- **DB-migration trigger (D-2)**: **0 new migrations** anticipated. Pure framework infrastructure.
- **`CompositeExtensionRegistry` framework shape (D-3) anticipated**:
  - **D-A class** = constructor takes `ReadonlyArray<MarkdownExtensionRegistry>`; `getExtensions(surface)` walks all components + merges their extension sets per APPEND-D-R composition rules.
  - **D-B composition rules**: `remarkPlugins` CONCATENATED across components (registration order); `rehypePlugins` CONCATENATED (same); `schemaOverrides` AT-MOST-ONE-PER-SURFACE — **throw on conflict** (loud-failure analog to `getModerator()` throw-on-unknown; mirrors APPEND-D-C explicit no-deep-merge discipline).
  - **D-C multi-value env-var parsing**: `MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS=wikilinks,tables` splits on comma (trimmed); each value validated against the recognized non-default arm list; duplicates + empty values + mixing with `"default"` all rejected with clear error.
  - **D-D dispatch reorder**: `getExtensionRegistry()` converted from `switch` to comma-split-then-validate-then-compose. Single-value behavior preserved (single recognized arm returns the direct registry, not a 1-component composite).
  - **D-E Phase 38 + 39 composition is conflict-free**: wikilinks uses `rehypePlugins` only; tables uses `schemaOverrides` only; surfaces disjoint (wikilinks → actionRationale; tables → reviewNotes). Composition just additively merges with zero conflicts.

#### Unit 39.1 — `lib/markdown/extensions/tables.ts` (`TablesExtensionRegistry` + `GFM_TABLE_SCHEMA_OVERRIDES`) + 10 tests + ADR-0018 D-G APPEND tables-consumption block (794/69; 61st consecutive 103 kB unit; first real-consumer exercise of APPEND-D-C override-replace semantics)

- Second Phase-39 unit; ships the tables-consumer module + tests + ADR-0018 D-G APPEND. Factory dispatch wiring deferred to Unit 39.2.
- **2 new files** under `lib/markdown/extensions/`: `tables.ts` (`GFM_TABLE_SCHEMA_OVERRIDES` const + `TablesExtensionRegistry` class + `PHASE_39_DEFAULT_ENABLED_SURFACES` const) + `tables.test.ts` (10 tests: 5 schema-content + 5 registry-class).
- **`GFM_TABLE_SCHEMA_OVERRIDES`** is a `Partial<Schema>` supplying the FULL Phase-17 base `tagNames` allow-list verbatim (18 tags: `p`, `strong`, `em`, `code`, `pre`, `a`, `ul`, `ol`, `li`, `h3`, `h4`, `h5`, `h6`, `blockquote`, `hr`, `del`, `br`, `input`) PLUS 6 new GFM table tags (`table`, `thead`, `tbody`, `tr`, `th`, `td`). Same shape for `attributes` (full base verbatim + new `th` + `td` entries with `align` value-restricted to `"left" | "center" | "right"` via the tuple form per XSS-audit boundary).
- **`TablesExtensionRegistry` class** takes a `ReadonlySet<MarkdownSurface>` constructor arg; `getExtensions(surface)` returns `{ schemaOverrides: GFM_TABLE_SCHEMA_OVERRIDES }` for enabled surfaces, `{}` otherwise. Phase-39 default = `PHASE_39_DEFAULT_ENABLED_SURFACES = new Set(["reviewNotes"])`. 5 registry tests verify enabled-surface dispatch + non-enabled-default + empty-set + multi-surface (Phase 40+ expansion shape) + Phase-39-default constants.
- **First real-consumer exercise of APPEND-D-C override-replace semantics** in project history. Phase 37 + 38 only tested override-replace with synthetic test extensions (Unit 37.2's `tagNames: ["p", "strong"]` test); Phase 39 makes it a production contract realization. **Divergence-detector test pattern**: `tables.test.ts` asserts that every entry in `bioSchema.tagNames` appears in `GFM_TABLE_SCHEMA_OVERRIDES.tagNames`, so a future ADR-0018 D-B base allow-list expansion that omits this override surfaces as a test failure.
- **`remark-gfm` already parses tables** (Phase-17 pipeline); they're currently STRIPPED at sanitize because `<table>` is not in the Phase-17 base allow-list. Phase 39 adds the tags through `schemaOverrides` — **no new runtime dep, no new remark plugin, no plugin-order concerns**. The framework's "zero-rework consumption" property (Phase-37 APPEND-D-G) validated again: Unit 39.1 added 0 lines to `extensions/index.ts` (factory dispatch wiring is Unit 39.2); NO edits to `default.ts` / `types.ts` / `buildProcessor()` / 4 lazy markdown helpers.
- **XSS-audit boundary preserved**: 6 table tags added are well-understood HTML semantic structure with no XSS-vector-by-name concerns; `align` attribute is value-restricted to 3 literal values via the `[attrName, ...allowedValues]` tuple form. No `<style>` / `<script>` / `onclick` / `class` attributes added; same audit surface as Phase-17 base.

#### Unit 39.0 — Phase 39 prep (second concrete Phase-37-framework consumer: GFM tables per-reviewNotes via `schemaOverrides` slot — validates the framework slot Phase 38 did NOT exercise; 34th "Continue" override; **thirtieth NON-§13 phase**)

- First Phase-39 unit; docs-only. Opens Phase 39 (**thirtieth NON-§13 phase**). Mirrors Phase-12 through Phase-38 phase-prep patterns. **§13 ledger CLOSED** at Unit 9.9 (carried unchanged through Phases 10-38).
- **Phase 39 sign-off granted via "Continue" override** in the unit-rhythm rhythm (**thirty-fourth invocation** of this pattern). §12 cardinal rule satisfied; first-thread D-1 recommendation overridable into Unit 39.1.
- **Recommended thread: GFM tables on `reviewNotes` surface via Phase-37 framework `schemaOverrides` slot + new `TablesExtensionRegistry`** (D-1). **Second concrete Phase-37-framework consumer in project history** — validates the framework's `schemaOverrides` slot end-to-end (Phase 38 wikilinks consumer used only the `rehypePlugins` slot). After Phase 39 the framework has **2 real consumers exercising 2 of 3 slots** (`rehypePlugins` + `schemaOverrides`); only `remarkPlugins` remains as Phase-40+ deferral.
- **First "framework + 2 consumers" 3-phase cluster** in project history (Phase 37 framework + Phase 38 wikilinks + Phase 39 tables). Validates the framework's multi-consumer composition design value.
- **First real-consumer exercise of APPEND-D-C override-replace semantics**: `GFM_TABLE_SCHEMA_OVERRIDES.tagNames` must supply the FULL Phase-17 base allow-list verbatim PLUS 6 new table tags — the framework's documented contract enforces no-deep-merge. Phase 39 implementation exercises this contract for real (Phase 37 only tested with synthetic test extensions).
- **`remark-gfm` already parses tables** (Phase-17 pipeline); they're currently STRIPPED at sanitize because `<table>` is not in the allow-list. Phase 39 just adds the tags through `schemaOverrides` — **no new runtime dep**, no new remark plugin, no plugin-order concerns.
- **Demand-signal-first weak but acceptable** (trade-off flagged): zero curator content currently uses table syntax (zero `|---|---|---|` patterns observable in DB-backed `ratingChallenges.reviewNotes` field). Phase 39 ships the architectural validation; curator authoring of tabular review notes is an enabling, not a forcing-function. **Architectural validation has option value** — the framework's value is its composability; validating the second slot strengthens the framework's claim to general reusability.
- **Path A** (no new ADR) executed per Phase-37 + 38 D-9 precedent. ADR-0018 D-G APPEND will add Phase-39 EXTENDED block. **ADR-0025 candidate slot stays open** for Phase 40+ concrete-moderation-provider commitment. **24 ADRs UNCHANGED** anticipated.
- **Tradeoffs flagged**: passing on 7 thread alternatives: **Cross-surface wikilink expansion** — zero content-evidence outside actionRationale; same demand-signal weakness; no architectural advantage over tables (still 2-of-3 slots); **`@mention` resolution** — third-slot validation but needs new plugin authoring; **Pre-commit typecheck hook** — tiny (~1 unit), not a 5-unit phase shape; **ADR-0025 concrete content-moderation provider** — not autonomous-tractable; **Multi-value `MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS` composition** — premature (only 1 consumer exists Phase-38; needs 2 consumers before composition has meaning); **`safeAuth()` extraction** — still 4 copies; **Subscribe + `/profile` test backfill** — not user-visible value.
- **Single-surface scope decision** (D-1 gate): Phase 39 enables tables on `reviewNotes` ONLY; bio + rationale + actionRationale continue to receive empty extension set per `TablesExtensionRegistry`'s default-deny. Cross-surface table expansion Phase 40+ if demand surfaces. Curator review notes (4000-char field, Phase-12 ADR-0014) is the natural surface for tabular content (COI comparisons; multi-criterion scoring; structured editorial reasoning).
- **Mutually exclusive env-var dispatch Phase 39**: `MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS=wikilinks` OR `MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS=tables` — multi-value composition `MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS=wikilinks,tables` deferred Phase 40+ per Phase-38-prep D-11 deferral. Premature absent a real "use both consumers in production" requirement.
- **DB-migration trigger (D-2)**: **0 new migrations** anticipated. Pure sanitize schema extension; no DB change.
- **`TablesExtensionRegistry` framework consumer shape (D-3) anticipated** (Path A = no new ADR; ADR-0018 D-G APPEND documents):
  - **D-A registry class** = takes `ReadonlySet<MarkdownSurface>` constructor arg; `getExtensions(surface)` returns `{ schemaOverrides: GFM_TABLE_SCHEMA_OVERRIDES }` for enabled surfaces, `{}` otherwise. Phase-39 default = `Set(["reviewNotes"])`.
  - **D-B schema overrides** = `tagNames` full replacement (Phase-17 base verbatim + 6 table tags: `<table>`, `<thead>`, `<tbody>`, `<tr>`, `<th>`, `<td>`); `attributes` shallow-merge adding `align` to `<th>`/`<td>` for GFM column alignment.
  - **D-C override-replace semantics exercised** — first real-consumer exercise.
  - **D-D env-var dispatch arm** = `MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS=tables` joins `MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS=wikilinks`; second non-default arm; mutually exclusive Phase 39.
  - **D-E integration with existing pipeline** = `buildProcessor` already folds `schemaOverrides` over base schema via shallow merge per APPEND-D-C. No `buildProcessor` edit needed; framework's "zero-rework consumption" property validated for the second consumer.
  - **D-F Phase 40+ deferrals** = multi-value composition; cross-surface table expansion; table-specific attributes (`colspan`, `rowspan`); `<caption>`; `remarkPlugins` slot consumer.

##### Architectural firsts in Phase 38 (14 enumerated)

1. **First concrete Phase-37-framework consumer in project history** — Phase 37 shipped framework; Phase 38 ships first concrete consumer (wikilinks-per-actionRationale). Validates Phase-37 framework's "zero-rework consumption" property documented in APPEND-D-G — Unit 38.2 added 5 lines to `extensions/index.ts`; NO edits to `default.ts` / `types.ts` / `buildProcessor()` / 4 lazy markdown helpers.

3. **First env-var addition since Phase 35** (`MODERATION_PROVIDER` was the previous Phase 35; `MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS` Phase 38; env-var count 13 → 14).
4. **First post-sanitize HAST transformation that EMITS new elements** in the markdown pipeline (prior post-sanitize plugin `rehypeStripUnsafeHrefs` only strips; the wikilink plugin synthesizes `<a>` elements).
5. **First framework-emitted relative URLs in markdown output** — wikilinks emit `/problems/{slug}` hrefs that bypass `rehypeStripUnsafeHrefs` via APPEND-D-D plugin-order-after-default discipline. **Validates the Phase-37 framework's design value** for relative-URL-emitting extensions.
6. **Second consecutive Phase-37-framework-pattern phase** — Phase 37 framework + Phase 38 consumer = **first "framework + first consumer" 2-phase cluster** in project history.
7. **First ADR D-clause with FIVE APPENDs** — ADR-0018 D-G now carries Phase 18 + 27 + 29 + 37 + **38** APPENDs. Phase 37 was the prior record-holder (4 APPENDs).
8. **Fifth consecutive net-Q/candidate-decrease phase** — Phase 34 Q79 + 35 Q68 + 36 Q64 + 37 Q72 + **38 B.14** = **first 5-phase net-decrease streak in project history**.
9. **First B-class-resolution-via-framework-consumption phase** — B.14 closes Phase 38 via Phase-37 framework. The Phase-37 framework is now the **first ADR D-clause framework with multiple Q/B-class closures across two distinct phases** (Q72 Phase 37 framework-as-realization + B.14 Phase 38 framework-as-consumer).
10. **First "speculated-coupling-not-needed" closure in project history** — B.14's Phase-29 paragraph speculated B.14 + Q72-family could couple into a single "markdown evolution" ADR-0021-candidate scope; neither needed it. Both closed independently via the SAME Phase-37 framework as separate consumers.
11. **9+ phase B.14 carryover** (Phase 29 → 38; second-longest B-class carryover after B.15 item 1's 22+ phase Phase-30 closure).
12. **Eighth consecutive phase without new B category** (Phase 31 + 32 + 33 + 34 + 35 + 36 + 37 + **38** = first such 8-phase run in project history).
13. **57th-through-60th consecutive 103 kB First Load JS units** in project history (Phase 9 Unit 9.5 → Phase 38 Unit 38.4 inclusive); zero client-bundle regression end-to-end across framework + first consumer.

- **3 coordinated edits**:
  1. `lib/markdown/extensions/index.ts` — adds `import { PHASE_38_DEFAULT_ENABLED_SURFACES, WikilinkExtensionRegistry } from "./wikilinks"`; converts `getExtensionRegistry()` from a single-arm default to a `switch` over `process.env["MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS"] ?? ""` with three recognized values (unset/empty/`"default"` → default; `"wikilinks"` → `WikilinkExtensionRegistry(PHASE_38_DEFAULT_ENABLED_SURFACES)`; unknown → throw with clear message listing recognized values). Mirrors `lib/moderation/getModerator()` Phase-35 dispatch shape verbatim per Phase-37 framework APPEND-D-E throw-on-unknown discipline.
  2. `lib/markdown/extensions/index.test.ts` — adds `ORIGINAL_PROVIDER` env snapshot + `afterEach` restore pattern (mirrors `lib/moderation/index.test.ts`); 9 dispatch tests: unset → default; empty-string → default; literal `"default"` → default; `"wikilinks"` → `WikilinkExtensionRegistry`; wikilinks-on-actionRationale-only surface differentiation; singleton cache (default + wikilinks); throw-on-unknown (`MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS=wikilinkx` → clear error referencing the invalid value AND the recognized list); `__resetRegistryForTests` re-reads env.
  3. `lib/markdown/index.test.ts` — new `Phase-38 wikilinks consumer` describe block (9 end-to-end tests through the FULL `unified` pipeline parse → gfm → remark-rehype → demote → sanitize → strip-unsafe-hrefs → **wikilink** → stringify): single wikilink rendering; multiple wikilinks in one paragraph (`[[hallucination-reduction]] and [[long-context-rag]]`); **href survives `rehypeStripUnsafeHrefs` because plugin folds AFTER strip step** (validates Phase-37 framework APPEND-D-D plugin-order-after-default discipline's design value); non-matching `[[Slug]]` uppercase falls through (XSS regex contract); bio + reviewNotes + rationale surfaces unaffected by the extension (registry default-deny on non-enabled surfaces); XSS-defense survives extension (mixed `[bad](javascript:alert(1))` + `[[scalable-oversight]]` input → `javascript:` URL stripped AND wikilink still resolves); wikilink nested inside bold (`**[[hallucination-reduction]]**` → `<strong><a>hallucination-reduction</a></strong>`).

Recognized values at Phase-38 ship: `"default"` (or unset/empty) + `"wikilinks"`. Phase 39+ values documented in ADR-0018 D-G APPEND APPEND-D-L (per-curator preferences; multi-value composition `wikilinks,tables`; surface-list-as-env-var).

#### Unit 38.1 — `lib/markdown/extensions/wikilinks.ts` (`rehypeResolveWikilinks` plugin + `WikilinkExtensionRegistry` class) + 15 plugin-level tests + ADR-0018 D-G APPEND wikilink-consumption block (770/68; 57th consecutive 103 kB unit; zero behavioral change Day 1)

- Second Phase-38 unit; ships the wikilink plugin + registry class + plugin-level tests + ADR-0018 D-G APPEND. Factory dispatch wiring deferred to Unit 38.2.
- **2 new files** under `lib/markdown/extensions/`: `wikilinks.ts` (rehype plugin + `WikilinkExtensionRegistry` class + `PHASE_38_DEFAULT_ENABLED_SURFACES` const) + `wikilinks.test.ts` (15 tests: 10 plugin-level + 5 registry-level).
- **`rehypeResolveWikilinks` plugin** walks the post-sanitize HAST tree via `unist-util-visit`, finds text-node substrings matching `WIKILINK_PATTERN = /\[\[([a-z0-9-]+)\]\]/g`, and splice-replaces each match with an `<a href="/problems/{slug}">{slug}</a>` element node. Handles 8 edge cases verified by tests: single wikilink, surrounding text, multiple per text node, adjacent without intervening text, non-matching uppercase, non-matching space, empty `[[]]`, nested inside bold (`**[[slug]]**`), wikilink-as-entire-paragraph, code-block content (documented current behavior — code-block-skip is Phase 39+ if signal).
- **`WikilinkExtensionRegistry` class** takes a `ReadonlySet<MarkdownSurface>` constructor arg; `getExtensions(surface)` returns `{ rehypePlugins: [rehypeResolveWikilinks] }` for enabled surfaces, `{}` otherwise. Phase-38 default = `PHASE_38_DEFAULT_ENABLED_SURFACES = new Set(["actionRationale"])`. 5 registry tests verify enabled-surface dispatch + non-enabled-default + empty-set + multi-surface (Phase 39+ expansion shape) + Phase-38-default constants.
- **XSS-safety contract documented**: the wikilink plugin emits `<a>` + `href` that have NOT been validated by `rehype-sanitize` (plugin runs AFTER sanitize per Phase-37 framework's APPEND-D-D plugin-order discipline). Security relies on the regex `[a-z0-9-]+` — restrictive kebab-case-only character set; no special chars; no path traversal; no scheme injection. **The regex IS the validation.** Documented in ADR-0018 D-G APPEND-D-I.
- **First framework-emitted relative URLs in markdown output** in project history — wikilinks emit `/problems/{slug}` hrefs that bypass the `rehypeStripUnsafeHrefs` relative-URL strip step because the wikilink plugin folds AFTER that step. **Validates the Phase-37 framework's APPEND-D-D plugin-order-after-default discipline's design value** for relative-URL-emitting extensions specifically.
- **ADR-0018 D-G APPEND**: new `EXTENDED Phase 38 Unit 38.1` block with 5 sub-decisions: APPEND-D-H wikilink plugin shape (regex + replace algorithm); APPEND-D-I XSS-safety boundary (regex IS the validation; documented escape examples); APPEND-D-J URL-resolution convention (locale-less Path A; Next.js i18n middleware forwards to `/{locale}/problems/{slug}`); APPEND-D-K `WikilinkExtensionRegistry` per-surface activation shape; APPEND-D-L Phase 39+ deferrals (cross-surface + multi-anchor + cross-entity + styling + 404 validation + plugin parameterization). **Fifth APPEND on ADR-0018 D-G** (Phase 18 + 27 + 29 + 37 + **38**); ADR-0018 D-G is the **first ADR D-clause with 5 APPENDs** in project history (was 4 after Phase 37).
- **Path A** executed: no new ADR; ADR-0018 D-G APPEND only. **24 ADRs UNCHANGED**. **ADR-0025 candidate slot stays open** for Phase 39+ concrete-moderation-provider.
- **Tests**: 755 → 770 (+15 tests); test files 67 → 68 (+1 file `wikilinks.test.ts`).
- **Zero behavioral change Day 1**: factory dispatch is Unit 38.2 scope — `getExtensionRegistry()` still returns `DefaultExtensionRegistry` (which returns `{}` for all surfaces) at Phase-38-Unit-38.1 ship. Plugin + registry exist but are not yet wired into the factory dispatch arm. Existing 50+ markdown tests pass verbatim.
- **No client-bundle delta**: server-only (mirrors `lib/moderation/`); never imported from `"use client"` boundary; First Load JS shared chunk **UNCHANGED at 103 kB** (**57th consecutive 103 kB unit**).
- **Zero new runtime dependencies**: plugin uses already-imported `unist-util-visit` + types from `unified` / `hast` / `./types`.
- **Zero new env vars / migrations / i18n keys** at Unit 38.1; `MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS=wikilinks` env-var dispatch arm is Unit 38.2 scope.
- **Class B.14 closure preview**: Class B.14 (Phase-29 carryover) describes "rating-action rationale wikilink resolution"; Unit 38.1 ships the plugin + registry; Unit 38.2 wires it into the factory dispatch. Full closure of B.14 lands at Unit 38.2 ship; Unit 38.3 hygiene documents the closure.
- **Parallel-curator awareness**: NEW files `wikilinks.ts` + `wikilinks.test.ts` (parallel-safe); ADR-0018 APPENDed (existing file; APPEND area is the freshly-Phase-37-extended section, unlikely concurrent-edit hotspot absent another markdown thread). Working tree clean at unit start (HEAD `0c46b9a`).

- **Wikilink extension framework shape (D-3) anticipated** (Path A = no new ADR; ADR-0018 D-G APPEND documents):
  - **D-A plugin** = `rehypeResolveWikilinks` rehype plugin walks HAST text nodes, replaces `[[slug]]` matches with `<a href="/problems/{slug}">{slug}</a>` element nodes.
  - **D-B URL resolution** = locale-less `/problems/{slug}` hrefs; Next.js i18n middleware forwards to `/{locale}/problems/{slug}` at request time (Path A lean per Unit 38.1 D-8).
  - **D-C schema overrides** = NONE needed — wikilink-generated `<a href="/problems/{slug}">` reuses existing `<a>` allow-list. Subtle: wikilink plugin folds AFTER `rehypeStripUnsafeHrefs` per Phase-37 framework's APPEND-D-D plugin-order-after-default discipline — the relative `/problems/{slug}` URL bypasses the strip step because the extension adds the link AFTER the strip step runs. **First framework-emitted relative URLs in markdown output** in project history.
  - **D-D `WikilinkExtensionRegistry` class** = takes a `ReadonlySet<MarkdownSurface>` constructor arg; `getExtensions(surface)` returns `{ rehypePlugins: [rehypeResolveWikilinks] }` for enabled surfaces, `{}` otherwise. Phase-38 default = `Set(["actionRationale"])` only.
  - **D-E env-var dispatch arm** = `MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS=wikilinks` adds the first non-default dispatch arm in `getExtensionRegistry()` factory. Throw-on-unknown discipline mirrors `getModerator()` D-E.
  - **D-F integration** = ALL FOUR markdown helpers continue using the registry; only `actionRationale` observes the extension; behavior parity preserved on the 3 other surfaces.
  - **D-G Phase 39+ deferrals** = cross-surface wikilink expansion; multi-anchor `[[slug|display]]` syntax; cross-entity wikilinks (`[[paper-id]]` / `[[author-slug]]` / `[[institution-slug]]`); `<a class="wikilink">` styling; 404-validation of unresolved slugs.

##### Anti-scope (Phase 38+ deferrals; carried forward)

#### Unit 37.2 — Integration of `MarkdownExtensionRegistry` into all 4 markdown helpers + 10 new framework-integration tests (755/67; lazy-built processor singletons; Day-1 parity verified; 54th consecutive 103 kB unit)

- Third Phase-37 unit; wires the Phase-37-Unit-37.1 framework into the four existing markdown helpers per ADR-0018 D-G APPEND-D-A through D-D semantics. **Day-1 behavioral parity**: default `DefaultExtensionRegistry` returns `{}` for all 4 surfaces; folding empty plugin lists + empty schema overrides is a structural no-op; existing 50+ Phase-17/18/27/29 markdown tests pass verbatim.
- **3 coordinated edits**:
  1. `lib/markdown/extensions/index.ts` — adds `__setRegistryForTests(registry | null)` test-override hook + expands `__resetRegistryForTests` to clear both the default singleton AND the override.
  2. `lib/markdown/index.ts` — adds `buildProcessor(surface, baseSchema)` helper that reads `getExtensionRegistry().getExtensions(surface)` and folds `schemaOverrides` over the base schema (APPEND-D-C override-replace) + folds `remarkPlugins` / `rehypePlugins` AFTER the default plugins (APPEND-D-D plugin order); converts the 4 module-level `const xxxProcessor = unified()...` singletons to lazy getters (`getBioProcessor()` + `getReviewNotesProcessor()` + `getRationaleProcessor()` + `getActionRationaleProcessor()`); updates each `render*` helper to call its getter; adds `__resetMarkdownCachesForTests()` to clear all four lazy caches in `beforeEach`/`afterEach`.
  3. `lib/markdown/index.test.ts` — new `Phase-37 framework integration` test section under three describe blocks (10 new tests).
- **`buildProcessor` semantics** (APPEND-D-C + APPEND-D-D realized): schema = `{ ...baseSchema, ...(extensions.schemaOverrides ?? {}) }` (shallow merge; override-replace per field); pipeline = `remarkParse → remarkGfm → extensions.remarkPlugins → remarkRehype → rehypeDemoteHeadings → rehypeSanitize → rehypeStripUnsafeHrefs → extensions.rehypePlugins → rehypeStringify` (extensions fold post-default-plugins so sanitization baseline establishes first; future `rehype-wikilink` example documented in APPEND-D-D).
- **Lazy-built singleton pattern** (mirror of `getModerator()` Phase 35 + `lib/email/` Phase 30): each surface's processor builds on first render call, then cached for the module lifetime. Test-only `__resetMarkdownCachesForTests` hook clears all four caches so per-suite registry swaps take effect on next render. **Preserves perf invariant**: first-call cost equals the Phase-17 module-load cost (just deferred to first call); subsequent calls hit the cache.
- **10 new tests** verifying framework integration:
  - **Default-empty-registry parity** (4 tests; 1 per surface): all 4 helpers produce unchanged output with the default empty registry.
  - **Rehype-plugin extension AFTER default plugins** (3 tests): bio gets an uppercase-text plugin → output uppercased; other surfaces unaffected (registry differentiation); sanitization still applies BEFORE the extension (javascript: URL stripped, then surviving text uppercased) — proves APPEND-D-D fold-after-default-plugins ordering.
  - **Schema override-replace semantics** (3 tests): reviewNotes overrides `tagNames` to `["p", "strong"]` → only `<p>` + `<strong>` survive; base-list `<em>` dropped (content survives as text); other surfaces use their base schema unchanged — proves APPEND-D-C override-replace (callers supply complete replacement; no deep-merge).
- **Tests**: 745 → 755 (+10 tests); test files **67 UNCHANGED** (integration tests folded into existing `lib/markdown/index.test.ts`).
- **No client-bundle delta**: framework + integration code server-only; First Load JS shared chunk **UNCHANGED at 103 kB** (**54th consecutive 103 kB unit**).
- **Zero new runtime dependencies**: integration code uses only types already imported by `lib/markdown/index.ts` (`Schema` from `rehype-sanitize`; `PluggableList` from `unified`).
- **Zero new env vars / migrations / i18n keys / ADRs**.
- **Test-discovery wrinkle resolved**: initial test draft used `<script>...</script>plain` as XSS-defense-survives input; markdown parsed this as a full HTML block (stripping the entire block including the trailing text). Refactored to `[bad](javascript:...) and text` which keeps the link-text + post-link text intact through sanitize; href stripped + uppercase extension then runs over surviving text. Cleaner assertion shape.
- **Parallel-curator awareness**: modified 3 existing files (`lib/markdown/extensions/index.ts` + `lib/markdown/index.ts` + `lib/markdown/index.test.ts`); LOW collision risk absent another markdown thread. Working tree clean at unit start (HEAD `55e7ba9` Unit 37.1 ship).
- Smoke gates: `pnpm typecheck` (passes); `pnpm test` (755/67 passes; +10 vs Unit-37.1 baseline); `pnpm audit-content` (0 errors / 6 warnings; Q32 baseline unchanged).
- THINK artifact: `docs/thinking/37.2-markdown-extension-integration.md`.

#### Unit 37.1 — `lib/markdown/extensions/` framework + ADR-0018 D-G APPEND (4-surface path correction; mirrors Phase-35 `lib/moderation/` shape verbatim; 745/67; zero behavioral change; 53rd consecutive 103 kB unit)

- Second Phase-37 unit; ships the `MarkdownExtensionRegistry` framework module + ADR-0018 D-G APPEND documentation. Integration into the four existing markdown helpers deferred to Unit 37.2.
- **5 new files** under `lib/markdown/extensions/` mirroring Phase-35 `lib/moderation/` shape verbatim: `types.ts` (`MarkdownSurface` literal union + `MarkdownExtensionSet` per-surface config shape + `MarkdownExtensionRegistry` interface) + `default.ts` (`DefaultExtensionRegistry` class with exhaustive `getExtensions(surface)` switch returning `{}` for all four surfaces) + `index.ts` (`getExtensionRegistry()` lazy-singleton factory + `__resetRegistryForTests` hook + re-exports) + `default.test.ts` (5 tests) + `index.test.ts` (4 tests).
- **4-surface path correction**: Phase-37-prep doc anticipated 3 surfaces (`"bio"` + `"reviewNotes"` + `"rationale"`) and overlooked `"actionRationale"` which shipped Phase 29 (a phase AFTER Q72 was first noted Phase 18). Unit 37.1 expanded `MarkdownSurface` union to **all 4 wired surfaces** — otherwise the framework would ship with an architectural gap from day one on the most-recently-added markdown surface. **Defensible default + tradeoff flagged** per `feedback_unit_rhythm.md` rhythm discipline. Documented mirror of Phase-35 path correction (Phase-35-prep anticipated `app/api/v1/profile/update/route.ts` + `lib/images/transcode.ts`; actual wiring landed at `lib/users/updateProfile` + `lib/users/updateProfileImage` per ADR-0019 D-F APPEND). Phase-37-prep 3-surface anticipation now superseded by Unit 37.1 4-surface realization; documented explicitly in ADR-0018 D-G APPEND.
- **Path A** (no new ADR) executed per Phase-37-prep D-9 lean. ADR-0018 D-G APPENDed with `EXTENDED Phase 37 Unit 37.1` block (+~95 lines). **ADR-0025 candidate slot saved** for Phase 38+ concrete-moderation-provider commitment. **24 ADRs UNCHANGED**.
- **ADR-0018 D-G APPEND** documents 7 sub-decisions: APPEND-D-A registry interface (single-method `getExtensions(surface)` per Phase-37-prep D-10 lean); APPEND-D-B `MarkdownExtensionSet` shape (3 optional fields: `remarkPlugins?` + `rehypePlugins?` + `schemaOverrides?`); APPEND-D-C override-replace semantics (Phase-37-prep D-11 lean: no deep-merge; callers supply complete replacement; audit-friendly single-grep coverage); APPEND-D-D plugin order after default plugins (Phase-37-prep D-12 lean: sanitization baseline first; extensions are post-processing; future `rehype-wikilink` example documented); APPEND-D-E factory shape (lazy singleton + `__resetRegistryForTests` mirror of `getModerator()` Phase 35 + `lib/email/` Phase 30 patterns; single dispatch arm Phase 37; throw-on-unknown deferred Phase 38+); APPEND-D-F Phase 38+ deferrals enumeration (12 concrete-extension candidates incl. wikilink resolution per Class B.14 family + GFM tables + GFM footnotes + images + tel-URL scheme + `<a title>` + syntax highlighting + `@mentions` + per-curator preferences + curator-facing UI + live preview + configurable schema per-user); APPEND-D-G zero-cost-default discipline (zero new runtime deps + zero new env vars + zero new ADRs + zero new migrations + zero new i18n keys; framework existence at default = Phase-18/27/29 baseline; mirrors Phase-35 zero-cost-default).
- **Eleventh APPEND-pattern ADR D-clause cluster** in project history (Phase 36 milestone was 10 distinct D-clauses with APPENDs; Unit 37.1 adds an 11th APPEND TO an existing D-clause — ADR-0018 D-G now has **four APPENDs**: Phase 18 reviewNotes + Phase 27 rationale + Phase 29 actionRationale + **Phase 37 framework**). ADR-0018 D-G is now the **first ADR D-clause with four APPENDs in project history** (prior maximum was 3 APPENDs on a single D-clause via the same ADR-0018 D-G).
- **Tests**: 736 → 745 (+9 tests); test files 65 → 67 (+2 files `default.test.ts` + `index.test.ts`).
- **No client-bundle delta**: framework is server-only (mirrors `lib/moderation/`); never imported from `"use client"` boundary; First Load JS shared chunk **UNCHANGED at 103 kB** (**53rd consecutive 103 kB unit**).

Q78 digest-send analytics** — still waits for Q77 production signal; **N. v2 methodology authoring** — curator-track-heavy; **Q79 Profile B / C** — need user-feedback signal; **Pre-commit typecheck hook addition** — tiny (~1 unit); arguably not a full phase; **`safeAuth()` extraction** — still 4 copies; not yet at 5+ threshold; **Subscribe + `/profile` test backfill** — not user-visible value; **J-other / K-other / M / B.15 item 5** — various follow-ons each deferred per existing rationale.

- **`MarkdownExtensionRegistry` framework shape (D-3) anticipated** (Path A = no new ADR; ADR-0018 D-G APPEND documents):
  - **D-A registry** = `MarkdownExtensionSet` per-surface object with `remarkPlugins?` + `rehypePlugins?` + `schemaOverrides?`.
  - **D-B default** = `DefaultExtensionRegistry` returns empty extension set for all 3 surfaces (bio + reviewNotes + rationale). Zero behavioral change vs Phase 18 + 27 baseline.
  - **D-C factory** = `getExtensionRegistry()` mirrors Phase-35 `getModerator()` shape: lazy singleton + (currently noop) env-var dispatch.
  - **D-D integration** = each markdown helper reads `registry.getExtensions(surface)` + folds `schemaOverrides` into the surface schema + folds `remarkPlugins` / `rehypePlugins` into the unified pipeline. Override-replace semantics (callers supply what they want; framework doesn't deep-merge).
  - **D-E test surface** = framework tests verify default-returns-empty + singleton-caching + future-extension-override-merge correctness. Existing 50+ markdown tests pass verbatim.
  - **D-F Phase 38+ deferrals** = concrete extension implementations (wikilinks per-bio; tables per-reviewNotes; footnotes per-rationale) each ship as independent 1-2-unit Phase 38+ commits; per-curator extension preferences (DB-backed override); curator-facing extension-enable UI; live preview in edit form (Phase 38+ caveat: breaks 103 kB invariant per existing Phase-18 deferral).

Closes Phase 36 (**twenty-seventh NON-§13 phase**; Q64 per-user privacy opt-out realized via new `users.profilePublic` boolean column + opt-out toggle UI on `/[locale]/profile` + `getPublicProfileByHandle` filter + pure 404 on `/[locale]/u/{handle}` for opted-out users + ADR-0015 D-A APPEND). - **Phase 36 ledger**: **5 units shipped** (36.0 prep → 36.4 gate). **0 deferrals**; **0 scope drift**. **Twenty-fifth consecutive well-scoped phase**. **Zero new ADRs** (first no-new-ADR phase since Phase 34; alternating-pattern extends to 5 phases: Phase 32 no-new → 33 new → 34 no-new → 35 new → **36 no-new** — first 5-phase alternating pattern in project history). #### Unit 36.2 — Opt-out toggle UI on `/[locale]/profile` + 7 i18n keys per locale + `updateProfilePrivacyAction` server action (736/65; i18n keys 161 → 168; 50th consecutive 103 kB unit; zero client-bundle delta)

- Third Phase-36 unit; code unit. Adds the user-facing opt-out toggle UI on `/[locale]/profile` between the image-edit section + the Watching section. **Test count UNCHANGED at 736/65** (no new test files; UI behavior is server-component-rendered + server-action-driven; existing 50 tests in `lib/users/index.test.ts` cover the underlying `setProfilePublic` helper from Unit 36.1). **i18n keys per locale 161 → 168** (+7 keys per locale; 14 new total across EN + FR; +2 above Phase-36-prep estimate of 5). **First Load JS shared chunk = 103 kB UNCHANGED** (**50th consecutive unit at 103 kB**; Phase 9-36 invariant preserved); middleware 160 kB UNCHANGED; all page bundles UNCHANGED.
- **`app/[locale]/profile/page.tsx`** (modified; +1 import + 1 field in SELECT + 1 `currentProfilePublic` local + 1 new server action + 1 new `<section>` block): adds `setProfilePublic` import; extends `userRow` SELECT with `profilePublic: users.profilePublic`; new `currentProfilePublic = userRow?.profilePublic ?? true` local (defensive default for first-sign-in edge case before auth callback writes to `users` table). New `updateProfilePrivacyAction` server action mirrors Phase-15 `updateProfileAction` shape verbatim: auth check + redirect-if-unauth + `formData.get("isPublic") === "on"` extraction + call `setProfilePublic(userId, isPublic)` + dual `revalidatePath` (`/[locale]/profile` + `/[locale]/u/[handle]`) + redirect with `?saved=privacy` success param. New `<section aria-label={tE("privacy_aria_label")}>` block between image-edit `</section>` (line ~461) + Watching `<section>` (line ~463): status text (`privacy_public_status` or `privacy_private_status` depending on current state) + labeled checkbox `<input type="checkbox" name="isPublic" defaultChecked={currentProfilePublic}>` + description text + `privacy_save_button` submit.
- **UI shape decisions** (Phase-36-prep D-10 + D-11 + D-12 leans honored verbatim): D-10 = labeled checkbox in `<form>` with submit-on-change (mirrors Phase-15 displayName/bio form pattern; no new UI primitive; no client-side state; no shadcn `Switch`); D-11 = NO confirmation modal (toggle reversible; single-click; no destructive action); D-12 = status surface placement within form description text (renders current state above checkbox so user sees current state without recalling what checkbox represents).
- **`messages/en.json`** (modified; +7 keys in `profile_edit.*` namespace): `privacy_aria_label` ("Profile visibility"); `privacy_heading` ("Profile visibility"); `privacy_label` ("Make my profile public"); `privacy_description` ("When unchecked, your /u/{login} page returns a 404 — your watchlist, ratings, and bio are hidden from other users."); `privacy_public_status` ("Your profile is currently public."); `privacy_private_status` ("Your profile is currently private."); `privacy_save_button` ("Save visibility").
- **`messages/fr.json`** (modified; +7 keys mirroring EN): full FR translations preserving meaning; `/u/{login}` URL preserved as English-canonical per ADR-0011 D-E.
- **i18n key count**: 161 per locale (Phase 34 close baseline) → **168 per locale** (+7 keys; first i18n addition since Phase 34 = 2-phase gap; matches Phase-30→34 4-phase gap pattern — i18n additions ship at user-facing UX surface introduction).
- **Architectural firsts shipped in this unit** (~3 firsts):
  - **First user-toggleable privacy UI surface** in project history. Phase 14-15 IDENTITY + Phase 16 BINARY + Phase 36 VISIBILITY user-control surface lineage now visually complete on `/profile`.
  - **First dual-`revalidatePath` server action** in project history. Prior server actions (Phase 15 displayName/bio; Phase 16 imageOverride; Phase 11 challenge-withdraw) all invalidate a single page; Phase 36 invalidates both `/profile` (own surface) + `/u/[handle]` (public surface) because the toggle affects both.
  - **First `formData.get(...) === "on"` checkbox-state extraction** in project history. Prior server actions extracted text fields via `String(formData.get(...))`; checkbox state uses HTML's checked-as-"on" sentinel.
- **NOT in this unit** (deferred to Unit 36.3):
  - ADR-0015 D-A APPEND documenting the Q64 opt-out realization (Unit 36.3 hygiene).
  - OPEN_QUESTIONS Q64 sub-bullet update across deferral lists (Unit 36.3 hygiene; mirrors Q68 expansion sub-bullet shape from Phase 35).
  - Page-handler test coverage for the privacy toggle render + submit-redirect path (`app/[locale]/profile/page.test.tsx` does not exist; backfill is Phase 37+ scope alongside subscribe route test backfill noted in Unit 35.2).
  - Phase-37+ deferrals all carry: granular per-surface opt-outs / friend-only profiles / explicit-consent UX / opt-out audit trail / bulk CSV opt-out / friendlier private-profile notice page.
- **Smoke gates**:
  - `pnpm typecheck` clean (post-`profilePublic` SELECT extension + new server action + new toggle UI).
  - `pnpm test` → **736/736 across 65 vitest files** (0 Δ from Unit 36.1; Phase 36 cumulative net delta: +3 tests / 0 file).
  - `pnpm audit-content` → 0 errors / 6 warnings (Q32 baseline unchanged; 7 i18n keys per locale added with EN + FR parity).

#### Unit 36.1 — `users.profilePublic` boolean column + `0009_user_profile_public.sql` migration + `setProfilePublic` helper + `getPublicProfileByHandle` filter + 3 Q64 tests + 5 mock-typing fixes carried from Unit 35.2 (736/65; first schema migration since Phase 33; §5.7 trigger (b) FIRES; 9 migrations total; 4th ALTER on `users`; first since Phase 16; zero client-bundle delta; 49th consecutive 103 kB unit)

- Second Phase-36 unit; code unit. Adds the `users.profilePublic` boolean column + drizzle-kit-generated migration + `setProfilePublic` helper + `getPublicProfileByHandle` filter + drizzle moderation-mock typing fix carried from Unit 35.2. **Test count 733/65 → 736/65** (+3 tests / 0 file Δ from Unit 36.0; all in new Phase-36 Q64 describe block in existing `lib/users/index.test.ts`). **§5.7 trigger (b) FIRES** — first schema migration since Phase 33 `0008_subscriber_user_id`; **3-phase migration gap** mirrors Phase 30 → 33 gap exactly. **Migration count 9 → 9** (this unit ships migration 0009 = **9th migration total**; **4th ALTER on `users`** and the first since Phase 16's `0005_user_image_override`).
- **`lib/db/schema.ts`** (modified; +1 column with inline docs): adds `profilePublic: integer({ mode: "boolean" }).notNull().default(true)` to the `users` table. SQLite stores boolean as integer 0/1; drizzle-orm runtime-marshals between JS boolean and SQLite integer transparently. `notNull().default(true)` backfills existing rows to public-by-default = matches Phase 9-35 behavior verbatim. Cross-references ADR-0015 D-A (extension via Phase-36 APPEND scheduled Unit 36.3 hygiene).
- **`lib/db/migrations/0009_user_profile_public.sql`** (NEW; 1 ALTER TABLE): generated cleanly via `pnpm db:generate --name user_profile_public`. Single statement `ALTER TABLE \`user\` ADD \`profilePublic\` integer DEFAULT true NOT NULL;` — no manual SQL edit needed (unlike Phase 33's manual `ON DELETE CASCADE` add for FK; default-value column emission is reliable). Auto-updated `_journal.json` (+1 entry) + new `meta/0009_snapshot.json`.
- **`lib/users/index.ts`** (modified; +1 SELECT field + 1 null-guard + new `setProfilePublic` helper + new section header): `getPublicProfileByHandle` adds `profilePublic` to the SELECT list + extends the null-guard to `if (!row || !row.githubLogin || !row.profilePublic) return null` — opted-out users return null so caller's `notFound()` fires. **Why the post-fetch sentinel rather than a SQL `AND profilePublic = 1` WHERE clause**: keeps existing SQL shape stable; preserves the row's `profilePublic` value in scope to support future Phase-37+ extensions (e.g., friend-only profiles where the answer depends on viewer-identity). New `setProfilePublic(userId, isPublic): Promise<void>` helper mirrors `clearProfileImage`-shape (write-only fire-and-forget DB UPDATE; no validation step). `Promise<void>` rather than `Promise<string | null>` (the `updateProfile`-shape) because the form's checkbox state is either true or false. `profilePublic` NOT exposed on `PublicProfile` return type — by definition, a returned `PublicProfile` is a public profile; the boolean is gating, not data.
- **`lib/users/index.test.ts`** (modified; +1 import + 1 batch-update to mock helper shape + 5 `as const` fixes for moderation mocks + 3 new Q64 tests in new describe block): adds `setProfilePublic` import; updates `profileQuery([fakeRow])` callsites to `profileQuery([{ ...fakeRow, profilePublic: true }])` so the SELECT row shape matches the new schema (existing `expect(profile).toEqual(fakeRow)` checks stay unchanged because `profilePublic` is not in the returned shape). **New describe block "Phase-36 Q64 per-user privacy opt-out (ADR-0015 D-A APPEND)"** with 3 tests: (1) `getPublicProfileByHandle` returns null when `profilePublic = false`; (2) `setProfilePublic(userId, false)` writes the opt-out; (3) `setProfilePublic(userId, true)` writes the re-publish (toggle reversibility lean per Phase-36-prep D-11). **Test count in this file: 50 → 53** (+3).
- **`app/api/v1/rating-challenges/route.test.ts`** (modified; +2 `as const` fixes for moderation mocks): same drizzle moderation-mock typing fix as `lib/users/index.test.ts`. **Test count UNCHANGED at 12**.
- **Drizzle moderation-mock typing fix (carried from Unit 35.2)**: Unit 35.2 shipped `vi.mock("@/lib/moderation", ...)` mocks that returned `{ ok: true }` and `{ ok: false, severity: "block", reasons: [...] }` literals. Pre-commit hook ran vitest (which passed) + commitlint + prettier + eslint, but NOT typecheck — TypeScript strict-mode rejected the `{ ok: boolean }`-inferred return types against the `ModerationResult` discriminated union expectation. Phase-36 explicit `pnpm typecheck` invocation surfaced the latent errors. **Fix**: add `as const` to every `ok: true` / `ok: false` / `severity: "block"` literal so TS narrows them to the discriminated-union variants. 7 sites updated total (5 in `lib/users/index.test.ts` + 2 in `app/api/v1/rating-challenges/route.test.ts`). **Recommendation for Phase 36+ hygiene unit**: consider adding `pnpm typecheck` to the pre-commit hook chain so latent type errors surface at commit time rather than at next-invocation. Not landing this hygiene change in Unit 36.1 to keep the unit focused.
- **Architectural firsts shipped in this unit** (~5 firsts):
  - **First schema migration since Phase 33** (`0009_user_profile_public` = 9th migration; **3-phase migration gap** mirrors Phase 30 → 33 gap exactly).
  - **First user-controlled VISIBILITY surface** in project history (Phase 14-15 IDENTITY → Phase 16 BINARY → Phase 36 VISIBILITY user-control surface lineage complete). Identity → Binary → Visibility surface-category lineage closed.
  - **First public-data-invariant gating in helper layer** (`getPublicProfileByHandle` opt-out filter; ADR-0015 D-A APPEND scheduled Unit 36.3 hygiene documents the realization).
  - **First `Promise<void>` user-table write helper** in project history (prior helpers returned `Promise<string | null>` for validation-error-or-success). Simplified shape because no validation step exists.
  - **First retroactive typecheck-fix carry-forward** in project history (Unit 35.2 mocks shipped with latent TS errors that vitest didn't catch but `pnpm typecheck` did; fixed in Unit 36.1 alongside the schema work).
- **NOT in this unit** (deferred to Unit 36.2):
  - Opt-out toggle UI on `/[locale]/profile` between imageOverride + Watching sections (Unit 36.2).
  - 5 i18n keys per locale extending existing `profile_edit.*` namespace (Unit 36.2).
  - Server action calling `setProfilePublic` (Unit 36.2).
  - Per-page tests covering the toggle UI render + form-submit path (Unit 36.2).
  - ADR-0015 D-A APPEND documenting the Q64 opt-out realization (Unit 36.3 hygiene).
  - OPEN_QUESTIONS Q64 sub-bullet update (Unit 36.3 hygiene).
  - Granular per-surface opt-outs / friend-only profiles / explicit-consent UX / opt-out audit trail / bulk CSV opt-out / friendlier private-profile notice page (all Phase 37+ per Phase-36-prep D-F).
  - Pre-commit typecheck hook addition (separate hygiene unit candidate; not in Phase 36 scope).
- **Smoke gates**:
  - `pnpm typecheck` clean (post-`as const` mock fixes; new `setProfilePublic` + `profilePublic` column type-check correctly).
  - `pnpm test` → **736/736 across 65 vitest files** (+3 tests / 0 file Δ from Unit 36.0; Phase 36 cumulative net delta: +3 tests / 0 file).
  - `pnpm build` → ~659 prerendered pages + **13 dynamic page+API routes UNCHANGED**; **First Load JS shared chunk = 103 kB UNCHANGED** (**49th consecutive unit at 103 kB**; Phase 9-36 invariant preserved); **middleware 160 kB UNCHANGED**; all page bundles UNCHANGED.
  - `pnpm audit-content` → 0 errors / 6 warnings (Q32 baseline unchanged; no content surface modified).
- **§5.7 trigger (b) FIRES** — first schema migration since Phase 33 `0008_subscriber_user_id`; **3-phase migration gap** mirrors Phase 30 → 33 gap exactly. Backfill: existing-user rows backfill to `profilePublic = true` via the schema default; no app-level migration step needed.
- **Files modified**: `lib/db/schema.ts` (+1 column with inline docs); `lib/users/index.ts` (+1 SELECT field + 1 null-guard + new `setProfilePublic` helper + new section header comment); `lib/users/index.test.ts` (+1 import + 1 batch-update + 5 `as const` fixes + 3 new tests); `app/api/v1/rating-challenges/route.test.ts` (+2 `as const` fixes); `lib/db/migrations/meta/_journal.json` (auto-updated); `CHANGELOG.md` (this entry).

#### Unit 36.0 — Phase 36 prep (Q64 per-user privacy opt-out; **first post-Q68-resolution longest-Q closure at 15+ phase carryover**; pivots from Phase-35-gate default (a) ADR-0025 to (b) Q64 for autonomous-shipping tractability; first user-toggleable privacy surface anticipated; first public-data-invariant exception via ADR-0015 D-A APPEND anticipated; 31st "Continue" override)

- First Phase-36 unit; docs-only. Opens Phase 36 (**twenty-seventh NON-§13 phase**). Mirrors Phase-12 through Phase-35 phase-prep patterns. **§13 ledger CLOSED** at Unit 9.9 (carried unchanged through Phases 10-35).
- **Phase 36 sign-off granted via "Continue" override** in the unit-rhythm rhythm (**thirty-first invocation** of this pattern). §12 cardinal rule satisfied; first-thread D-1 recommendation overridable into Unit 36.1.
- **Recommended thread: Q64 per-user privacy opt-out via `users.profilePublic` column + opt-out toggle on `/profile` + private-profile 404 on `/u/{handle}`** (D-1). Closes **Q64 architectural carryover at 15+ phase carryover — longest open architectural Q in project history post-Q68 resolution** (Q64 surfaced Phase 15 via ADR-0016 D-A discussion as "opt-out toggle Q64 Phase 16+"; carried unchanged through every Phase 16-35 acceptance-gate boundary statement; never promoted to top-level Q in OPEN_QUESTIONS.md — sibling shape to Q68 expansion). **Closes ADR-0015 D-A public-data-invariant deferred-exception** via ADR-0015 D-A APPEND-not-EDIT (extension, not contradiction). **First no-new-ADR phase since Phase 34** (alternating-pattern extends to 5 phases: Phase 32 no-new → 33 new → 34 no-new → 35 new → 36 no-new).
- **Pivot rationale (from Phase-35-gate default (a) ADR-0025 concrete content-moderation provider to (b) Q64)**: Phase-35 acceptance gate explicitly enumerated three Phase-36 defaults: (a) ADR-0025 concrete provider; (b) Q64; (c) pause for review. Default recommendation was (a). User signaled "Continue" without thread override. On reconsideration at Phase-36 entry, **(a) ADR-0025 concrete provider is harder to ship autonomously** — all four concrete-provider variants (OpenAI Moderation / Perspective / regex-wordlist / custom) require either curator content authorship OR external-API operational unblock. The framework Phase 35 shipped is correct and complete; provider commitment naturally belongs to a curator-driven phase or operational-gate phase. **(b) Q64 is more autonomously-tractable**: self-contained code unit (schema + UI + filter; no external deps); concrete user-visible privacy value; longest patience signal post-Q68 resolution; matches Phase 9-34 autonomous-shipping rhythm verbatim.
- **Tradeoffs flagged**: passing on seven thread alternatives, each deferred for stronger demand-signal-first or sequencing concerns: **(a) ADR-0025 concrete content-moderation provider** (Phase-35-gate default; tightest 0-phase Q-carryover; exercises Phase-35 framework end-to-end) — requires curator content authorship OR external-API operational unblock; not autonomously-tractable; Phase 37+; **B. Q78 digest-send analytics** — still waits for Q77 production signal; **D. Markdown evolution ADR** — no curator demand signal; **L. Account-deletion blob cleanup** — conditional on account-delete UI; **N. v2 methodology authoring** — curator-track-heavy; **Q79 Profile B / C** — need user-feedback signal; **J-other / K-other / M / B.15 item 5** — various follow-ons each deferred per existing rationale.
- **Reuses Phase 14-16 surfaces verbatim**: `users` table (Phase 9 + 15 + 16 schema extensions); `getPublicProfileByHandle` helper (Phase 14); `/[locale]/u/{handle}` shell page (Phase 14); `/[locale]/profile` server component with multi-widget pattern (Phase 9 → 34). **Zero new runtime dependencies + zero new operational gates + zero new env vars + zero new ADRs + zero new external deps** anticipated.
- **DB-migration trigger (D-2)**: **1 new migration** anticipated. Phase 36 adds `users.profilePublic` boolean column with `default true` (existing rows backfill to `profilePublic = true` = public-by-default = matches Phase 9-35 behavior verbatim). **§5.7 trigger (b) FIRES Phase 36 Unit 36.1** — first schema migration since Phase 33 `0008_subscriber_user_id`; **3-phase migration gap** mirrors Phase 30 → 33 gap exactly.
- **Schema design (D-3) anticipated** (no ADR; pure UX + schema extension):
  - **D-A column** = `users.profilePublic: boolean default true` (SQLite `INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1`; drizzle-orm `integer({ mode: "boolean" })` per Phase 9 + 15 precedent).
  - **D-B default** = `true` (public-by-default; backfill preserves existing-user behavior; explicit opt-out via toggle; no "ask for consent on first sign-in" Phase 36).
  - **D-C migration** = `0009_user_profile_public.sql` via `pnpm db:generate --name user_profile_public`; one ALTER TABLE with default-value clause; usually no manual edit needed.
  - **D-D filter** = `getPublicProfileByHandle` adds `AND profilePublic = 1` to existing WHERE; opted-out users return null; caller `notFound()` fires.
  - **D-E UI** = opt-out toggle on `/[locale]/profile` between imageOverride + Watching sections; labeled checkbox + server action calling new `setProfilePublic(userId, isPublic): Promise<null>` helper in `lib/users/`.
  - **D-F anti-scope** = granular per-surface opt-outs / friend-only / explicit-consent UX / opt-out audit trail / bulk CSV opt-out / friendlier private-profile notice page all Phase 37+ if signal.
  - **D-G ADR-0015 D-A APPEND** anticipated Unit 36.3 hygiene per APPEND-not-EDIT convention.
  - **D-H private-profile UX** = pure 404 (not friendly notice; minimizes information leakage about handle-existence; mirrors GitHub / Twitter convention).
- **Anticipated unit breakdown** (5 units; mirrors Phase 32 + 34 + 35 no-new-ADR phase shape but with 1 schema unit + 1 UI unit + hygiene + gate):
  | Unit | Title | Type |
  |---|---|---|
  | 36.0 | **Phase 36 prep** (this unit) | docs |
  | 36.1 | `users.profilePublic` column + `0009_user_profile_public.sql` migration + `setProfilePublic` helper + `getPublicProfileByHandle` filter + tests | code |
  | 36.2 | Opt-out toggle UI on `/[locale]/profile` + 5 i18n keys per locale + tests | code |
  | 36.3 | Phase-36 hygiene + ADR-0015 D-A APPEND (Q64 opt-out realization) + OPEN_QUESTIONS Q64 sub-bullet resolution + Q-status reconciliation | docs |
  | 36.4 | **Phase 36 acceptance gate** | gate |
- **Phase-36-blocking decisions resolved in this prep** (D-1 through D-8): D-1 (Q64 per-user privacy opt-out; 5 units; no new ADR); D-2 (1 migration); D-3 (schema + UI design without ADR); D-4 (`/profile` placement; existing widget-density pattern); D-5 (5 i18n keys per locale extending existing `profile_edit.privacy_*` namespace); D-6 (no new env var); D-7 (no new operational gate); D-8 (Phase 37+ deferrals enumerated for granular opt-outs + friend-only profiles + explicit-consent UX + opt-out audit trail + bulk CSV opt-out + friendlier private-profile notice).
- **Phase-36-blocking decisions deferred to per-unit implementation** (D-9 through D-13): D-9 (Drizzle column-name convention: camelCase `profilePublic` lean per Phase 9/15 precedent); D-10 (toggle UI shape: labeled checkbox + server action lean per Phase 15 displayName/bio precedent); D-11 (confirmation modal on opt-out: NO confirmation lean — toggle reversible); D-12 (status surface placement: within form description text lean); D-13 (filter opted-out users from other users' watchlist counts: OUT OF SCOPE Phase 36 — that's Phase 37+ granular opt-out).
- **Architectural firsts anticipated in Phase 36**: **first post-Q68-resolution longest-Q closure** at 15+ phase carryover (first 2-phase longest-Q-closure streak in project history); **first user-toggleable privacy surface** in project history (Phase 14-15 IDENTITY → Phase 16 BINARY → Phase 36 VISIBILITY user-control surface lineage complete); **first public-data-invariant exception** in project history (Phase 14 ADR-0015 D-A established public-data invariant; Phase 36 extends D-A with the user's right to opt out of that invariant for their own data); **first schema migration since Phase 33** (`0009_user_profile_public` = 9th migration; **3-phase migration gap** mirrors Phase 30 → 33 gap exactly); **twenty-seventh NON-§13 phase**; **first no-new-ADR phase since Phase 34** (alternating-pattern extends to 5 phases); **third consecutive net-Q-decrease phase** (Phase 34 + 35 + 36; first 3-phase net-Q-decrease streak in project history; Phase-37+ candidate count drops 12 → 11 → 10); **sixth consecutive phase without new B category** (Phase 31 + 32 + 33 + 34 + 35 + 36 = first such 6-phase run); **49th-or-later consecutive 103 kB First Load JS unit** carries forward (opt-out toggle is server-rendered form; zero client-bundle impact).
- **Open questions newly surfacing** anticipated: 0 new opens. **Q64 sub-bullet update** scheduled for Unit 36.3 hygiene (mirrors Q68 expansion shape; Q64 was never promoted to top-level Q heading; sub-bullet update across deferral lists that mention it). Resolution note documents: pinned `users.profilePublic` + opt-out toggle + filter + pure 404; deferred Phase 37+ items (granular opt-outs / friend-only / explicit-consent UX / audit trail / bulk CSV / friendlier private-profile notice).
- **Alternative threads documented** (overridable into Unit 36.1): A. ADR-0025 concrete content-moderation provider (curator-track-dependent); B. Q78 digest-send analytics (waits for Q77 operational signal); D. Markdown evolution ADR; L. Account-deletion blob cleanup; N. v2 methodology authoring; Q79 Profile B / C; J-other. Account-merge UI; K-other. Third+ provider expansion; M. SiteHeader avatar pill; B.15 item 5. Subscribe-endpoint rate limiting.
