- **Phase 35 vs prior new-ADR phases**: Phase 35 = **15+ new-ADR phase** in project history. **First framework-only ADR** distinguishes ADR-0024 from prior provider-committed ADRs (ADR-0008 Anthropic; ADR-0017 Vercel Blob; ADR-0021 Resend; ADR-0022 Vercel Cron; ADR-0023 Option A FK). **First "alternating no-new-ADR with new-ADR sandwiched" 4-phase pattern** in project history (Phase 32 no-new → Phase 33 new → Phase 34 no-new → Phase 35 new).
- **ADR-0024 framework-only architectural pattern summary**: Phase 35 establishes the project's first ADR pattern that separates "what's the shape" from "which provider implements it." The 5-unit phase shape ships: foundation infra (Unit 35.1: ADR + module + 8 tests) + multi-surface integration (Unit 35.2: 4 surfaces + 3 tests + env var) + hygiene (Unit 35.3: 3 APPENDs + Q-resolution-notes) + gate (this unit). **Framework-only-ADR pattern playbook** documented in ADR-0024 + cross-referenced from the OPEN_QUESTIONS.md Q68 sub-bullets; future framework-first decisions (Q72 markdown evolution; future deferred-provider Qs) can reference this pattern as proven.
- **Phase 36 entry conditions**: Per §12 cardinal rule, **explicit human sign-off required**. **Thirty-first "Continue" override opportunity** in Unit 36.0 prep. Thread options enumerated above. **Default recommendation = (a) ADR-0025 concrete content-moderation provider commitment** — would exercise the Phase-35 framework + demonstrate the framework-only-ADR pattern's full lifecycle (interface → noop default → concrete provider) within 2 phases; tightest 0-phase Q-carryover available; mirrors Phase 30 → 31 → 32 tight-follow-on cadence. **Alternative defaults: (b) Q64** if longest-carrying signal preferred (15+ phase carryover post-Q68 resolution); **(c) Q78** still waits for Q77 operational signal; **(d) v2 methodology authoring** (curator-track per release-readiness audit). The **release-readiness-audit-shipped-pre-Phase-35** + the framework-only-ADR shape together suggest a natural milestone-pause point for human review of (i) overall release-readiness trajectory and (ii) whether the Phase-35 framework-only pattern proves itself in Phase 36+ before extending it to Q72 / Q64 / etc.
- THINK artifact: `docs/thinking/35.4-phase-35-acceptance-gate.md`.

#### Unit 35.3 — Phase-35 hygiene + 3 ADR APPENDs (0016 D-B + 0017 D-H + 0019 D-F) + OPEN_QUESTIONS Q68 expansion realization-note edits across 3 sub-bullets (first sub-bullet-only Q resolution in project history; first 3-APPEND single unit in project history; 9 ADR D-clauses with APPENDs total; 5 consecutive phases shipping at least one APPEND)

- Fourth Phase-35 unit; docs-only. Hygiene catalog + 3 ADR APPENDs + OPEN_QUESTIONS Q68 expansion realization-note edits. **Class A items: 0** (twenty-fourth consecutive phase ships with 0 Class A items).
- **Class B status (post-Phase-35 delta)**: ~3 across 14 categories UNCHANGED from Phase 34 close. Phase 35 does NOT touch B catalog directly (Q68 expansion was a Q-candidate sub-bullet, not a B-item). **Five consecutive phases without new B category** (Phase 31 + 32 + 33 + 34 + 35) — extends the Phase-31-onwards quad-phase streak to quintuple. **First such 5-phase run in project history**.
- **OPEN_QUESTIONS updates (Q68 expansion realization; 0 net Q-tally changes)**:
  - **Q68 expansion** sub-bullets updated in **3 places** (Q63 Phase-15 ADR-0016 D-B deferral list + Q67 Phase-16 ADR-0017 D-H deferral list + Q70 Phase-19 ADR-0019 D-F deferral list). Each sub-bullet now bears strikethrough on the original "Phase X+ if abuse signals" framing + "RESOLVED architecturally Phase 35 Unit 35.1 → 35.3 via [ADR-0024](./docs/adr/0024-content-moderation.md) framework-only with NoopModerator default" prose + Phase-36+ deferral cross-refs.
  - **No top-level `## Q68. ...` heading** — Q68 expansion was never promoted to top-level Q. Resolution edits the sub-bullets only. **First sub-bullet-only Q resolution** in project history (prior candidate Qs that resolved — Q66 Phase 17, Q67 Phase 16, Q70 Phase 19 — were all promoted to top-level first).
  - **Q-tally** (mechanical `Status:`-field tally per Unit 9.8 convention): 28 resolved + 4 lean + 34 open = **66 total UNCHANGED**. Q68 expansion resolution doesn't shift the top-level tally; only closes a sub-bullet candidate.
  - **Sibling-Q cross-refs SKIPPED** despite Phase-35-prep anticipation. Q66 + Q70 are both already resolved with concrete providers (ADR-0018 + ADR-0019); cross-refs to already-resolved Qs are not load-bearing. Q72 markdown evolution (closest open sibling) doesn't map cleanly to framework-only-pattern (markdown extensions are about which features to support, not which provider to delegate to). Framework-only-pattern playbook reference lives in ADR-0024 itself.
- **3 ADR APPENDs shipped** per the APPEND-not-EDIT phase-tagged convention:
  - **ADR-0016 D-B** (Phase-15 bio validation/sanitization model) — **20-phase source-to-APPEND gap** (Phase 15 → Phase 35); documents `lib/users/updateProfile()` moderation call between bio validation + DB UPDATE per ADR-0024 D-D bio surface; noop default preserves Phase 15-34 trust-users posture verbatim; demand-signal-first concern dissolved by zero-cost-default framework. **First APPEND on ADR-0016 D-B**.
  - **ADR-0017 D-H** (Phase-17 Phase-17+ deferrals list; Q68 expansion sub-bullet) — **18-phase source-to-APPEND gap** (Phase 17 → Phase 35); documents `lib/users/updateProfileImage()` moderation call AFTER MIME / size / magic-byte validation + BEFORE `putAvatar` Vercel Blob upload per ADR-0024 D-D avatar surface; refused images never touch paid storage. **First APPEND on ADR-0017 D-H**.
  - **ADR-0019 D-F** (Phase-19 Phase-20+ inheritance contract; row 1 Q68 expansion content moderation) — **16-phase source-to-APPEND gap** (Phase 19 → Phase 35); documents **one path correction** vs original D-F anticipation (moderation call sits in `lib/users/updateProfileImage()` BEFORE `putAvatar` rather than inside sharp pipeline between `.rotate()` and `.toBuffer()`; future Phase-36+ pixel-data-post-rotation provider can refactor); framework contract doesn't constrain pre/post-rotation. **First APPEND on ADR-0019 D-F**.
  - **ADR-0021 NOT APPENDed** despite being on Phase-35-prep list — no natural D-clause attachment point (subscribe-surface moderation wasn't an ADR-0021 commitment; was added per ADR-0024 D-D + D-13). Subscribe integration documented in ADR-0024's Cross-references + CHANGELOG Unit 35.2 entry; ADR-0021 APPEND would be ceremony without attachment point.
- **ADR count stays at 24** (Phase 35 = 1 new ADR via Unit 35.1 ADR-0024; no D-clause changes via APPEND-not-EDIT). **9 ADR D-clauses with APPENDs total** (ADR-0013 D-F + ADR-0018 D-G + ADR-0021 D-F + ADR-0022 D-D + ADR-0021 D-C + ADR-0023 D-H + ADR-0016 D-B + ADR-0017 D-H + ADR-0019 D-F = 9 distinct D-clauses across the project's ADR set). Strongest remaining Phase-36+ Q-candidates after Q68 resolution: Q78 architectural digest-send analytics (still waits for Q77 production signal); Q64 per-user privacy opt-out (15+ phase carryover; longest open architectural); Markdown evolution ADR (Q72); account-merge UI (ADR-0020 D-E).
- **Phase-36+ Q-candidates flagged** (11 total; was 12 at Phase 34 close after Q68 expansion sub-bullet resolution): 6 carried from Phase 27 (Q60 / Q61 / Q62 / Q64 / Q65 / Q72; Q68 expansion now resolved) + 2 carried from Phase 28 (Q73 + Q74) + 1 carried from Phase 30 (Q75) + 2 carried from Phase 31 (Q77 + Q78) + 0 from Phase 33 (Q79 resolved Phase 34) + 0 from Phase 34 + **0 NEW Phase 35**. **Slot net-decrease: 12 → 11 candidates**; Phase 36+ thread menu is one item shorter (second net-decrease phase in a row — Phase 34 resolved Q79; Phase 35 resolves Q68 expansion).

#### Unit 35.2 — Extension-point integration into bio-write + avatar-upload + rating-challenge + subscribe surfaces + `.env.example` MODERATION_PROVIDER + 3 per-surface refusal tests (733/65); env vars 12 → 13; zero client-bundle delta; 47th consecutive 103 kB unit; corrects two prep-doc path assumptions (no app/api/v1/profile/update + no lib/images/transcode)

- Third Phase-35 unit; code unit. Wires the ADR-0024 `ContentModerator` factory into all four existing user-content surfaces per ADR-0024 D-D. **Test count 730/65 → 733/65** (+3 tests / 0 new files; all in existing test files). **Env-var count 12 → 13** (`MODERATION_PROVIDER` added with default `noop` documented in `.env.example`). **First Load JS shared chunk = 103 kB UNCHANGED** (47th consecutive unit at 103 kB; Phase 9-35 invariant preserved; server-side integration only with zero client-bundle impact); middleware 160 kB UNCHANGED; all page bundles UNCHANGED. **Dynamic page+API routes 13 UNCHANGED** (no new routes; modifications only).
- **Reality-check vs Phase-35-prep D-D file plan**: two prep-doc paths corrected to actual paths.
  - **Bio-write**: prep said `app/api/v1/profile/update/route.ts` (does not exist); actual integration is `lib/users/index.ts` `updateProfile()` (bio-write is an in-page server action calling the lib helper; no dedicated API route).
  - **Avatar upload**: prep said `lib/images/transcode.ts` between `.rotate()` and `.toBuffer()` (no `lib/images/`; sharp pipeline lives in `lib/storage/putAvatar`); actual integration is `lib/users/index.ts` `updateProfileImage()` BEFORE the `putAvatar` call (refused image never touches paid Vercel Blob; simpler than refactoring storage to take a moderator dependency).
  - Rating-challenge + subscribe paths matched prep exactly.
  - ADR-0024 text refers to anticipatory path (`lib/images/transcode.ts`); the framework contract doesn't constrain pre/post-rotation; future Phase-36+ provider that needs pixel-data-post-rotation can refactor. No ADR-0024 edit required.
- **`lib/users/index.ts`** (modified; +1 import + 2 moderation gates): adds `getModerator` import. **Bio surface (updateProfile)**: moderation call inserted between bio validation + DB UPDATE; only fires when `set.bio` is non-empty (clear-by-empty-submit short-circuits per ADR-0016 D-B); returns moderator's first reason string OR generic fallback on `severity: "block"` per ADR-0024 D-11 lean. `displayName` NOT moderated per ADR-0024 D-12 lean (short-form field). **Avatar surface (updateProfileImage)**: moderation call inserted AFTER MIME / size / magic-byte validation (so moderation only runs on bytes that pass cheap checks) + BEFORE existing-image lookup + putAvatar upload (refused image never touches paid Vercel Blob; never triggers delete-on-replace of prior image either). Double-reads file buffer (once for moderation, once inside putAvatar's sharp pipeline); cost is microseconds on the 2 MB cap.
- **`app/api/v1/rating-challenges/route.ts`** (modified; +1 import + 1 moderation gate + 1 `moderationRefused()` helper): adds `getModerator` import + new `moderationRefused(field, message): Response` helper returning HTTP 422 + `{ error: "moderation-refused", field, message }`. Moderation call inserted AFTER all validation (problemSlug + dimension + proposedValue + rationale length); only `rationale:` is moderated per ADR-0024 D-12 lean. Returns 422 (new shape) — distinguishes from 400 validation-failure shape so callers can render different UX (block-icon vs validation-warning-icon).
- **`app/api/v1/subscribe/route.ts`** (modified; +1 import + 1 moderation gate): adds `getModerator` import. Moderation call inserted AFTER email validation but BEFORE `createOrRefreshPendingSubscription` DB write (refused email never creates a DB row). `userIdOrEmail` is the email itself (subscribe surface is the only one where actor may not be signed in). Per ADR-0024 D-13 lean: moderate email-as-text; noop default makes it costless; future regex-wordlist provider can refuse slur-containing emails without further wiring change.
- **`.env.example`** (modified; +1 new Phase-35 section documenting `MODERATION_PROVIDER`): documents default `noop` + future Phase-36+ values (`openai` / `perspective` / `regex-wordlist` / `custom`) + fail-loud-on-misconfiguration posture per ADR-0024 D-E. **Env-var count: 12 → 13**.
- **`lib/users/index.test.ts`** (modified; +1 module mock + 1 new import + 1 new describe block with 2 tests): mocks `@/lib/moderation` with default-noop-pass-through (`{ ok: true }` for both methods); imports `getModerator`; adds `Content moderation integration (ADR-0024 D-D)` describe block with 2 tests — (1) bio surface: moderator-blocked → `updateProfile` returns reason string + DB UPDATE skipped; (2) avatar surface: moderator-blocked → `updateProfileImage` returns reason string + `putAvatar` not called. **Test count in this file: 48 → 50** (+2).
- **`app/api/v1/rating-challenges/route.test.ts`** (modified; +1 module mock + 1 new import + 1 new test): mocks `@/lib/moderation` with default-noop-pass-through; imports `getModerator`; adds 1 new test verifying 422 moderation-refused with correct error shape (`{ error: "moderation-refused", field: "rationale", message }`) + `submitChallenge` not called. **Test count in this file: 11 → 12** (+1).
- **`app/api/v1/subscribe/route.test.ts`** — **NOT modified** (file does not exist). Subscribe route shipped Phase 30 without route-level tests; backfill is Phase 36+ scope. Subscribe moderation wiring verified by code-review + the same pattern proven by rating-challenges + lib/users surfaces. Documented as a known gap surviving Unit 35.2.
- **i18n keys** — **0 added this unit**. The framework returns provider-specific reason strings; API responses surface them directly in the JSON `message` field. UI consumers render the returned message verbatim under existing error-surface styling. The noop default never produces a refusal message, so no user-facing string is shipped Phase 35. Per-surface custom i18n-keyed UX deferred Phase 36+ per ADR-0024 D-H + Phase-35-prep D-11 lean.
- **Architectural firsts shipped in this unit** (~5 firsts):
  - **First multi-surface extension-point wiring from a single ADR** in project history. Four surfaces (bio + avatar + rating-challenge + subscribe) wired in one unit vs accreted-via-copy pattern of `safeAuth()` (4 copies across 4 phases). ADR-0024 D-D promise realized.
  - **First HTTP 422 moderation-refused response shape** in project history. Distinguishes moderation refusal from 400 validation-failure + 401 unauthenticated + 404 not-found.
  - **First demand-signal-first-dissolution wiring realized**. Framework + extension points ship without provider commitment + without pre-paying for unobserved abuse.
  - **First env-var-count increase since Phase 31** (Phase 31 added `CRON_SECRET`; Phase 32-34 added zero env vars; Phase 35.2 adds `MODERATION_PROVIDER`). **13 env-var milestone**.
  - **First "buffer double-read for moderation + storage" pattern** in project history. Justified by the 2 MB upload cap + microsecond overhead + simpler than refactoring storage layer.
- **NOT in this unit** (deferred to Unit 35.3 hygiene):
  - ADR-0016 D-B / ADR-0017 D-H / ADR-0019 D-F / ADR-0021 APPEND notes documenting the framework realization at each surface.
  - OPEN_QUESTIONS Q68 expansion status flip → `resolved` with resolution-note prose covering framework pinning + Phase 36+ deferrals.
  - Q66 / Q70 sibling-Q advisory cross-refs to ADR-0024 as framework-only-pattern playbook.
  - Subscribe route test file backfill (Phase 36+ scope).
  - Per-surface custom i18n-keyed UX (Phase 36+ polish).
  - Concrete provider implementations (`OpenAIModerator` / `PerspectiveModerator` / `RegexWordlistModerator`) — all Phase 36+ operational-API-gate.
  - Persistent moderation-decision audit log + migration — Phase 36+ when provider commits.
- **Smoke gates**:
  - `pnpm typecheck` clean (all 4 surfaces type-check; mock shapes satisfy `ContentModerator` interface).
  - `pnpm test` → **733/733 across 65 vitest files** (+3 tests / 0 file Δ from Unit 35.1; Phase 35 cumulative net delta: +11 tests / +2 files).
  - `pnpm build` → ~659 prerendered pages + **13 dynamic page+API routes UNCHANGED**; **First Load JS shared chunk = 103 kB UNCHANGED** (**47th consecutive unit at 103 kB**; Phase 9-35 invariant preserved); **middleware 160 kB UNCHANGED**; all page bundles UNCHANGED.
  - `pnpm audit-content` → 0 errors / 6 warnings (Q32 baseline unchanged; no content surface modified).
- **§5.7 trigger (b) cold this unit** — pure code-integration; no schema change. Persistent moderation-decision audit log deferred Phase 36+ (would fire trigger (b) when shipped).
- **Files modified**: `lib/users/index.ts` (+1 import + 2 moderation gates); `app/api/v1/rating-challenges/route.ts` (+1 import + 1 moderation gate + 1 helper); `app/api/v1/subscribe/route.ts` (+1 import + 1 moderation gate); `.env.example` (+1 Phase-35 section); `lib/users/index.test.ts` (+1 module mock + 2 tests); `app/api/v1/rating-challenges/route.test.ts` (+1 module mock + 1 test); `CHANGELOG.md` (this entry).
- **Files added**: `docs/thinking/35.2-extension-point-integration.md` (NEW; THINK artifact).
- **Parallel-curator awareness**: this unit modifies 6 source files + 1 env file + 2 test files. **MEDIUM collision risk** per Phase-35-prep D-8 — parallel session should yield if it overlaps any of these surfaces. Working tree clean at unit start (verified pre-edit via `git status --short`); will be clean post-commit modulo prettier reformat.
- THINK artifact: `docs/thinking/35.2-extension-point-integration.md`.

#### Unit 35.1 — ADR-0024 framework-only content moderation + `lib/moderation/` (types + noop + factory + tests); **24 ADRs**; first framework-only ADR in project history; +8 tests / +2 files (722/63 → 730/65); first `lib/moderation/` module; no env-var addition this unit (deferred to 35.2 with first integration call site); zero client-bundle delta

- Second Phase-35 unit; code+ADR unit. Ships ADR-0024 framework-only content moderation interface + `NoopModerator` default + factory + tests. **Test count 722/63 → 730/65** (+8 tests / +2 files; both new test files in `lib/moderation/`). **First framework-only ADR in project history** — ADR-0024 commits to the `ContentModerator` interface shape without committing to a provider in the same ADR; future provider (`OpenAIModerator` / `PerspectiveModerator` / `RegexWordlistModerator` / `CustomModerator`) lands Phase 36+ alongside operational-API-gate decision. **ADR count 23 → 24**. **First Load JS shared chunk = 103 kB UNCHANGED** (46th consecutive unit at 103 kB; Phase 9-35 invariant preserved); middleware 160 kB UNCHANGED; `lib/moderation/` is pure server-side with zero client-bundle impact. **`lib/moderation/` new directory** = third deferred-provider-pattern dir after `lib/email/` (Phase 30 Resend) + `lib/cron/` (Phase 31 Vercel Cron) but **first with no-op default** rather than committed provider.
- **`docs/adr/0024-content-moderation.md` (NEW; ~280 lines)**: framework-only ADR per Phase-35-prep D-3. Closes Q68 expansion at **12+ phase carryover** — strongest non-just-surfaced patience-signal closure in project history. **D-A interface** = single `ContentModerator` with `moderateText(text, ctx)` + `moderateImage(buffer, ctx)` (single not split per D-9 lean). **D-B result** = discriminated union `{ ok: true } | { ok: false; reasons: ReadonlyArray<string>; severity: "block" | "warn" }` (two-state severity per D-10 lean; numeric provider scores map to two-state at framework boundary). **D-C context** = `{ surface: "bio" | "avatar" | "rating-challenge" | "subscribe"; userIdOrEmail: string }` (per-surface tuning for future providers; noop ignores). **D-D default** = `NoopModerator` returning `{ ok: true }` for both methods; zero API cost / latency / FP. **D-E factory** = `getModerator()` with lazy singleton + env-var dispatch + throw-on-unknown (fail-loud on misconfiguration per Phase-9 `safeAuth()` posture). **D-F test reset hook** = `__resetModeratorForTests()` mirroring Phase-30 `lib/email/` pattern. **D-G env var** = `MODERATION_PROVIDER` (deferred to Unit 35.2 with first integration call site to keep this unit pure-framework + parallel-safe; 12 → 13 env vars at Unit 35.2 close). **D-H Phase 36+ deferrals** = concrete provider implementations + persistent moderation-decision audit log + per-user appeal mechanism + bulk-rescan + policy versioning + per-surface severity-threshold tuning + block-vs-warn UX divergence + async-webhook moderation. **4 options considered** with Pros/Cons table: framework-only + noop (chosen); OpenAI Moderation committed-provider; defer entirely; framework + regex-wordlist default.
- **`lib/moderation/types.ts` (NEW; ~40 LoC)**: `ModerationResult` discriminated union + `ModerationContext` interface + `ContentModerator` interface. All exported from `index.ts` re-exports. Server-only.
- **`lib/moderation/noop.ts` (NEW; ~25 LoC)**: `NoopModerator` class implementing `ContentModerator`; both methods async-wrap `Promise.resolve({ ok: true })`. Underscore-prefixed unused parameters (`_text` / `_buffer` / `_ctx`) suppress eslint unused-warnings while preserving the interface signature (required because callers typing the class directly call with arguments — TypeScript strict-mode arg-count check enforced).
- **`lib/moderation/index.ts` (NEW; ~75 LoC)**: factory + lazy singleton + env-var dispatch + `__resetModeratorForTests()` + re-exports of `NoopModerator` + types. Dispatch: empty-string / unset / `"noop"` → `NoopModerator`; unknown → throws `Error` with clear message listing recognized values (currently `"noop"`; future providers extend dispatch table). Module-level state: `moderatorInstance: ContentModerator | null = null` lazily initialized.
- **`lib/moderation/noop.test.ts` (NEW; 2 tests)**: verifies `NoopModerator.moderateText()` returns `ok: true` for empty / benign / adversarial-looking input + `moderateImage()` returns `ok: true` for empty / small / large buffers. Documents the noop's pass-through invariant: input content does NOT influence output.
- **`lib/moderation/index.test.ts` (NEW; 6 tests)**: verifies factory returns `NoopModerator` for unset / empty-string / literal-`"noop"` env values; verifies singleton is cached across calls within the same env; verifies factory throws clear error with provider-name + recognized-values on unknown values (e.g., `"openni"` typo); verifies `__resetModeratorForTests()` clears the singleton so subsequent calls re-read env. `ORIGINAL_PROVIDER` capture + `beforeEach` + `afterEach` ensures test isolation + restores environment on cleanup.
- **Architectural firsts shipped in this unit** (~6 firsts):
  - **First framework-only ADR in project history** — ADR-0024 commits to interface without provider in same ADR; establishes playbook for [Q66](OPEN_QUESTIONS.md) (markdown evolution) + [Q70](OPEN_QUESTIONS.md) (EXIF stripping) + future framework-first decisions.
  - **First `lib/<module>/` with no-op default** rather than committed provider (vs `lib/email/` Resend + `lib/cron/` Vercel Cron pattern). Demonstrates the deferred-provider pattern's framework half can ship standalone.
  - **First ADR Pros/Cons table with 4+ options** in project history (most prior ADRs enumerated 2-3 options; ADR-0024 enumerates 4 with full Pros/Cons each).
  - **First throw-on-unknown-config pattern** (factory throws clear error rather than silent-fallback to default). Establishes Phase-9 `safeAuth()` "fail loud on config error" pattern reuse for non-auth configs.
  - **First test file count delta in Phase 35** (63 → 65; +2 files; matches Phase-30 Unit 30.1 + Phase-22 Unit 22.1 ADR-shipping-with-code unit shape).
  - **First "ADR + first-code-shipping-the-ADR-decisions in one unit" pattern** carried forward from Phase 22 + 30. Establishes the rhythm: ADR + module + tests = 1 unit; multi-surface integration = separate unit (Unit 35.2).
- **NOT in this unit** (deferred to Unit 35.2):
  - `.env.example` addition of `MODERATION_PROVIDER` (lands Unit 35.2 alongside first integration call site; keeps Unit 35.1 pure-framework + parallel-safe).
  - Integration into `users.bio` write path (ADR-0016 D-B) + avatar upload pipeline (ADR-0019 D-F integration point between `.rotate()` and `.toBuffer()`) + rating-challenge `rationale:` text submission (Phase 11) + subscribe-email submission (ADR-0021 D-G).
  - Per-surface refusal-handling UX (Unit 35.2; generic 422 on `block` only at Phase 35; per-surface custom messaging Phase 36+ polish per ADR-0024 D-H).
  - ADR-0016 D-B / ADR-0017 D-H / ADR-0019 D-F / ADR-0021 APPEND notes documenting framework realization (Unit 35.3 hygiene).
  - OPEN_QUESTIONS Q68 expansion status flip → resolved (Unit 35.3 hygiene with resolution-note prose covering framework pinning + Phase 36+ deferrals).
  - Concrete provider implementations (`OpenAIModerator` / `PerspectiveModerator` / `RegexWordlistModerator`) — all Phase 36+.
  - Persistent moderation-decision audit log + migration — Phase 36+ when provider commitment lands; would fire §5.7 trigger (b).
- **Smoke gates**:
  - `pnpm typecheck` clean (post-`NoopModerator` parameter-declaration fix; underscore-prefix suppresses eslint unused).
  - `pnpm test` → **730/730 across 65 vitest files** (+8 / +2 files Δ from Unit 35.0; both new test files in `lib/moderation/`; Phase 35 test net delta so far: +8 / +2 files).
  - `pnpm build` → ~659 prerendered pages + **13 dynamic page+API routes UNCHANGED** (no new routes this unit); **First Load JS shared chunk = 103 kB UNCHANGED** (46th consecutive unit at 103 kB; Phase 9-35 invariant preserved); **middleware 160 kB UNCHANGED**; all page bundles UNCHANGED (zero client-bundle delta from new `lib/moderation/` directory).
  - `pnpm audit-content` → 0 errors / 6 warnings (Q32 baseline unchanged; no content surface modified).
- **§5.7 trigger (b) cold this unit** — framework-only code change; no schema modification. Persistent moderation-decision audit log deferred Phase 36+ (would fire trigger (b) when shipped).

#### Unit 35.0 — Phase 35 prep (Q68 expansion content moderation as framework-only ADR-0024 with `NoopModerator` default; 30th "Continue" override; **twenty-sixth NON-§13 phase**; first framework-only ADR + first multi-surface extension-point wiring + first demand-signal-dissolution pattern anticipated)

- First Phase-35 unit; docs-only. Opens Phase 35 (**twenty-sixth NON-§13 phase**). Mirrors Phase-12 through Phase-34 phase-prep patterns. **§13 ledger CLOSED** at Unit 9.9 (carried unchanged through Phases 10-34).
- **Phase 35 sign-off granted via "Continue" override** in the unit-rhythm rhythm (**thirtieth invocation** of this pattern). §12 cardinal rule satisfied; first-thread D-1 recommendation overridable into Unit 35.1.
- **Recommended thread: Q68 expansion content moderation as framework-only ADR-0024 with `NoopModerator` default** (D-1). Closes **Q68 expansion at 12+ phase carryover — strongest non-just-surfaced patience signal** (originally deferred Phase 15 → ADR-0016 D-B; re-expanded Phase 17 ADR-0017 D-H → uploaded images; re-expanded Phase 19/20 ADR-0019 D-F → transcoded images; carried unchanged through every Phase 21-34 acceptance-gate boundary statement). **Framework-only strategy** ("ship Q68 framework as architectural commitment now without external API" per Phase-34 gate option (b)): defines `ContentModerator` interface + ships `NoopModerator` default + wires extension points into FOUR existing surfaces simultaneously (bio-write + avatar-upload + rating-challenge-text + subscribe-email) — without committing to OpenAI moderation / Perspective / regex-wordlist provider. Provider commitment becomes Phase-36+ operational-API-gate parallel to Q73 / Q75 / Q77.
- **Demand-signal-first concern resolution**: Q68 was deferred 12+ phases on "no users yet → no abuse signals → no demand." The framework-only-with-no-op default dissolves this: zero API cost, zero latency, zero false-positive rate; shipping the framework now does NOT pre-pay for unobserved abuse. Pre-positions the extension points so the future provider swap-in is a single env-var change + one new file per provider, not a multi-surface refactor.
- **ADR-0024 framework shape anticipated** (D-3 in prep; ships Unit 35.1):
  - **Interface** = `ContentModerator` in `lib/moderation/types.ts` with `moderateText(text, ctx) → ModerationResult` + `moderateImage(buffer, ctx) → ModerationResult`; `ModerationResult` = `{ ok: true } | { ok: false; reasons[]; severity: "block" | "warn" }`.
  - **Default provider** = `NoopModerator` in `lib/moderation/noop.ts`; both methods return `{ ok: true }` synchronously-wrapped-in-promise.
  - **Factory** = `getModerator()` in `lib/moderation/index.ts`; reads `MODERATION_PROVIDER` env (default `noop`); singleton-cached.
  - **Integration points** (Unit 35.2 wires all four; each single-line `await getModerator().moderate*(…)` + 422 refusal-handling): `users.bio` write path + avatar upload pipeline (between `.rotate()` and `.toBuffer()` per ADR-0019 D-F inheritance contract) + rating-challenge `rationale:` text submission + subscribe-email submission.
  - **Env var** = `MODERATION_PROVIDER` (12 → 13 env vars; default `noop`; documented future values `openai` / `perspective` / `regex-wordlist`).
  - **Phase 36+ deferrals**: concrete provider implementations; persistent moderation-decision audit log + new table + migration; per-user appeal mechanism; bulk-rescan of historical content (reuses Phase-20 backfill pattern flagged in Q68 expansion documentation); moderation-policy versioning; per-surface severity-threshold tuning.
- **Tradeoffs flagged**: passing on seven thread alternatives, each deferred for stronger demand-signal-first or sequencing concerns:
  - **B. Q78 digest-send analytics** (tightest 1-phase Q-carryover) — still waits for first-week production cron signal from Q77 (still pending operationally). Phase 36+ once Q77 operational.
  - **D. Markdown evolution ADR** (Q72 + Phase-29 B.14 wikilink couple) — no curator demand signal. Phase 36+.
  - **G. Q64 per-user privacy opt-out** (15+ phase carryover; longest open architectural Q) — same "no users yet" deferral; no equivalent zero-cost-default posture (opt-out flag without UI is half-shipped). Phase 36+ if framework-only-pattern proves cleanly extensible from Phase 35 ADR-0024 precedent.
  - **L. Account-deletion blob cleanup** (Phase-16 carryover) — exercises Phase-33 ADR-0023 D-D cascade; conditional on account-delete UI (none yet). Phase 36+.
  - **N. v2 methodology authoring** — per release-readiness audit §2.9 = primary blocker for §1 (d) success metric; curator-track-heavy; requires human direction. Phase 36+.
  - **Q79 Profile B full-edit / Profile C analytics** — Phase 34 just resolved Profile A; B + C need user-feedback signal to order.
  - **J-other / K-other / M / B.15 item 5** — various follow-ons each deferred per existing rationale.
- **DB-migration trigger (D-2)**: **0 new migrations** anticipated. Framework-only ADR with extension-point integration touches code surfaces only — no schema change. §5.7 trigger (a) FIRED Unit 9.6 carried; trigger (b) cold Phase 35. Persistent moderation-decision audit log deferred Phase 36+ (would fire trigger (b) when shipped).
- **Anticipated unit breakdown** (5 units; mirrors Phase 30 5-unit-foundation-ADR-with-multi-surface-integration shape):
  | Unit | Title | Type |
  |---|---|---|
  | 35.0 | **Phase 35 prep** (this unit) | docs |
  | 35.1 | **ADR-0024 framework-only content moderation** + `lib/moderation/` (types + noop + factory + tests) | code+ADR |
  | 35.2 | Extension-point integration into bio-write + avatar-upload + rating-challenge + subscribe surfaces + per-surface tests + `.env.example` update | code |
  | 35.3 | Phase-35 hygiene + ADR-0016 D-B / ADR-0017 D-H / ADR-0019 D-F / ADR-0021 APPEND notes (framework-only realization) + OPEN_QUESTIONS Q68 expansion status flip + Q68-sibling Q66 / Q70 framework-only-pattern advisory cross-refs | docs |
  | 35.4 | **Phase 35 acceptance gate** | gate |
- **Phase-35-blocking decisions resolved in this prep** (D-1 through D-8): D-1 (Q68 expansion framework-only with no-op default; 5 units; new ADR-0024); D-2 (0 migrations); D-3 (ADR-0024 interface + factory + noop shape); D-4 (four integration surfaces enumerated); D-5 (env var `MODERATION_PROVIDER` default `noop`); D-6 (test surface ~+10-14 tests across +4-5 files); D-7 (Phase 36+ deferrals enumerated); D-8 (parallel-curator collision risk MEDIUM on Unit 35.2 multi-surface wiring).
- **Phase-35-blocking decisions deferred to per-unit implementation** (D-9 through D-13): D-9 (single `ContentModerator` interface vs split text/image: single lean); D-10 (severity field shape: `block | warn` two-state lean); D-11 (per-surface refusal UX: generic lean for Phase 35); D-12 (rating-challenge surface: `rationale:` only lean); D-13 (subscribe-email integration: skip-entirely lean with no-op default).
- **Architectural firsts anticipated in Phase 35**: **first framework-only ADR in project history** (ADR-0024 commits to interface without provider in same ADR; establishes playbook for Q66 / Q70 / future framework-first decisions); **first multi-surface extension-point wiring from a single ADR** (four surfaces wired simultaneously vs accreted-via-copy pattern of `safeAuth()`); **first Q-carryover resolution at 12+ phase age** in project history (Q64 still open at 15+ phases; Q68 closing at 12+ phases = closing longest closeable signal first); **first "demand-signal-first concern dissolved by zero-cost-default framework" pattern** in project history; **twenty-sixth NON-§13 phase**; **first ADR-shipping phase since Phase 33** (alternating no-new-ADR-with-new-ADR pattern: Phase 32 no-new → Phase 33 new → Phase 34 no-new → Phase 35 new); **`lib/moderation/` new directory** = third deferred-provider-pattern dir after `lib/email/` + `lib/cron/` but first with no-op default rather than committed provider; **first 13-env-var milestone** (12 → 13; `MODERATION_PROVIDER` added with `noop` default); **46th+ consecutive 103 kB First Load JS unit** carries forward (pure server-side work; zero client-bundle impact).
- **Open questions newly surfacing** anticipated: 0 new opens. **Q68 expansion status flip from `open` to `resolved` (framework-only with no-op default pinned)** scheduled for Unit 35.3 hygiene with resolution note documenting: pinned framework-only ADR + four surfaces wired; deferred-to-Phase-36+ provider commitment + audit log + appeal + bulk-rescan + policy versioning + severity calibration; sibling-Q cross-refs to Q66 + Q70 pointing to framework-only pattern as proven playbook. Matches Phase-30 Q67 + Phase-15 Q63 + Phase-34 Q79 status-flip-with-deferred-sub-threads precedent.
- **Alternative threads documented** (overridable into Unit 35.1): B. Q78 digest-send analytics; D. Markdown evolution ADR; G. Q64 per-user privacy opt-out; L. Account-deletion blob cleanup; N. v2 methodology authoring; Q79 Profile B / Profile C; J-other. Account-merge UI; K-other. Third+ provider expansion; M. SiteHeader avatar pill; B.15 item 5. Subscribe-endpoint rate limiting.

#### Unit 34.1 — `getSubscriptionsForUser` helper + `formatLastDigestLabel` pure helper + `/profile` "Digest subscriptions" widget + 8 i18n keys per locale + 4 tests (722/63); realizes Q79 Profile A + ADR-0023 D-H first-row deferral; zero client-bundle cost

- Second Phase-34 unit; code unit. Realizes Phase-34 prep D-3 (Q79 Profile A read-only "manage my subscriptions" widget on `/[locale]/profile`; no new ADR). **Test count 718/63 → 722/63** (+4 / +0 file Δ from Unit 34.0; 4 new `formatLastDigestLabel` tests in existing `lib/subscribers/index.test.ts`). **First Load JS shared chunk = 103 kB UNCHANGED** (**45th consecutive unit at 103 kB**; Phase 9-34 invariant preserved); **middleware 160 kB UNCHANGED**; `/profile` page bundle UNCHANGED at 2.31 kB / 117 kB First Load JS (server-rendered widget; zero client-bundle cost). **Dynamic page+API routes 13 UNCHANGED** (no new routes).
- **`lib/subscribers/index.ts`** (modified; +2 exports): adds **`getSubscriptionsForUser(userId): Promise<Subscriber[]>`** DB helper (third in-module DB helper after `getVerifiedSubscribersForDomain` Phase 31 + `createOrRefreshPendingSubscription` Phase 30; SELECTs `WHERE userId = ? AND status = 'verified'` — verified-only filter matches Phase-31 cron-route's posture; multi-row-per-user supported) + **`formatLastDigestLabel(date | null): string`** pure helper (returns "Never" for NULL; ISO date `YYYY-MM-DD` via `.toISOString().slice(0, 10)` for populated timestamps; matches project's existing date-rendering convention from Phase-9 watchlist + Phase-11 challenges + Phase-22+ markdown rationale). Adds `and` import to existing `drizzle-orm` import. Mirrors the Phase-30 pure/DB split pattern carried through Phase 31 + 33.
- **`lib/subscribers/index.test.ts`** (modified; +4 tests + 1 new import): coverage on `formatLastDigestLabel` pure helper — (1) returns "Never" when NULL; (2) returns ISO date when populated; (3) truncates time component to date-only (slice 0,10 — even at 23:59:59.999 boundary); (4) handles Unix epoch as a real date (not NULL — distinguishes "no digest sent" from "digest sent at epoch"). `getSubscriptionsForUser` DB-integration tests remain deferred (Phase-30 B.20 scope; live-DB required); pure helper coverage exercises the user-visible label contract.
- **`app/[locale]/profile/page.tsx`** (modified; +1 import + 1 page-handler block + 1 new `<section>` widget): adds `taxonomy` import from `#site/content` + `getSubscriptionsForUser` + `formatLastDigestLabel` + `parseDomainSubscriptions` imports from `@/lib/subscribers`. Page-handler block calls `getSubscriptionsForUser(userId)` + maps each row to a display-shape `{ id, unsubscribeToken, domainTitlesLabel, lastDigestLabel }` (parses `domainSubscriptions` JSON → joins matching taxonomy titles with comma; formats `lastDigestSentAt` via the new pure helper). Widget `<section>` placed between **Watching** + **Your rating challenges** sections (mirrors existing multi-widget density pattern; visual consistency with the per-section spacing established Phase 10-33). Empty state = "You haven't subscribed to the weekly digest yet" + CTA → `/[locale]/digest` (mirrors Watching section's empty-state pattern). Populated state = `<ul>` with per-row: domain title(s) joined as list + last-digest label (font-mono ISO date) + per-row "Unsubscribe" `<a>` link (href = `/api/v1/subscribe/unsubscribe/{token}`; reuses Phase-30 ADR-0021 D-E unsubscribe-token flow; **no new server action; no JS confirmation; direct href click matches single-click email-link UX**).
- **`messages/en.json` + `messages/fr.json`** (modified; +8 keys per locale extending existing `profile.*` namespace): `subscriptions_heading` ("Digest subscriptions" / "Abonnements au digest"); `subscriptions_aria_label`; `subscriptions_empty_message`; `subscriptions_empty_cta`; `subscriptions_last_digest_label`; `subscriptions_last_digest_never`; `subscriptions_unsubscribe_link`; `subscriptions_unsubscribe_aria_label`. **8 new keys per locale × 2 locales = 16 i18n entries**. **i18n keys per locale 153 → 161** (+8). **First i18n addition since Phase 30** = 4-phase gap (Phase 31 + 32 + 33 = no i18n additions; subscriber-list-email arc's UX surface = Phase 34 is the first user-facing addition since the foundation).
- **§5.7 trigger (b) cold this unit** — Phase 34 reads existing `subscriber` table by `userId` (Phase-33 FK column); no schema change.
- **Class B status** — Phase 34 does NOT touch B catalog directly (closes Q79 Profile A UX-side without B-item movement).
- **Architectural firsts shipped in this unit** (~7 firsts):
  - **First `getSubscriptionsForUser` selector helper** in `lib/subscribers/` (third in-module DB helper; closes the "3 helpers per `lib/<table>/` module" pattern alongside `getVerifiedSubscribersForDomain` + `createOrRefreshPendingSubscription`).
  - **First user-visible consumer of Phase-33 `subscriber.userId` FK** in project history (Phase 33 shipped the column + auto-populate logic; Phase 34 = first user-facing surface that reads from it).
  - **First multi-widget profile page extension since Phase 16** (Phase-15 displayName/bio + Phase-16 imageOverride were the last `/profile` widget additions; Phase 34 adds the Digest subscriptions widget = first `/profile` widget addition in 18 phases).
  - **First i18n addition since Phase 30** = 4-phase gap (Phase 31 + 32 + 33 all skipped i18n changes; Phase 30 added the `email.*` namespace; Phase 34 extends `profile.*` with 8 keys).
  - **First subscriber-list-email feature UX surface** in project history (Phase 30 foundation + Phase 31 scheduler + Phase 32 cleanup + Phase 33 auth-linkage were all infra-side; Phase 34 = first user-visible surface that exercises the feature end-to-end).
  - **Zero client-bundle delta** — server-rendered widget; `/profile` page bundle UNCHANGED at 2.31 kB / 117 kB First Load JS (Phase-33 close baseline preserved).
  - **First `formatLastDigestLabel` ISO-date label formatter** in project history (date-or-Never label pattern; foundation for Phase 35+ relative-time formatters if `next-intl` formatter wiring lands).

- **Widget design (D-3) anticipated** (no ADR; pure UX):
  - **D-A selector** = new `getSubscriptionsForUser(userId): Promise<Subscriber[]>` in `lib/subscribers/` (mirrors Phase-31 `getVerifiedSubscribersForDomain` pattern).
  - **D-B placement** = inside existing `/[locale]/profile` server component between **Watching** + **Your rating challenges** sections.
  - **D-C row fields** = domain title (taxonomy-derived) + last-digest-sent label (ISO date or "Never") + per-row "Unsubscribe" link (reuses Phase-30 unsubscribeToken).
  - **D-D empty state** = "You haven't subscribed to the weekly digest yet" + CTA → `/[locale]/digest`.
  - **D-E status filter** = `status === "verified"` only (pending + unsubscribed hidden; matches Phase-31 cron filter).
  - **D-F multi-row handling** = displays all `userId === session.user.id` rows; multiple anonymous email-only rows from same user (NULL userId) won't appear — matches email-as-primary-identity Phase-30 model.
  - **D-G Phase 35+ deferrals** = Profile B full-edit UX; per-problem subscription UI; cross-domain summary toggle; bulk-unsubscribe button.

Account-deletion blob cleanup** — now also enables the ADR-0023 D-D cascade-on-user-delete to actually fire (Phase 33 ships constraint; Phase 34+ delete flow exercises it); **M. SiteHeader avatar pill**; **N. v2 methodology authoring**; **B.15 item 5. Subscribe-endpoint rate limiting** (~1-2 units; Phase 34+ if spam observed).

#### Unit 33.2 — `subscriber.userId` FK column + migration `0008_subscriber_user_id` + `decideSubscriberUserId` pure helper + subscribe-route `safeAuth()` integration + 6 tests (718/63); realizes ADR-0023 D-A/D-B/D-C/D-E/D-F/D-G

- Third Phase-33 unit; code unit. Realizes ADR-0023 **D-A** (Option A FK extension `subscriber.userId text references users(id) on delete cascade` nullable) + **D-B** (schema column + index posture) + **D-C** (subscribe-route POST handler `safeAuth()` check + auto-populate userId) + **D-E** (re-subscribe semantics: update userId on existing-row refresh) + **D-F** (cross-user re-subscribe: preserve existing userId) + **D-G** (anonymous → authenticated migration: update userId on existing anonymous row). **Test count 712/63 → 718/63** (+6 / +0 file Δ from Unit 33.1; 6 new `decideSubscriberUserId` tests in existing `lib/subscribers/index.test.ts`). **First Load JS shared chunk = 103 kB UNCHANGED** (44th consecutive unit at 103 kB; Phase 9-33 invariant preserved); **middleware 160 kB UNCHANGED**; all page bundles UNCHANGED. **Dynamic page+API routes 13 UNCHANGED** (Phase 33 modifies existing `/api/v1/subscribe` route; no new routes).
- **`lib/db/schema.ts`** (modified): adds `userId` nullable text FK column to `subscribers` table per ADR-0023 D-A/D-B. **DB table count UNCHANGED at 7** (column extension; not new table). Doc-comment block extended documenting the Phase-33 inheritance + "third migration on the `subscriber` table within 4 phases" + "first migration-cluster-resume-after-1-phase-gap" framing.
- **`lib/db/migrations/0008_subscriber_user_id.sql`** (NEW): generated via `pnpm db:generate` (drizzle-kit auto-named `0008_tan_smasher.sql`; renamed to project's meaningful-name convention; `_journal.json` tag updated). Single-line SQL: `ALTER TABLE \`subscriber\` ADD \`userId\` text REFERENCES user(id) ON DELETE CASCADE;`. **`ON DELETE CASCADE` clause manually added** to match the schema-level intent (drizzle-kit's ALTER TABLE migration omits the cascade clause; SQLite supports column-level REFERENCES with ON DELETE actions via ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN syntax; manual edit preserves the ADR-0023 D-D cascade-on-user-delete contract). **Migration count 8 → 9**. **Third migration in 4 phases** (Phase 30 + 31 + 33; Phase 32 gap). **Third migration on the `subscriber` table within 4 phases** (Phase 30 create + 11 columns; Phase 31 `+lastDigestSentAt`; Phase 33 `+userId`) — **most active table-extension cluster in project history**. **First "migration-cluster-resume-after-1-phase-gap"** in project history — mirrors Phase 15-16 cluster shape with post-cluster-pause extension. **First FK column added to a project-owned table** (prior FK columns were created at table-introduction time in `0000_initial_auth` + `0001_watchlist` + `0002_rating_challenges` + `0003_rating_challenge_review`; Phase 33 = first FK column added via ALTER to an already-shipped table).
- **`lib/subscribers/index.ts`** (modified; +1 export + extended existing helper): adds **`decideSubscriberUserId(existingUserId, sessionUserId): string | null`** pure helper realizing ADR-0023 D-E/D-F/D-G decision logic. Three cases:
  - D-G anonymous → authenticated migration: `existingUserId === null && sessionUserId !== null` → return sessionUserId (upgrade).
  - D-F + D-E preserve: `existingUserId !== null` → return existingUserId (conservative; preserves existing across both cross-user case AND anonymous-submit-over-authenticated case).
  - Both NULL: stay anonymous.
  Extends **`createOrRefreshPendingSubscription(email, domains, sessionUserId?)`** signature with new optional `sessionUserId: string | null = null` arg (backward-compatible default; callers that don't pass continue working as anonymous-only). Logic threads `decideSubscriberUserId` through all three state-transition branches per ADR-0023 D-E table: (a) no row → INSERT with `userId = sessionUserId`; (b) existing `verified` + userId-changes → UPDATE only `userId` + `updatedAt` (no status / token churn) per D-G migration; (c) existing `pending_verification` or `unsubscribed` → UPDATE with refreshed tokens + `userId = decideSubscriberUserId(...)`. Mirrors the Phase-30 state-transition shape with userId added throughout.
- **`lib/subscribers/index.test.ts`** (modified; +6 tests): coverage on `decideSubscriberUserId` pure helper — (1) D-G anonymous → authenticated migration; (2) D-E same-user no-op preserve; (3) D-F cross-user conservative preserve; (4) D-E preserve when session NULL (do NOT clear); (5) both NULL → stay NULL; (6) handles non-trivial UUID strings unchanged. **Replaces the deferred B.20 DB-helper-test scope** for the userId decision logic — the pure helper is fully unit-testable without live DB; D-E full state-transition table coverage deferred to a future B.21 if signal warrants (Phase-33 ships the 6-case minimum exercising all 3 decision branches).
- **`app/api/v1/subscribe/route.ts`** (modified; +`safeAuth()` + `auth()` import + `sessionUserId` threading): adds `auth()` import from `@/lib/auth` + per-route `safeAuth()` defensive wrapper (4th in-place copy per ADR-0023 Negative consequences extraction-threshold pattern; extraction deferred until 5+ copies). Subscribe POST handler reads `safeAuth()` → `session?.user?.id ?? null` → passes as third arg to `createOrRefreshPendingSubscription`. **Defensive on `auth()` throw** per ADR-0023 D-G — when DB unavailable or session-read fails, `safeAuth()` returns NULL and the route falls through to Phase-30 anonymous-only path (preserves backward-compatible behavior under failures).
- **`lib/db/migrations/meta/0008_snapshot.json`** (NEW; drizzle-kit-generated companion): bundled in this commit (not Unit 33.3 carry-forward like Unit 31.3 → 31.4 pattern — Unit 33.2 catches this synchronously).
- **§5.7 trigger (b) FIRES Phase 33** per D-A column extension migration. §5.7 trigger (a) FIRED Unit 9.6 carried.
- **Class B item B.15 partial closure progression**: B.15 items 1 + 2 closed Phase 31; item 3 partial close Phase 31; item 4 closed Phase 32; item 5 (subscribe-endpoint rate limiting) carried Phase 34+. Phase 33 does NOT touch B.15 directly (closes Q76 architectural follow-on which is separate from B.15 catalog). **B.15 close-rate stays at 80% within 2 phases of surfacing** (4 of 5 items addressed by Phase 32 close).
- **Architectural firsts shipped in this unit** (~8 firsts):
  - **First FK column added to a project-owned table via ALTER** (prior FK columns were created at table-introduction time).
  - **First manual SQL edit to a drizzle-kit-generated migration** to add `ON DELETE CASCADE` clause (drizzle-kit's ALTER TABLE migration omits the cascade; SQLite ALTER TABLE supports column-level REFERENCES with ON DELETE actions).
  - **Third migration on the `subscriber` table within 4 phases** — most active table-extension cluster in project history.
  - **Third migration in 4 phases** (Phase 30 + 31 + 33; Phase 32 gap) — first "migration-cluster-resume-after-1-phase-gap" in project history.
  - **First `decideSubscriberUserId` 10-case decision-table pure helper** in project history (mirrors Phase-30 pure/DB split pattern; ADR-0023 D-E enumerates 10 explicit state-transition cases; the 3-branch pure helper covers all 10 via composition).
  - **First fourth in-place `safeAuth()` copy** per ADR-0023 Negative consequences extraction-threshold pattern (Phase-7 D-8 established "5+ copies" threshold; current count = 4 after this unit; extraction = Phase 34+ candidate if 5th copy surfaces).
  - **First subscribe-route `auth()` integration** (Phase 30 subscribe route was auth-unaware; Phase 33 reads session for userId-population).
  - **First "complete-feature 4-phase pair" code-side realization** (Phase 30 + 31 + 32 + 33 = subscriber-list-email full delivery arc; Phase 33 = the closing unit).
- Smoke gates (code unit): `pnpm typecheck` clean; `pnpm test` → **718/718 across 63 vitest files** (+6 / +0 file Δ from Unit 33.1); `pnpm build` → ~659 prerendered pages + **13 dynamic page+API routes** UNCHANGED; **First Load JS shared chunk = 103 kB UNCHANGED** (44th consecutive unit at 103 kB; Phase 9-33 invariant preserved); **middleware 160 kB UNCHANGED**; all page bundles UNCHANGED; `pnpm audit-content` → 0 errors / 6 warnings (Q32 baseline unchanged).
- **Files added**: `lib/db/migrations/0008_subscriber_user_id.sql` (NEW; 1 line ALTER); `lib/db/migrations/meta/0008_snapshot.json` (NEW; drizzle-kit-generated companion snapshot).
- **Files modified**: `lib/db/schema.ts` (+1 column `userId` FK + ~20-line doc block); `lib/db/migrations/meta/_journal.json` (+1 entry idx 8); `lib/subscribers/index.ts` (+1 export `decideSubscriberUserId` + extended `createOrRefreshPendingSubscription` signature + 3 state-transition branches threading userId); `lib/subscribers/index.test.ts` (+6 tests + 1 new import); `app/api/v1/subscribe/route.ts` (+`auth()` import + `safeAuth()` defensive wrapper + `sessionUserId` threading to createOrRefreshPendingSubscription); `CHANGELOG.md` (this entry).
- **Parallel-curator awareness**: this unit touches MODIFIED `lib/db/schema.ts` + NEW migration file (sequential `idx` in journal) + MODIFIED `lib/subscribers/index.ts` + `test.ts` (MEDIUM collision risk per Phase-33 prep flagged; LOW absent another schema or subscriber thread) + MODIFIED `app/api/v1/subscribe/route.ts` (LOW collision absent another subscribe-route thread). No parallel-session activity observed during Phase 33 work to date.

#### Unit 33.1 — ADR-0023 per-user-account subscriptions (**23 ADRs**; Q76 Option A FK extension; closes ADR-0021 D-C Phase-31+ anticipation at 2-phase Q-carryover; subscriber-list-email arc completes Phase 30 → 31 → 32 → 33 = 4-phase complete-feature pair; first 2-phase ADR-cycle gap since Phase 19→28)

- Second Phase-33 unit; docs-only. Authors **ADR-0023 per-user-account subscriptions** + ADR README index update. **ADR count 22 → 23**. **ADR-0023 candidate slot claimed at 2-phase age** since ADR-0022 shipped Phase 31 (Phase 32 = no-new-ADR phase). **First 2-phase ADR-cycle gap since Phase 19 → 28 = 9-phase gap** — shorter ADR-cycle-gap than the prior 9-phase Phase 19-28 cycle; confirms 1-phase + 2-phase gaps as the recurring ADR-cycle cadence.
- **Closes ADR-0021 D-C Phase-31+ anticipation explicitly at 2-phase Q-carryover** — D-C clause anticipated per-user-account subscriptions as the first Phase-31+ candidate; Phase 31 shipped scheduler (B.15 item 1 follow-on); Phase 32 shipped cleanup (B.15 item 4 follow-on); Phase 33 closes the architectural D-C anticipation. **First ADR to close an explicit prior ADR's Phase-N+1 anticipation at tight follow-on** in project history (mirrors ADR-0020's pattern of explicit ADR-0012 D-B partial-supersession but for anticipation-vs-supersession). **Subscriber-list-email arc completes Phase 30 → 31 → 32 → 33 = 4-phase complete-feature pair** — first such contiguous-feature arc in project history (prior 4-phase contiguous-feature clusters mixed scope or shipped separate sub-features per phase).
- **Eight D-clauses pinned** (mirrors ADR-0021's + ADR-0022's 8-clause shape verbatim — **third consecutive new-ADR phase to ship 8 D-clauses**; confirms 8-clause shape as the stable new-ADR-phase ADR shape):
  - **D-A model** = **Option A FK extension** `subscriber.userId text references users(id) on delete cascade` nullable (rejects: Option B separate `user_subscriptions` table / Option C stay-anonymous-only-indefinitely / Option D defer-to-Phase-34+). Matches Phase-9 `accounts.userId` + `sessions.userId` + Phase-11 `ratingChallenges.userId` precedent (text type for UUID; cascade-on-delete).
  - **D-B row shape** = nullable `userId` column preserving anonymous email-only path verbatim (NULL = anonymous Phase-30 path; NOT NULL = authenticated Phase-33 path). **No new index** beyond FK; **no `unique` constraint** on `userId` (one user may have multiple subscriptions via different emails).
  - **D-C subscribe-route POST handler** = `auth()` check via defensive `safeAuth()` wrapper + auto-populate `userId = session.user.id` if signed-in else NULL. **No new UI flow + no opt-in toggle + no opt-out + no new i18n keys**; signed-in form submission = implicit consent to linkage.
  - **D-D cascade-on-user-delete** = automatic via FK `ON DELETE cascade`. Phase 33 ships the constraint; future account-deletion flow (Phase 34+ candidate; NOT in Phase 33 scope) benefits automatically. Aligns with §3.1 ADR-0015 privacy posture — deleting a user account also removes subscription metadata.
  - **D-E re-subscribe semantics** = existing row + new signed-in submit updates `userId` to current session user's id (extends Phase-30 D-D step 3 state-transition table with 10 explicit cases enumerated covering all combinations of {none, verified, pending_verification, unsubscribed} existing × {anonymous, signed-in-same-user, signed-in-different-user} new-submit + NULL vs populated existing userId).
  - **D-F cross-user re-subscribe semantics** = **preserve existing `userId`** when existing row has different userId than current session user (conservative posture). `allowDangerousEmailAccountLinking: false` Phase-28 default prevents the edge case at sign-in time; Phase 34+ may revise if user-merge signal emerges.
  - **D-G anonymous → authenticated migration semantics** = update `userId` on existing anonymous row when same-email signed-in submit arrives (smooth upgrade; no row ambiguity). Existing Phase-30 anonymous subscribers naturally become authenticated subscribers on first signed-in re-submit.
  - **D-H Phase 34+ deferrals** enumerated (~9 items): "manage my subscriptions" page on `/[locale]/profile` (Q79 candidate); curator analytics dashboard; per-problem subscriptions extension; subscription-preference editing UX; cross-domain aggregated weekly summary; subscriber-list export tooling; user-merge UI (revisits D-F); account-deletion flow (uses the cascade); per-user-account-subscription audit trail; soft-delete vs hard-delete asymmetry (intentional Phase 33).
- **Per-unit deferred decisions documented in Phase-33 prep D-9/10/11 carried forward** to Unit 33.2 (Drizzle FK syntax matches Phase-9 precedent; subscribe-route auth() integration shape; test coverage shape for FK behavior).
- **§5.7 trigger (b) FIRES Phase 33** via D-A column extension migration (anticipated `0008_subscriber_user_id.sql` Unit 33.2). **Third migration in 4 phases** (Phase 30 + 31 + 33; Phase 32 gap = first "migration-cluster-resume-after-1-phase-gap" in project history).
- **Q76 resolution path documented**: ADR-0023 D-A pins Option A; Option B (separate `user_subscriptions` table) + Option C (anonymous-only-indefinitely) become rejected alternatives. **Q76 status flip to `resolved` scheduled for Unit 33.3 hygiene** (mirrors Phase-30 Q67 status flip via ADR-0017 + Phase-15 Q63 status flip via ADR-0016 precedent).
- **Phase-33 architectural firsts pinned by this ADR** (~12 firsts):
  - **First ADR to close an explicit prior ADR's Phase-N+1 anticipation at tight follow-on** (ADR-0021 D-C → ADR-0023 at 3-phase elapsed; closes Q76 explicit anticipation; mirrors ADR-0020 D-B partial-supersession pattern but for anticipation-vs-supersession).
  - **First "complete-feature 4-phase pair" in project history** (Phase 30 + 31 + 32 + 33 = subscriber-list-email full delivery arc: foundation + scheduler + cleanup + authenticated-linkage).
  - **First 2-phase ADR-cycle gap since Phase 19 → 28 = 9-phase gap** (shorter than Phase 19→28 cycle; confirms 1-phase + 2-phase gaps as recurring ADR-cycle cadence).
  - **ADR-0023 candidate slot claimed at 2-phase age** (opened Phase 31; Phase 32 = no-new-ADR phase; Phase 33 = claim).
  - **Third consecutive new-ADR phase to ship 8 D-clauses** (Phase 30 + 31 + 33 all 8 D-clauses; **confirms 8-clause shape as stable new-ADR-phase ADR shape**).
  - **First non-zero-migration phase 3 of 4 phases anticipated** (Phase 30 + 31 + 33; Phase 32 gap).
  - **Third migration on the `subscriber` table within 4 phases anticipated** (Phase 30 = create + 11 columns; Phase 31 = `+lastDigestSentAt`; Phase 33 = `+userId` FK) — **most active table-extension cluster in project history**.
  - **First "migration-cluster-resume-after-1-phase-gap"** in project history (Phase 30 + 31 cluster + Phase 32 gap + Phase 33 resume; mirrors Phase 15-16 cluster shape but extends with the post-cluster-pause pattern).
  - **First ADR-0023 to extend an existing module rather than introduce one** (ADR-0017/0018/0019/0021 all introduced new `lib/` modules; ADR-0020 extended `lib/auth/`; ADR-0023 extends `lib/subscribers/` + `app/api/v1/subscribe/` only — preserves the existing-module-extension precedent ADR-0020 established).
  - **First 10-case state-transition table in an ADR D-clause** (D-E enumerates {none, verified, pending_verification, unsubscribed} × {anonymous, signed-in-same-user, signed-in-different-user} × {NULL existing userId, populated existing userId} = 10 explicit combinations; more comprehensive than Phase-30 ADR-0021 D-D's 4-case re-subscribe state-transition).
  - **First ADR-0023 to anticipate the 4th `safeAuth()` in-place copy** without extracting (per Negative consequences) — extraction-threshold pattern preserved (Phase-7 D-8 established "5+ copies"; current count = 4 after Unit 33.2 ships).
  - **First "anonymous → authenticated migration" semantics** documented in an ADR (D-G); foundation for Phase-34+ user-merge UI if signal emerges (D-F conservative posture).
- **Reuses Phase 9 + 28 + 30 surfaces verbatim**: `accounts.userId` cascade-FK precedent (Drizzle syntax); `safeAuth()` defensive pattern (3 in-place copies Phase 9-30 + 4th Phase 33); `users.id` provider-agnostic primary key (works for both GitHub + Google sign-ins); `createOrRefreshPendingSubscription` from Phase-30 (signature extended with `userId` param in Unit 33.2); `auth()` from Phase-9 `lib/auth/` (reused for session lookup). **Zero new runtime dependencies + zero new operational gates + zero new env vars + zero new i18n keys** anticipated.
- **Reversibility commitments documented**: dropping the FK column Phase 34+ would require a migration but no application-code rip-up (the `userId`-population is one-line `if (session) { userId = session.user.id }` in subscribe route). **Provider-agnostic** — `users.id` works identically for GitHub (Phase 9) and Google (Phase 28) sign-ins; Phase 33 works for both.
- **Known limitations documented in ADR** (5 items): (1) **Third migration in 4 phases** — establishes "migration-cluster-resume-after-1-phase-gap" as a new shape (mirrors Phase 15-16 cluster shape with post-cluster-pause extension); (2) **Cross-user re-subscribe collision theoretically possible** but conservative-preserve posture per D-F; `allowDangerousEmailAccountLinking: false` Phase-28 default prevents the edge case at sign-in time; (3) **Auto-link without opt-in toggle** removes user control over linkage — implicit consent from form submission; (4) **No `unique` constraint on `userId`** allows one user to have multiple subscriptions via different emails — matches email-as-primary-identity model; (5) **`safeAuth()` 4th in-place copy** (not yet extracted to shared helper; deferred until 5+ in-place copies per Phase-7 D-8 extraction-threshold pattern).
- Smoke gates (docs-only): `pnpm audit-content` → 0 errors / 6 warnings (Q32 baseline since Phase 2; unchanged through every Phase 3-32 unit); typecheck / test / build untouched since no source files modified.

- **DB-migration trigger (D-2)**: **1 new migration** anticipated under D-1 recommendation (`0008_subscriber_user_id.sql`). **Third migration in 4 phases** (Phase 30 + 31 + 33; Phase 32 gap). **First "migration-cluster-resume-after-1-phase-gap"** in project history (Phase 30 + 31 = 2-phase migration cluster; Phase 32 = 0-migration gap; Phase 33 = resume = NEW shape; mirrors Phase 15-16 cluster shape but extends with the post-cluster-pause pattern). **Third migration on the `subscriber` table within 4 phases** (Phase 30 = create + 11 columns; Phase 31 = +`lastDigestSentAt`; Phase 33 = +`userId` FK) — **most active table-extension cluster in project history**. §5.7 trigger (a) FIRED Unit 9.6 carried; trigger (b) **FIRES Phase 33** per D-10 column extension.
- **ADR-0023 D-clauses anticipated** (D-3 through D-8): D-A per-user-account model = Option A FK extension verbatim per Q76 documentation (rejects: Option B separate table / Option C anonymous-only-indefinitely); D-B subscriber row shape = `subscriber.userId text references users(id) on delete cascade` nullable (preserves anonymous email-only path); D-C subscribe-route POST handler = `auth()` check + populate `userId = session.user.id` if signed-in else NULL (no opt-in toggle; auto-link); D-D cascade-on-user-delete = automatic via FK constraint (future account-deletion flow Phase 34+ benefits automatically); D-E re-subscribe semantics = update existing row's `userId` to current session user's id on re-submit (preserves Phase-30 D-D step 3 case-(d) state-transition with userId update); D-F cross-user re-subscribe = preserves existing `userId` if non-NULL different from current session (conservative; Phase 34+ may revise if user-merge signal); D-G anonymous → authenticated migration = update `userId = session.user.id` on existing anonymous row with same email (smooth upgrade); D-H Phase 34+ deferrals ("manage my subscriptions" page Q79 candidate / curator analytics dashboard / per-problem subscriptions / subscription-preference editing UX).

#### Unit 32.1 — `lib/cron/cleanup-stale-tokens.ts` + `app/api/v1/cron/cleanup-stale-tokens/route.ts` + `vercel.json` second cron entry + 5 tests (712/63); validates Phase-31 cron infrastructure reusability claim; no new ADR

- Second Phase-32 unit; code unit. Realizes Phase-32 prep D-3 (direct ADR-0022 D-A/D-D/D-F instantiation; no new ADR). **Test count 707/62 → 712/63** (+5 / +1 file Δ from Unit 32.0; 5 new `runCleanupStaleTokens` tests). **First Load JS shared chunk = 103 kB UNCHANGED** (43rd consecutive unit at 103 kB; Phase 9-32 invariant preserved); **middleware 160 kB UNCHANGED**; all page bundles UNCHANGED. **Dynamic page+API routes 12 → 13** (+1: `/api/v1/cron/cleanup-stale-tokens` at 187 B / 103 kB First Load JS).
- **`lib/cron/cleanup-stale-tokens.ts`** (NEW; ~70 lines): orchestrator with injectable `CleanupStaleTokensDeps` shape (`deleteStalePendingTokens` + `logError` + `now`). **Mirrors `runDigestSend` injectable-deps pattern** Phase 31 extracted exactly for this multi-cron reuse case — confirms the pattern scales additively. Single-call DELETE (atomic at SQLite-driver level; per-row try/catch unnecessary since DELETE is single-statement-atomic — matches ADR-0022 D-F's per-row-isolation-scope reasoning: digest-send needs per-row isolation because each row triggers an external Resend API call; cleanup is pure DB-side so single-statement atomicity suffices). Catches DB-level failures via outer try/catch; failed DELETE returns 0 + logs; next week's cron retries naturally (matches digest-send's "no in-week retry" posture per ADR-0022 D-F). Returns `CleanupStaleTokensResult { deletedCount, durationMs }` for Vercel dashboard observability.
- **`lib/cron/cleanup-stale-tokens.test.ts`** (NEW; 5 tests): coverage on (1) `deletedCount === 0` when nothing matches; (2) returns count returned by `deleteStalePendingTokens`; (3) passes current time as cutoff (deterministic time injection); (4) isolates thrown DELETE failure + returns 0 + logs `cleanup-delete-failed` event; (5) non-negative `durationMs`. **Mirrors `digest-send.test.ts` injectable-deps testing style** — all deps are `vi.fn()` mocks; production code path (DELETE statement) exercised end-to-end without touching SQLite. Replaces the original B.20 DB-helper-deferred scope for the cleanup-cron path (matches Phase-31 Unit 31.3 dep-injection coverage precedent).
- **`app/api/v1/cron/cleanup-stale-tokens/route.ts`** (NEW; ~70 lines): thin POST handler per Phase-32 prep D-3. **Second `app/api/v1/cron/` route prefix** in project history. **Second scheduled-trigger API endpoint** in project history. All logic delegated to `lib/cron/cleanup-stale-tokens`; route's job is (a) auth-check via **reused `checkCronAuth`** from `lib/cron/digest-send.ts` (single shared `CRON_SECRET`; **first reuse of the Phase-31 extracted helper** per Phase-32 prep D-5 — validates the multi-cron auth reusability claim documented in ADR-0022 D-D); (b) adapt Drizzle `db.delete(subscribers).where(and(eq(status, "pending_verification"), lt(verificationTokenExpiresAt, cutoff))).returning({ id })` into `CleanupStaleTokensDeps.deleteStalePendingTokens` (returns affected row count via `.length`); (c) return `CleanupStaleTokensResult` JSON for Vercel dashboard observability. **POST only** (Vercel Cron's HTTP method default); GET returns 405. `logError` uses `console.error` with `[cleanup-stale-tokens]` prefix (Vercel dashboard captures stderr; matches `digest-send` route's structured-log shape; one ESLint disable comment for `no-console`).
- **`vercel.json`** (modified): appends **second `crons` entry** per Phase-32 prep D-13. **Second `crons` entry in `vercel.json` in project history**. New entry: `{ path: "/api/v1/cron/cleanup-stale-tokens", schedule: "0 0 * * 2" }` (Tuesday 00:00 UTC weekly — offset from Monday digest-send per Phase-32 prep D-9 lean for "post-digest-send cleanup" framing). Standard cron expression `0 0 * * 2` = "at minute 0 of hour 0 on Tuesday." Top-level `crons` array now has 2 entries; future cron jobs (Phase 33+ retry queue Q78 Profile C, etc.) extend the array.
- **§5.7 trigger (b) cold this unit** — Unit 32.1 ships pure DELETE statement against existing schema; **no schema changes**; **no migrations**. **Returns to 0-migration pattern** after the 2-phase Phase-30 + 31 cluster (mirrors Phase 17-29's 13-phase 0-migration streak shape — short clusters bracketed by long 0-migration runs is the recurring rhythm). Migration count UNCHANGED at 8.
- **Class B item B.15 item 4 (stale verification-token cleanup job) — CLOSED at Unit 32.1** at **2-phase carryover** (Phase 30 → 31 → 32). **B.15 item count 4 → 3**.
- **Single-shared `CRON_SECRET` reuse realized**: both `/api/v1/cron/digest-send` (Phase 31) and `/api/v1/cron/cleanup-stale-tokens` (Phase 32) authenticate via the same `CRON_SECRET` env var via the same `checkCronAuth` pure-function helper. **Validates Phase-31 design intent**: ADR-0022 D-D documented "single shared secret across both cron endpoints" as anticipated; Phase 32 = realization. **Operational gate count UNCHANGED at 6** (Q77 covers both endpoints; no Q-promotion this unit). **Env var count UNCHANGED at 12** (no new env var; reuses `CRON_SECRET`).
- **Architectural firsts shipped in this unit** (~6 firsts):
  - **First reuse of Phase-31 `checkCronAuth` helper** — validates extraction-for-reuse design intent.
  - **First reuse of Phase-31 `runDigestSend` injectable-deps orchestrator shape** — `runCleanupStaleTokens` mirrors structure; confirms pattern scales additively.

  - **Second `lib/cron/` source file** in project history (Phase-31 `digest-send.ts` → Phase-32 `cleanup-stale-tokens.ts`).

Closes **Phase-30 B.15 item 4** at **2-phase carryover** (carried Phase 30 → 31 → 32). **First NO-NEW-ADR phase since Phase 29** = **2-phase new-ADR streak break** (Phase 30 + 31 = 2 consecutive new-ADR phases); also breaks the **4-phase new-ADR cluster** Phase 19 + 28 + 30 + 31. **Validates the Phase-31 cron infrastructure reusability claim** documented in ADR-0022 Negative consequences — `checkCronAuth` + `runDigestSend`-shaped orchestrator pattern + `vercel.json` `crons` array all designed to scale additively; Phase 32 = first reuse + extension. Anticipated phase shape: **4 units** (mirrors Phase 27 + 29 no-new-ADR phase shape; first 4-unit phase since Phase 29 = 3-phase gap; confirms 4-unit shape stability for no-new-ADR phases).
- **Tradeoffs flagged**: passing on three larger architectural threads, each deferred for demand-signal-first reasoning rather than scope-cap reasoning:
  - **B. Q78 architectural digest-send analytics + observability** (tightest 1-phase Q-carryover; ADR-0023 candidate; Phase-31 Profile-A lean) — **explicitly documented as "waits for first-week production cron signal to inform Profile A vs B vs C decision."** Q77 (`CRON_SECRET` provisioning) is still pending operationally; no production cron signal yet. Phase 33+ once Q77 operational.
  - **A. Q68 expansion content moderation** (10+ phase carryover; strongest non-just-surfaced patience signal; ADR-0023 candidate) — premature without user-base signal; multiple existing surfaces (bio + image + markdown rationale) but zero observed abuse signals. Phase 33+ if user-base + abuse signals accumulate.
  - **C. Q76 architectural per-user-account subscriptions** (1-phase Q-carryover; closes ADR-0021 D-C boundary) — **third consecutive migration phase would be unprecedented** (Phase 30 + 31 = 2-phase cluster; mirrors Phase-15-16 series shape; extending to 3 phases breaks the cluster-shape precedent). No demand signal (Phase-30 subscriber flow not yet operational since Q75 unresolved). Phase 33+ if signed-in users try to manage subscriptions.
  - Plus **D / G / J-other / K-other / L / M / N / B.15 item 5** all enumerated as Phase 33+ candidates.
- **Reuses Phase-31 cron infrastructure verbatim**: ADR-0022 (no new ADR — direct D-A/D-D/D-F instantiation); `lib/cron/digest-send.ts`-shaped orchestrator pattern (`runDigestSend` injectable-deps shape extends to `runCleanupStaleTokens`); `checkCronAuth` pure-function helper (single shared `CRON_SECRET`; both cron endpoints behind same belt-and-suspenders gate); `vercel.json` `crons` array (single config file; second entry appends); `app/api/v1/cron/` route prefix (Phase 32 = second route at this prefix). **Zero new runtime dependencies + zero new operational gates + zero new env vars + zero new ADRs** anticipated.
- **DB-migration trigger (D-2)**: **0 new migrations** anticipated under D-1 recommendation. **Returns to 0-migration pattern** after the 2-phase Phase-30 + 31 cluster (mirrors Phase 17-29's 13-phase 0-migration streak shape — short clusters bracketed by long 0-migration runs is the recurring rhythm). §5.7 trigger (a) FIRED Unit 9.6 carried; trigger (b) cold Phase 32.
- **Cleanup-cron design (D-3) anticipated** (no ADR; direct ADR-0022 D-A/D-D/D-F instantiation):
  - **D-A logic** = `DELETE FROM subscriber WHERE status = 'pending_verification' AND verificationTokenExpiresAt < now()`. Removes only stale `pending_verification` rows; preserves `verified` + `unsubscribed`. Side-effect-free aside from DELETE.
  - **D-B schedule** = TBD per Unit 32.1 (lean: **WEEKLY Tuesday 00:00 UTC** for offset from Monday digest send; alternative DAILY for bounded ~24-hour accumulation; lean WEEKLY for foundation phase given 24-hour token TTL bounds maximum accumulation at ~7 days × abandonment-rate).
  - **D-C auth** = `CRON_SECRET` bearer-token + `vercel-cron` header (same as digest-send cron per ADR-0022 D-D; single shared secret across both cron endpoints).
  - **D-D failure handling** = single-row DELETE batch; atomic so per-row try/catch unnecessary; route returns `{ deletedCount, durationMs }` for Vercel dashboard observability.
  - **D-E idempotency** = inherent — `DELETE WHERE` is naturally idempotent (re-run on same rows is a no-op).
  - **D-F Resend dependency** = NONE (no email sends; pure DB cleanup).
