#### Unit 28.2 — Google provider + per-provider sign-in UI + env vars + i18n + 11 new tests (616/56)

- Third Phase-28 unit; code unit. Realizes ADR-0020 D-A through D-F via in-place edits to `lib/auth/index.ts` + `components/auth-control/index.tsx` + `.env.example` + 2 i18n locale files + 2 new files in `lib/auth/`. **Test count 605/55 → 616/56** (+11 tests / +1 file Δ from Phase 27 close); **First Load JS 103 kB UNCHANGED** (37th consecutive unit at 103 kB; end-to-end through every Phase 9-28 unit); **middleware 160 kB UNCHANGED** since Phase 12.
- **`lib/auth/link-account.ts`** (new; 73 lines): pure helpers backing the NextAuth `linkAccount` event. Exports `PROVIDER_IDS = ["github", "google"] as const` + `type ProviderId` + `extractGithubLogin(account, profile, user)` pure function encoding ADR-0020 D-D's GitHub-only curator-of-record gate. Factored out for unit testability — `lib/auth/index.ts` instantiates NextAuth at module load (DB adapter initialization requires connectivity); pure helpers test in isolation without DB / process dependencies. Mirrors Phase-22+23 `scaffold.ts` pure-function-extraction precedent verbatim.
- **`lib/auth/link-account.test.ts`** (new; 11 tests across 2 describe blocks): 3 `PROVIDER_IDS` tests (length === 2; ordering [github, google]; ProviderId union assignability) — regression guards against accidental third-provider drift without ADR amendment per ADR-0020 D-B + ordering reversal breaking sign-in UI per ADR-0020 D-F; 8 `extractGithubLogin` tests covering: github+valid+user.id returns login (happy path); google account returns null (critical ADR-0020 D-D regression guard — Google sign-in MUST NOT populate githubLogin); unknown future provider (gitlab) returns null (defensive for Phase-29+ extensibility); profile.login missing/non-string/null/non-object/primitive all return null (Auth.js v5 type narrowing defenses); user.id undefined returns null.
- **`lib/auth/index.ts`** (modified; 76 lines was 65): imports `Google from "next-auth/providers/google"` + `extractGithubLogin, PROVIDER_IDS` from `./link-account`; `providers: [GitHub, Google]` (was `[GitHub]`); `events.linkAccount` reduced from 7-line inline to 3-line delegation to `extractGithubLogin`; docstring updated to cross-reference ADR-0020 D-A/B/D/E + Q73 operational gate parallel to Q54; preserves all other Phase-9 behavior verbatim (DB-backed sessions per ADR-0012 D-C; trustHost; DrizzleAdapter config).
- **`components/auth-control/index.tsx`** (modified; 122 lines was 95): signed-out branch replaced single-form-single-button with `<div role="group">` wrapping `PROVIDER_IDS.map(...)` → one `<ProviderSignInForm>` per provider; new internal `ProviderSignInForm` component wraps `signIn(provider, { redirectTo: "/" })` server-action verbatim per ADR-0012 D-D + ADR-0020 D-F redirect-to-provider flow; new internal `providerLabel(t, provider)` switch returns the per-provider i18n key (`sign_in_with_github` / `sign_in_with_google`); layout = `flex-col gap-1.5 sm:flex-row sm:items-center sm:gap-2` (stacked mobile + side-by-side `sm:` breakpoint per ADR-0020 D-F); button styling preserved verbatim; signed-in branch UNCHANGED Phase 28 (sign-out is provider-agnostic); docstring updated to cross-reference ADR-0020 D-F + Q73 graceful degradation mirroring Q54.
- **`.env.example`** (modified): adds Phase-28 Auth multi-provider block immediately before the Phase-9 Database block: `AUTH_GOOGLE_ID` + `AUTH_GOOGLE_SECRET` with Q73 operational gate comment + production/local-dev Google Cloud Console registration guidance + graceful-degradation note. **Env var count 7 → 9** (first env-var-count change since Phase 16 Q69 `BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN`).
- **`messages/en.json` + `messages/fr.json`** (modified): adds `auth.sign_in_with_github` + `auth.sign_in_with_google` keys per locale. EN values: "Sign in with GitHub" / "Sign in with Google". FR values: "Se connecter avec GitHub" / "Se connecter avec Google". Existing `auth.sign_in` key preserved (used as `<div role="group" aria-label={...}>` value). **i18n keys per locale 140 → 142** (+2 × 2 locales = +4 total).
- **Provider-array ordering** (per Phase-28 prep D-16): GitHub first, Google second. Preserves Phase-9 user expectation; the sign-in UI button order in `<AuthControl>` iterates `PROVIDER_IDS` directly, so the constant-ordering = button-ordering invariant is enforced by the data structure.
- **Schema unchanged** per Phase-28 prep D-2. `accounts` table already supports multi-provider per Auth.js v5 canonical adapter (one row per `(provider, providerAccountId)`); `users.githubLogin` stays nullable; new Google sign-ins leave `githubLogin` NULL. **Migration count stays at 6**; **twelfth consecutive 0-migration phase** maintained.
- **DB schema verification**: confirmed `accounts` Drizzle table has `(provider, providerAccountId)` composite primary key (canonical Auth.js v5 shape); Google sign-ins will INSERT into this table on first authentication; existing GitHub-only users' `accounts` rows untouched.
- **Bundle invariant verification**: `pnpm build` output confirms First Load JS shared chunk = **103 kB UNCHANGED**; middleware bundle = **160 kB UNCHANGED** since Phase 12; all 8 dynamic page+API routes preserved (no new routes; the Google sign-in flow reuses the existing `/api/auth/[...nextauth]` dynamic route); `/profile` = 117 kB UNCHANGED; `/u/{handle}/*` routes = 108 kB UNCHANGED.
- **Auth.js v5 default `allowDangerousEmailAccountLinking: false`** preserved per ADR-0020 D-E. No explicit config needed (Auth.js v5 defaults to false per their security guidance). Same-email-different-provider yields two separate `users.id` rows; account-merge UI deferred to Phase 29+.
- **Q54 + Q73 graceful degradation**: when either provider's env vars are unset, the GitHub provider treats clientId/clientSecret as undefined; Auth.js surfaces an OAuth-configuration error page on click for the affected provider. The OTHER provider's button still works if its env is set (independent failure modes). SiteHeader's `safeAuth()` catches DB-read failures and renders the signed-out branch with both buttons regardless (degraded but functional).
- **Smoke gates** (all green at this commit pre-pre-commit-hook):
  - `pnpm typecheck` → clean (no new type errors; `ProviderId` union + `extractGithubLogin` signature land cleanly).
  - `pnpm lint` → clean (eslint passes; prettier formatting preserved).
  - `pnpm test` → **616/616 across 56 vitest files** (+11 / +1 file from Phase 27 close 605/55).
  - `pnpm build` → ~659 prerendered pages + 8 dynamic page+API routes UNCHANGED; First Load JS 103 kB UNCHANGED; middleware 160 kB UNCHANGED.
  - `pnpm audit-content` → 0 errors / 6 warnings (Q32 baseline since Phase 2; unchanged through every Phase 3-27 unit).
- **Architectural firsts shipped in this unit**:
  - **Second OAuth provider configured** in `lib/auth/index.ts` since Phase 9 GitHub-only (closes Phase-9 Class B item 8 = 17-phase carryover).
  - **First pure-function extraction from `lib/auth/index.ts`** (Phase-22+23 `scaffold.ts` precedent applied to a Phase-9 surface; first refactor of `lib/auth/index.ts` since Phase 9 Unit 9.6 `linkAccount` landing).
  - **First multi-button signed-out UI** in `<AuthControl>` (Phase-9 single-button shape preserved through 18 NON-§13 phases).
  - **First env-var-count change since Phase 16** (Q69 BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN; 12 phases ago). Env count 7 → 9.
  - **First i18n-key addition to the `auth` namespace** since Phase-9 Unit 9.5 (16 phases; `auth.sign_in` / `auth.sign_out` / `auth.signed_in_fallback` were the only keys; Phase 28 adds 2 sibling keys).
  - **First time `lib/auth/` exports a non-runtime helper** (`link-account.ts` joins `curator.ts` as a pure-function sibling — Phase 28 first; Phase 12 `curator.ts` was the prior precedent).
  - **First test file added to `lib/auth/`** since Phase-12 `curator.test.ts` (16 phases gap; first new auth-test file in 16 phases).
- **Parallel-curator awareness**: this unit edits Phase-9 + Phase-15 long-untouched surfaces; LOW collision risk against any parallel session that picks a non-auth thread. Working tree clean at session start.

#### Unit 28.1 — ADR-0020 multi-provider OAuth (Google as second provider; lifts ADR-0012 D-B; **20 ADRs**)

- Second Phase-28 unit; docs-only. Authors `docs/adr/0020-multi-provider-oauth.md` pinning the multi-provider OAuth architectural commitment before code lands in Unit 28.2 (per MASTER_PROMPT.md §14.4 + ADR-0012 / ADR-0017 / ADR-0018 / ADR-0019 precedent: "pin the choice with Pros/Cons before code lands"). **First new ADR since Phase 19** (ADR-0019; Phase-19 EXIF stripping); **ADR-0020 candidate slot closes at 14 phases unclaimed**. **Eight-phase no-new-ADR streak ends** at this unit (Phase 18 + 20-27 = 8 phases; Phase 28 = new-ADR phase).
- **ADR-0020 = partial supersession of ADR-0012 D-B.** Status line "Supersedes (partial): ADR-0012 D-B (single-provider restriction lifted; ADR-0012's other four D-clauses preserved)". **First time an ADR D-clause is superseded by a follow-on ADR** (prior pattern: Phase-19+24+27 amended own-D-clause via implementation notes). Establishes the convention for future inter-ADR supersession-without-full-replacement (vs ADR-0005 immutability for code-shipping ADRs; this ADR + ADR-0012 stand side-by-side with explicit cross-references).
- **Six D-clauses pinned**:
  - **D-A** multi-provider runtime contract — `next-auth/providers/*` adapters allowed; other auth runtimes (Clerk SDK, Lucia, Iron Session) remain forbidden per ADR-0012 D-A; widens *providers* surface not *runtime* surface.
  - **D-B** second provider = Google OAuth via `next-auth/providers/google`; provider count exactly 2 at Phase 28 close (GitHub first, Google second); third+ requires follow-on ADR amendment or new ADR.
  - **D-C** env-var convention — Auth.js v5 canonical naming (`AUTH_GOOGLE_ID` + `AUTH_GOOGLE_SECRET`); env count 7 → 9; graceful degradation when unset (mirrors Q54 fallback).
  - **D-D** curator-of-record gate remains GitHub-only — `editorial.primary_curator` joins to `users.githubLogin` which is populated only via GitHub `linkAccount` event; Phase-9 Unit 9.6 verbatim preserved; non-GitHub users CAN submit + edit profile + view but CANNOT be curator-of-record / review challenges / appear in curator UI; **Q74** surfaced as Phase 29+ architectural candidate (non-GitHub-users-as-curators).
  - **D-E** account-linking strategy = Auth.js default `allowDangerousEmailAccountLinking: false`; same-email-different-provider → two separate `users.id` rows; Phase 29+ account-merge UI candidate if curator confusion surfaces.
  - **D-F** sign-in UX = per-provider buttons stacked mobile + `sm:` side-by-side; no chooser modal; no dropdown; mirrors ADR-0012 D-D redirect-to-provider pattern verbatim per provider; chooser-modal pattern Phase 30+ if 3+ providers.
- **Considered options table** lists 5 alternatives with explicit Pros/Cons: Option 1 NextAuth.js Google provider (chosen); Option 2 GitLab (smaller user base; Phase 29+ if curator demand); Option 3 Email-link (SMTP infrastructure; multi-phase scope; Phase 29+); Option 4 WebAuthn/passkeys (browser support uneven; UX still maturing; Phase 30+); Option 5 defer multi-provider expansion (continue with thread A/I/K-other; rejected — patience signal compounds; ADR-0020 slot stays unclaimed for 15th phase).
- **Decision drivers documented**: §5.8 auth recommendation (preserves GitHub OAuth "strongly preferred for community signal" — GitHub stays first provider); ADR-0012 D-A runtime singleton (Phase 28 stays inside this constraint); ADR-0012 D-B single-provider restriction (the surface this ADR lifts); ADR-0012 D-E editorial-identity model (GitHub-login-keyed; Phase 28 preserves); ADR-0013 Drizzle + Auth.js adapter (`accounts` table already supports multi-provider; zero migration work); reversibility (`lib/auth/` abstracts runtime; removing Google is one-line edit); Phase-9 "pre-emptive for non-existent user base" objection has 7+ phases of receipts (deferral cost compounds linearly).
- **Pros and Cons documented**: 7 Positive consequences (broadens user base; closes 17-phase patience signal; reuses Auth.js v5 idioms; zero schema migration; reversibility preserved; account-linking conservatism; per-provider UX clarity); 6 Negative consequences (Q73 operational gate; two `users.id` rows for same-email cross-provider users; non-GitHub users blocked from curator-of-record; env-var count grows 7 → 9; sign-in UI vertical space; configuration-as-code grows linearly with providers).
- **Cross-references**: ADR-0012 (supersedes D-B); ADR-0005 (rating-action immutability + curator-of-record); ADR-0013 (Auth.js adapter `accounts` table multi-provider support); Q54 + Q73 + Q74 (operational + architectural follow-ons); §5.8 + §13 + §14.4 (MASTER_PROMPT.md references).
- **ADR README index** updated: `docs/adr/README.md` adds the ADR-0020 row + appends ADR-0020 description to the chronological prose paragraph. "The next ADR will be numbered 0020." → "The next ADR will be numbered 0021." Mirrors Phase-19's ADR-0019 description shape.
- Smoke gates: `pnpm audit-content` → 0 errors / 6 warnings (Q32 baseline since Phase 2; unchanged through every Phase 3-27 unit); typecheck / test / build untouched since no source files modified.
- **Files added/modified**: `docs/adr/0020-multi-provider-oauth.md` (new; ~240 lines); `docs/adr/README.md` (1-row addition + appended description sentence).
- No new tests (docs-only). Anticipated test count delta: **+0** at Unit 28.1 close; Unit 28.2 lands +4 to +8 tests.
- No code surface changes (docs-only). All anticipated code lands in Unit 28.2 (`lib/auth/index.ts` + `components/auth-control/index.tsx` + `.env.example` + 2 i18n locale files + possibly new test files).

#### Unit 28.0 — Phase 28 prep (multi-provider OAuth via ADR-0020 candidate; Phase-9 Class B item 8 = 17-phase carryover; twenty-third "Continue" override)

- First Phase-28 unit; docs-only. Opens Phase 28 (**nineteenth NON-§13 phase**). Mirrors Phase-12 through Phase-27 phase-prep patterns. **§13 ledger CLOSED** at Unit 9.9 (carried unchanged through Phases 10-27); Phase 28 inferred-not-§13 like its eighteen predecessors.
- **Phase 28 sign-off granted via "Continue" override** in the unit-rhythm rhythm (**twenty-third invocation** of this pattern). §12 cardinal rule satisfied; first-thread D-1 recommendation overridable into Unit 28.1.
- **Recommended thread: H. Multi-provider OAuth expansion** (D-1). Lifts ADR-0012 D-B's "GitHub OAuth only" restriction via new **ADR-0020** that documents the multi-provider architectural commitment. Closes **Phase-9 Class B item 8** (carried **17 phases** since Phase 9 closed) — the **strongest non-multi-phase architectural patience signal** in the project. ADR-0020 slot has been open **14 phases** since ADR-0019 shipped Phase 19. **First new ADR since Phase 19**; **eight-phase no-new-ADR streak ends** (Phase 18 + 20-27 = 8 phases). Anticipated phase shape: **6 units** (mirrors Phase 19's new-ADR shape; **breaks the 8-phase 5-unit streak** Phase 20-27).
- **Tradeoffs flagged**: passing on **I. Subscriber-list email** (Phase-5 D-4 punt carried **21+ phases** — strongest overall patience signal; multi-phase scope better as 2-3 phase rollout: foundation → templates → scheduler; not a clean Phase-28 single-phase candidate) + **A. Q68 expansion content moderation** (~3-5 units; ADR-0020 candidate but defers OAuth patience signal further) + **K-other operational tail bundled** (low value-per-unit; Phase-9 item 8 has waited longer). Also passing on **B. Cropping UI** (~30 kB client dep regression risk), **C-other AVIF** (no observed bandwidth signal), **D. Q72 markdown extensions** (no curator demand Phase 18-27), **G. Q64 privacy opt-out** (4th ALTER migration breaks 11-consecutive 0-migration streak), **L. Account-deletion blob cleanup** (conditional on account-delete path), **M. SiteHeader avatar pill** (UI minor), **N. v2 methodology authoring** (curator-track).
- **The "pre-emptive for non-existent user base" objection has 7+ phases of receipts**. At each prior phase (Phase 20-27), deferral was defensible because Q54/Q55/Q69 operational gates were pending + the project hadn't deployed. But: (a) the deferral cost grows linearly each phase; (b) the project should plan the architectural surface BEFORE deployment so the deploy + user-acquisition timeline isn't blocked on multi-provider work; (c) Q54 itself names "GitHub OAuth registration" as an operational gate — broadening to multi-provider lets the curator confront the OAuth surface uniformly across providers (single Q54 + new **Q73** Google OAuth registration mirrors Q54's shape). **First phase since Phase 19 (10 phases) to reverse a sustained patience-signal deferral.**
- **No DB schema change anticipated** (D-2). The `accounts` table already supports multi-provider per Auth.js v5 default schema (one row per `(provider, providerAccountId)`); `users.githubLogin` stays nullable; new sign-ins via non-GitHub providers leave it NULL. Migration count stays at **6**; **twelfth consecutive 0-migration phase anticipated** (Phase 17-28; 15 of 19 phases since DB landed are 0-migration). §5.7 trigger (a) FIRED Unit 9.6 carried; trigger (b) cold.
- **Provider choice for ADR-0020** (D-3): **Google OAuth** as the second provider. Reasons: broadest reach among non-GitHub users (academic users especially have Google accounts via institutional emails); `next-auth/providers/google` adapter is the most-tested non-GitHub Auth.js v5 provider; aligns with §5.8's broader "OAuth" recommendation when GitHub-only is too narrow. Provider count after Phase 28: **2** (GitHub + Google). Account-linking strategy: Auth.js default `allowDangerousEmailAccountLinking: false`; same-email-different-provider → two separate `users.id` rows (Phase 29+ candidate for account-merge UI). Sign-in UX: per-provider buttons (no chooser modal); mirrors ADR-0012 D-D redirect-to-provider pattern verbatim per provider.
- **ADR-0020 D-clauses anticipated** (D-4): D-A multi-provider runtime contract (`next-auth/providers/*` adapters allowed; ADR-0012 D-A "no other auth runtimes" rule preserved); D-B second provider = Google (explicit selection); D-C env-var convention (`AUTH_GOOGLE_ID` + `AUTH_GOOGLE_SECRET` per Auth.js v5 canonical names; env count 7 → 9); D-D curator-of-record gate remains GitHub-only (`editorial.primary_curator` joins to `users.githubLogin` which is populated only via GitHub `linkAccount` event; non-GitHub users CANNOT be curators in Phase 28; Q74 surfaces this as Phase 29+ architectural question); D-E account-linking strategy (Auth.js default `allowDangerousEmailAccountLinking: false`); D-F sign-in UX is per-provider buttons (deferred chooser modal to Phase 30+ if 3+ providers).
- **`lib/auth/index.ts` extension scope** (D-5): in-place edit adding `Google` import + entry in `providers: [GitHub, Google]` array; `linkAccount` event guard `account.provider !== "github"` Phase-9 verbatim preserved (Google sign-in does NOT populate `githubLogin`); `users.githubLogin` stays nullable.
- **`components/auth-control/index.tsx` UI extension scope** (D-6): signed-out branch renders 2 `<form>` elements (one per provider) stacked vertically on mobile + side-by-side `sm:` breakpoint; each button shows provider name via new i18n keys `auth.sign_in_with_github` + `auth.sign_in_with_google`; signed-in branch unchanged. Existing `auth.sign_in` key kept (used as form-group aria-label). i18n count 140 → **142** per locale anticipated (+2 × 2 locales = +4 total).
- **Env-var contract changes** (D-7): `.env.example` extended with Google OAuth credentials block (`AUTH_GOOGLE_ID` + `AUTH_GOOGLE_SECRET`); env-var count 7 → **9**. Mirrors Phase-9's GitHub OAuth `.env.example` pattern.
- **Test surface anticipated** (D-8): extend `lib/auth/index.test.ts` (or create if absent) with provider-array assertions + `linkAccount` guard regression test; extend `components/auth-control/index.test.tsx` (or create) with signed-out 2-button rendering + signed-in unchanged tests. Anticipated test count delta: **+4 to +8 tests** → 609-613 / 55-56 vitest files at Phase 28 close.
- **New OPEN_QUESTIONS** (D-9): **Q73** (Google OAuth app registration; operational gate; mirrors Q54 shape) + **Q74** (non-GitHub users as curators; architectural; non-blocking; Phase 29+ if curator demand surfaces). **Q-tally freeze BROKEN at 8 phases** (Phase 20-27 = 8-phase freeze; Phase 28 breaks cleanly via +Q73 +Q74). OPEN_QUESTIONS delta: 26 resolved / 4 lean / 29 open → 26 resolved / 4 lean / **31 open** = **61 total** (+2 net).
- **ADR-0012 D-B amendment** (D-10): APPEND-not-EDIT phase-tagged note per the implementation-note convention codified Phase 23 + extended retroactively Phase 27. D-B's "GitHub OAuth as the ONLY initial provider" is **lifted** by ADR-0020 with explicit cross-reference. **First time an ADR D-clause is superseded by a follow-on ADR rather than amended via implementation note** (Phase 19-24-27 amended own-D-clause; Phase 28 amends an EARLIER ADR's D-clause to cross-reference a NEW ADR).
- **Anticipated unit breakdown** (6 units; mirrors Phase 19's new-ADR shape; breaks the 8-phase 5-unit streak):
  | Unit | Title | Type |
  |---|---|---|
  | 28.0 | **Phase 28 prep** (this unit) | docs |

  | 28.5 | **Phase 28 acceptance gate** | gate |

- **Phase-28-blocking decisions deferred to per-unit implementation** (D-12 through D-17): D-12 (sign-in UX layout: stacked mobile + `sm:` side-by-side); D-13 (account-linking dangerous-flag: Auth.js default `false`); D-14 (`<AuthControl>` test framework: keep existing pattern); D-15 (Q73 wording: mirror Q54 verbatim); D-16 (provider-array ordering: GitHub first); D-17 (Google provider profile field mapping: Auth.js default — name/email/picture).

#### Unit 27.3 — OPEN_QUESTIONS hygiene + ADR review (ADR-0018 D-G Phase-27 enhancement note + Phase-18 retroactive note appended; **19 ADRs**; first eight-phase Q-tally freeze)

- Fourth Phase-27 unit; docs-only. Mirrors Phase-13 through Phase-26 hygiene patterns. **One surface change**: ADR-0018 D-G gets a Phase-27 enhancement note appended + Phase-18 retroactive note back-filled per the APPEND-not-EDIT convention codified Phase 22+23 + extended Phase 24. **First APPEND-not-EDIT notes on a third distinct ADR** — convention now spans 3 ADRs (ADR-0014 D-D + ADR-0019 D-F + ADR-0018 D-G).
- **ADR-0018 D-G annotations**: (1) **REALIZED Phase 18 Unit 18.2** retroactive note (back-fills the Phase-18 reviewNotes sibling realization to make the three-phase audit trail visible directly on the ADR; this had been documented implicitly in Phase-18 hygiene catalog but not appended per the Phase-22+ convention); (2) **REALIZED Phase 27 Unit 27.1** note documenting `renderRationaleMarkdown` + `rationaleSchema` third sibling realization (signature `string → string` differs from prior siblings; 4 new call sites in one code unit; line-clamp-replacing-source-truncation pattern extended across 3 listing pages; Phase-28+ remaining markdown-promotion candidate = rating-action `rationale`).

Item 1 (markdown rationale) actually closes the open ADR-0018 D-G deferral wording "possibly rating-action `rationale`", so ADR annotation is the correct target per source-of-truth-of-deferral rule.
- **OPEN_QUESTIONS tally unchanged**: 26 resolved + 4 lean + 29 open = **59 total** (same as Phase 19-26 close). **Eighth consecutive no-promotion / no-status-change hygiene pass** (Phase 20-27). **First eight-phase Q-tally freeze in project history**.

ADR-0014 still carries Phase-22 + Phase-23 notes on D-D; ADR-0019 still carries Phase-24 note on D-F. **Total ADR count stays at 19**. **Eighth consecutive no-new-ADR phase** (Phase 20-27 = **first eight-phase no-new-ADR streak**). **Ninth phase since Phase 3 to close without new ADR** (Phase 18 + 20-27).

#### Unit 27.1 — `rationaleSchema` + `renderRationaleMarkdown` + 4 page updates + 10 new tests (605/55)

- Second Phase-27 unit; code. Closes **Phase-26 B.10 items 1+2** (markdown rationale + "View details" listing links) in one bundled code unit. **Third call site for the ADR-0018 D-G inheritance pattern** (bio Phase-17 + reviewNotes Phase-18 + rationale Phase-27). **First eight-phase no-new-ADR streak** anticipated at Phase 27 close.
- **Markdown lib extension** (`lib/markdown/`):
  - New `rationaleSchema` export in `sanitize-schema.ts` (sibling to `bioSchema` + `reviewNotesSchema`; **identical to `bioSchema` Phase-27** via explicit shallow copy `{ ...baseSchemaConfig }` per Phase-18 `reviewNotesSchema` precedent). Phase-28+ Q72-style divergence candidates for rationale-specific markdown features.
  - New `renderRationaleMarkdown(text: string): string` helper in `index.ts` + `rationaleProcessor` singleton (identical pipeline shape to `bioProcessor` + `reviewNotesProcessor`; uses `rationaleSchema`). **Differs from siblings in signature**: `string → string` (not `string | null → string | null`) because rationale is NOT NULL per Phase-11 schema (50-2000 chars required). No null-fallback path; caller-guaranteed non-empty.
- **+10 new vitest tests** at `lib/markdown/index.test.ts` (mirrors Phase-18 reviewNotes test pattern):
  - 7 tests for `renderRationaleMarkdown` (bold; https link; XSS strip javascript: URL; XSS strip raw script tag; heading demotion `#` → `<h3>`; unordered list render; type-guard `string → string`).
  - 3 tests for `rationaleSchema` parity with `bioSchema` (tagNames; protocols; attributes).
  - Test count: **605 / 55 files** (was 595/55 at Phase 26 close); +10 tests / 0 files Δ.
- **Detail page update** (`app/[locale]/u/[handle]/challenges/[id]/page.tsx`): replaces `<p className="...whitespace-pre-wrap">{challenge.rationale}</p>` with `<div className="...prose styling..." dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: renderRationaleMarkdown(challenge.rationale) }} />`. Reuses exact 14 arbitrary-variant prose styling utility classes Phase-18 established for reviewNotes. Phase-26 detail page now renders rationale as full markdown.
- **3 listing pages updated**: `/u/[handle]/challenges` + `/profile` + `/problems/[slug]/challenges` all switch from `truncateRationale(rationale)` source-truncation to `renderRationaleMarkdown(rationale)` + `line-clamp-3` CSS visual truncation (matches Phase-18 reviewNotes line-clamp on `/profile`). **`truncateRationale()` helper removed** from all 3 files (dead code after Phase 27; rationale is now always markdown-rendered with line-clamp). New shared `RATIONALE_PROSE_CLASSES` constant per listing page.
- **3 listing pages add "View details" link** per row: targets `/u/{githubLogin}/challenges/{id}` where `githubLogin` is the submitter login (per-problem listing uses `row.submitterLogin`; per-user uses `profile.githubLogin`; profile uses signed-in `githubLogin`; profile additionally guards with `isPublicChallengeStatus()` because detail page 404s for rejected/withdrawn — those are private to submitter on profile listing). Reuses existing accent-link styling.
- **i18n: 1 new key** `public_challenges.view_details` added to both `en.json` ("View details →") + `fr.json` ("Voir les détails →"). i18n keys per locale: **139 → 140** (Phase 26 added 12 in `public_challenge_detail`; Phase 27 adds 1 in `public_challenges`).
- **Server-only rendering**: all markdown render is server-side (Phase-18 ADR-0018 D-F invariant carried). No client bundle impact; **First Load JS shared chunk UNCHANGED at 103 kB** end-to-end through every Phase 9-27 unit; **Middleware bundle UNCHANGED at 160 kB**. **All 4 page bundles UNCHANGED**: `/profile` 117 kB; `/u/{handle}/challenges` 108 kB; `/u/{handle}/challenges/[id]` 108 kB; `/problems/[slug]/challenges` 108 kB.
- **No new dep Phase 27**. Reuses Phase-17 `unified` markdown stack verbatim.
- **No new env var Phase 27**.
- **No new ADR Phase 27**. ADR-0018 D-G inheritance contract already pinned the multi-surface pattern. ADR count stays at **19**.
- **No DB schema change Phase 27**. `ratingChallenges.rationale` column unchanged (still TEXT 50-2000 chars); only render layer promotes to markdown. Migration count stays at **6**.
- Smoke gates: `pnpm typecheck` clean; `pnpm lint` clean; `pnpm test` → **605/605 across 55 vitest files** (+10 / 0 from Phase 26); `pnpm build` → 8 dynamic page+API routes UNCHANGED; First Load JS 103 kB UNCHANGED; all 4 page bundles UNCHANGED; `pnpm audit-content` → 0 errors / 6 warnings (Q32 baseline unchanged).
- Files modified: `lib/markdown/sanitize-schema.ts` (added `rationaleSchema`); `lib/markdown/index.ts` (added `rationaleProcessor` + `renderRationaleMarkdown`); `lib/markdown/index.test.ts` (+10 tests); `app/[locale]/u/[handle]/challenges/[id]/page.tsx` (Phase-26 detail page; rationale render → markdown); `app/[locale]/u/[handle]/challenges/page.tsx` (listing; markdown + line-clamp + "View details" link; `truncateRationale` removed); `app/[locale]/profile/page.tsx` (listing; markdown + line-clamp + "View details" link with public-status guard; `truncateRationale` removed; `isPublicChallengeStatus` + `tPC` imports added); `app/[locale]/problems/[slug]/challenges/page.tsx` (listing; markdown + line-clamp + "View details" link; `truncateRationale` removed); `messages/en.json` + `messages/fr.json` (+1 `view_details` key).

#### Unit 26.4 — Phase 26 acceptance gate (per-challenge detail page realized; **19 ADRs**; operational-script-keystone streak broken at 6; first user-facing UX since Phase 18)

- Fifth and final Phase-26 unit; docs-only. Closes Phase 26 (**seventeenth NON-§13 phase**; per-challenge detail page realized via `/[locale]/u/[handle]/challenges/[id]` route). Mirrors Phase-12 through Phase-25 acceptance-gate patterns. **§13 ledger CLOSED at Unit 9.9** (carried unchanged through Phases 10-25).
- **Phase 26 ledger**: **5 units shipped** (26.0 prep → 26.4 gate). **0 deferrals**; **0 scope drift**. **Fifteenth consecutive well-scoped phase**. **Matches Phase 20-25 5-unit shape** despite breaking the operational-script-keystone rhythm.
- **Phase-26 closure summary**: Phase-11 + Phase-13 carryover CLOSED in Unit 26.1 via `app/[locale]/u/[handle]/challenges/[id]/page.tsx` + `getPublicChallengeDetailById` helper + `shouldShowReviewerInfo` predicate + 12 new i18n keys × 2 locales + 3 new visibility-predicate tests. Page renders full rationale + (when status=accepted) reviewer info + reviewNotes markdown + acceptedActionId filename. Server-side visibility filter via helper: returns null on id-miss, ownership-mismatch, or private-status (rejected/withdrawn). Defense-in-depth: helper additionally nullifies reviewer fields for non-accepted public statuses regardless of DB row state. **Operational-script-keystone streak BROKEN cleanly at 6 phases** (Phase 20-25 = 6 consecutive; Phase 26 = first non-operational-script-keystone since Phase 19). **First user-facing UX since Phase 18** = 8 phases of curator/server-only work. **First new public page route since Phase 14** = 12 phases without new public route.

#### Unit 26.1 — `/[locale]/u/[handle]/challenges/[id]` page + `getPublicChallengeDetailById` helper + i18n keys + visibility tests

- Second Phase-26 unit; code. Ships `/[locale]/u/[handle]/challenges/[id]` page route + `getPublicChallengeDetailById` helper + new `public_challenge_detail` i18n namespace (12 keys × 2 locales) + 3 new visibility-predicate tests. **Closes Phase-11 + Phase-13 carryover** (12+ phases each). **Ships first user-facing UX since Phase 18** = 8 phases of curator/server-only work; **first new public page route since Phase 14** = 5 phases without new public route.
- **Page route** (`app/[locale]/u/[handle]/challenges/[id]/page.tsx`): server component with `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"` per Phase-14 D-11 (DB-backed read on every request). Resolves `locale` via `isLocale` (404 on invalid); `handle` via `getPublicProfileByHandle` (404 on no-match); `id` UUID passes to `getPublicChallengeDetailById({ id, profileUserId: profile.userId })` (404 on no-match, ownership mismatch, OR private status). Mirrors Phase-14 listing page styling (status badge CSS classes; `back_to_challenges` link to `/u/{handle}/challenges`). New page bundle: **108 kB** (matches `/[locale]/u/[handle]/challenges` listing); First Load JS shared chunk **103 kB UNCHANGED**.
- **Render structure**: header (page title + submitter @handle + submitted date + status badge); details `<dl>` (problem linked to `/problems/<slug>` + dimension translated + proposed value mono); full rationale (`whitespace-pre-wrap` per Phase-13 listing precedent; **NOT** markdown — Phase 27+ if F. markdown rationale promotes); **when status === "accepted"**: reviewer info `<dl>` (reviewer @login linked + reviewed date + acceptedActionId filename mono) + reviewNotes markdown rendered via Phase-18 `renderReviewNotesMarkdown` helper + same 14 arbitrary-variant prose styling utility classes Phase-18 established (XSS-safe via ADR-0018 sanitization pipeline).
- **`getPublicChallengeDetailById({ id, profileUserId })` helper** in `lib/rating-challenges/index.ts`: single query joining `ratingChallenges` + aliased users (submitter via `users` + reviewer via drizzle `alias(users, "reviewer_users")`). Server-side visibility filter: `id` matches AND `userId === profileUserId` AND `status ∈ PUBLIC_CHALLENGE_STATUSES`. Returns null on any filter miss → caller maps to `notFound()`. Reviewer fields (`reviewerLogin` / `reviewedAt` / `reviewNotes` / `acceptedActionId`) populated ONLY when `status === "accepted"` per `shouldShowReviewerInfo` pure predicate; non-accepted public statuses (`submitted` / `under_review`) get nulls regardless of DB row state (defense-in-depth vs UI filtering).
- **`shouldShowReviewerInfo(status)` pure predicate** added to same module: returns `true` only for `accepted` (matches Phase-13 Unit 13.0 D-3 + Phase-14 listing precedent — curator deliberation private until terminal-accepted). Unit-testable without DB.
- **+3 new visibility tests** at `lib/rating-challenges/public-visibility.test.ts` (new `describe` block "shouldShowReviewerInfo — Phase-26 D-3 detail-page visibility" covering: `accepted` → true; `submitted` → false; `under_review` → false). Test count: **595 / 55 files** (was 592/55 at Phase 25 close); +3 tests / 0 files Δ.
- **New `public_challenge_detail` i18n namespace** (12 keys × 2 locales): `back_to_challenges` / `page_title` / `submitter_label` / `submitted_at_label` / `problem_label` / `dimension_label` / `proposed_value_label` / `rationale_label` / `reviewer_label` / `reviewed_at_label` / `review_notes_label` / `action_attached_label`. **First i18n key change since Phase 17 close** (i18n keys per locale: 127 → 139). FR keys machine-translated per Phase-17/18 precedent.
- **No new dep Phase 26**. Reuses `next-intl` + `next/navigation` (Phase-7) + `drizzle-orm` + `drizzle-orm/sqlite-core` `alias()` (existing) + `renderReviewNotesMarkdown` from Phase-18.
- **No new env var Phase 26**. Reuses Turso vars (Q55).
- **Server-side rendering only**: `force-dynamic` route. No client bundle impact; **First Load JS shared chunk UNCHANGED at 103 kB** per ADR-0018 D-F invariant extension; **Middleware bundle UNCHANGED at 160 kB**.
- **Page bundle counts**: dynamic page+API routes count: 7 → **8** (added `ƒ /[locale]/u/[handle]/challenges/[id]`). Total prerendered ~659 unchanged.
- **No new ADR Phase 26**. Phase-13 ADR-0014 D-? public-visibility rules already pinned; Phase-26 applies verbatim. ADR count stays at **19**. **Eighth phase since Phase 3 to close without new ADR** (Phase 18 + 20 + 21 + 22 + 23 + 24 + 25 + 26; **seven-phase no-new-ADR streak** — first ever).
- Smoke gates: `pnpm typecheck` clean; `pnpm lint` clean; `pnpm test` → **595/595 across 55 vitest files** (+3 / 0 from Phase 25); `pnpm build` → **8 dynamic page+API routes** (was 7; +1 `/[locale]/u/[handle]/challenges/[id]`); First Load JS 103 kB UNCHANGED; `pnpm audit-content` → 0 errors / 6 warnings (Q32 baseline unchanged).
- Files modified: `lib/rating-challenges/index.ts` (added `shouldShowReviewerInfo` predicate + `PublicChallengeDetail` interface + `getPublicChallengeDetailById` helper with drizzle `alias` import); `lib/rating-challenges/public-visibility.test.ts` (+3 tests); `messages/en.json` (+12 keys in new `public_challenge_detail` namespace); `messages/fr.json` (+12 FR keys). Files added: `app/[locale]/u/[handle]/challenges/[id]/page.tsx` (server component).

#### Unit 26.0 — Phase 26 prep (per-challenge detail page; first user-facing UX since Phase 18; **19 ADRs unchanged**; twenty-first "Continue" override)

- First Phase-26 unit; docs-only. Opens Phase 26 (**seventeenth NON-§13 phase**). Mirrors Phase-12 through Phase-25 phase-prep patterns. **§13 ledger CLOSED** at Unit 9.9 (carried unchanged through Phases 10-25); Phase 26 inferred-not-§13 like its sixteen predecessors.
- **Phase 26 sign-off granted via "Continue" override** in the unit-rhythm rhythm (**twenty-first invocation** of this pattern). §12 cardinal rule satisfied; first-thread D-1 recommendation overridable into Unit 26.1.
- **Recommended thread: per-challenge detail page** (D-1). Ships `/[locale]/u/[handle]/challenges/[id]` route — full rationale + accepted-status reviewer/reviewNotes/acceptedActionId display. **Closes Phase-11 + Phase-13 carryover** (12+ phases each). **Ships first user-facing UX since Phase 18** = 8 phases of curator/server-only work; breaks the 6-phase operational-script-keystone rhythm (Phase 20-25). Anticipated phase shape: **5 units** (matches Phase 20-25); **no new ADR** (Phase-13 ADR-0014 D-? public-visibility rules already pinned; Phase 26 applies verbatim).
- **Tradeoffs flagged**: passing on **H. Multi-provider OAuth expansion** (Phase-9 Class B item 8 carried **15 phases** — strongest architectural patience signal; ADR-0020 candidate; pre-emptive for non-existent user base) + **K-other. Operational tail bundled** (~2.5 units; would be 7th consecutive operational-script-keystone; diminishing returns at this point — user-facing UX gap more pressing after 8 phases of curator/server-only work). Also passing on **F. Markdown rating-action `rationale`** (would couple to E but Phase 26 lean keeps rationale as plain-text per Phase-13 listing precedent; Phase 27+ if user demand).
- **No DB schema change anticipated** (D-2). Read-only DB query via existing helpers (`getChallengeById`, `getPublicProfileByHandle`); detail page introduces a new helper `getPublicChallengeDetailById({ id, profileUserId })` that joins users (submitter) + users (reviewer; aliased) + applies server-side visibility filter. Migration count stays at **6**; **tenth consecutive 0-migration phase anticipated** (Phase 17-26; 13 of 17 phases since DB landed are 0-migration). §5.7 trigger (a) FIRED Unit 9.6 carried; trigger (b) cold.
- **Route shape anticipated** (D-3): `/[locale]/u/[handle]/challenges/[id]` — extends existing Phase-14 per-user namespace (`/u/{handle}` + `/u/{handle}/challenges`); adds `[id]` UUID segment. Params validated: `locale` via `isLocale`; `handle` resolves to `userId` via `getPublicProfileByHandle`; `id` UUID passes through. **Visibility logic**: resolve handle (404 on no-match); fetch challenge (404 on no-match); verify `challenge.userId === profile.userId` (404 on mismatch — prevents enumeration via handle/id pair shopping); verify `challenge.status ∈ PUBLIC_CHALLENGE_STATUSES` (404 on private rejected/withdrawn). **Render**: status badge + submitter @handle + submitted date + problem title (linked) + dimension + proposed value + full rationale (plain text; `whitespace-pre-wrap` per Phase-13 listing precedent). **When status = accepted**: reviewer @login + reviewed date + reviewNotes markdown (via Phase-18 `renderReviewNotesMarkdown` helper) + acceptedActionId filename. **When status ∈ {submitted, under_review}**: no reviewer info / no reviewNotes / no acceptedActionId (deliberation in progress per Phase-13 D-3).
- **Visibility helper anticipated** (D-4): new `getPublicChallengeDetailById({ id, profileUserId }): Promise<PublicChallengeDetail | null>` in `lib/rating-challenges/index.ts`. Single query joining `ratingChallenges` + aliased users (submitter + reviewer). Server-side visibility filter (defense-in-depth vs UI filtering). Returns null for any filter miss (single 404 path on page). Extends existing `PublicChallengeRow` interface with `reviewerLogin` / `reviewedAt` / `reviewNotes` fields (populated only for accepted status; null for non-accepted public states).
- **i18n keys anticipated** (D-5): new `public_challenge_detail` namespace (~6-8 keys: `back_to_challenges` / `problem_label` / `dimension_label` / `proposed_value_label` / `submitted_label` / `reviewer_label` / `reviewed_label` / `review_notes_label`) + reuse existing `public_challenges.*` keys for status/rationale/action-attached. **First i18n key change since Phase 17 close** (i18n keys per locale: 127 → ~133 anticipated).
- **Test surface anticipated** (D-6): new page route test covering 8 cases — renders for all 3 public statuses (submitted/under_review/accepted); 404 on private rejected/withdrawn; 404 on handle/id mismatch; 404 on missing handle; 404 on missing id; reviewer info hidden for submitted/under_review; reviewer info shown for accepted; reviewNotes renders markdown for accepted. Anticipated test count delta: **+6 to +8 tests** → 598-600 / 55 or 56 vitest files at Phase 26 close.
- **No new ADR Phase 26** (D-7). Phase-13 ADR-0014 D-? public-visibility rules already pinned; Phase-26 applies verbatim. **Eighth phase since Phase 3 to close without new ADR** (Phase 18 + 20 + 21 + 22 + 23 + 24 + 25 + 26 anticipated; **seven-phase no-new-ADR streak**). ADR count stays at **19**.

Phase 25 demonstrates the convention applies cleanly to bulk-catch-up phases that close multiple class-B items at once.

#### Unit 25.1 — 4 operational follow-ons bundled (methodology-version + batch-emit + --no-prior-fill + --user-id; +7 tests; 592/55)

- Second Phase-25 unit; code. Ships 4 small operational follow-ons across Phase 22+23+24 surfaces. Closes **Phase-22 B.6 items 3+4**, **Phase-23 B.7**, and **Phase-24 B.8 item 1**. **Sixth consecutive operational-script-keystone phase** in project history (Phase 20-25).
- **Item 1 — Methodology-version dynamic read** (Phase-22 B.6 item 3 closure): new `readMethodologyVersion({ contentRoot }): Promise<string>` helper in `lib/rating-challenges/scaffold.ts`. Reads `<contentRoot>/methodology/v*.mdx` files (filters out locale variants matching `^v\d+\.mdx$`); sorts filenames lexically descending; tries each via frontmatter regex `/^version:\s*["']([^"']+)["']/m`; returns first parsed version OR fallback `METHODOLOGY_VERSION` constant (`"1.0.0"`). `ScaffoldInput` gains optional `methodologyVersion?` parameter; `buildRatingActionYaml` uses `input.methodologyVersion ?? METHODOLOGY_VERSION`. CLI orchestration calls helper + passes through. **Phase 26+ if v2 methodology lands** (e.g., `content/methodology/v2.mdx` with `version: "2.0.0"`), the helper picks it up automatically without code change. Defensive: helper failure → fallback to hardcoded constant (no functional regression).
- **Item 2 — Batch-emit-multiple `--all-under-review`** (Phase-22 B.6 item 4 closure): new flag on `scripts/emit-challenge-action.ts`. When set: skip `<challenge-id>` positional arg; query `db.select().from(ratingChallenges).where(eq(status, "under_review"))`; process each in series via existing per-challenge flow (factored into new `scaffoldOne()` helper); per-row try/catch (one bad row doesn't kill batch); summary at end with succeeded / failed counts. Exit 0 if all succeeded; 1 if any failed. **Mutually exclusive with `<challenge-id>` positional arg** (parseArgs validates with helpful error). Stackable with `--dry-run` (prints each YAML separated by `---`) and `--no-prior-fill`. Refactor: extracted `validateChallenge()` + `scaffoldOne()` helpers to share single-challenge logic between modes.
- **Item 3 — `--no-prior-fill` opt-out** (Phase-23 B.7 closure): new flag on same script. When set: skip `readPriorRatingAction` call; pass `priorAction: null` to `buildRatingActionYaml` (forces Phase-22 placeholder behavior on OTHER 4 dimensions + `prior_action` + `signals_considered` + `watchlist`). Useful when prior data is suspect / curator wants to re-author OTHER dimensions from scratch. Compatible with `--all-under-review` (both flags can stack for batch placeholder mode).
- **Item 4 — `--user-id=<id>` flag** (Phase-24 B.8 item 1 closure): new flag on `scripts/backfill-resize-webp.ts`. When set: AND existing `isNotNull(imageOverride)` query result with `r.id === userId` filter in TS (post-query). Mutually compatible with `--dry-run`. Defends against accidental full-batch run when only one user needs the re-process (e.g., user reports broken avatar after upload; curator surgically re-runs).
- **+7 new vitest tests** at `lib/rating-challenges/scaffold.test.ts`: 5 covering `readMethodologyVersion` (no methodology dir → fallback; empty methodology dir → fallback; v1.mdx frontmatter parse; v2.mdx picks highest version Phase 26+ forward-compat; ignores locale variants like `v1.fr.mdx`) + 2 covering `buildRatingActionYaml` methodologyVersion parameter (explicit override; default fallback when undefined). Test count: **592 / 55 files** (was 585/55 at Phase 24 close); +7 tests / 0 files Δ.
- **No new dep Phase 25**. Reuses `yaml@2.9.0` (Phase-0) + drizzle-orm (Phase-9) + `fs/promises` (Node built-in) + `RatingActionSchema` (Phase-0).
- **No new env var Phase 25**. Reuses existing Turso vars (Q55) + `BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN` (Q69).
- **Server-only**: all helpers + scripts are server-only. No client bundle impact; **First Load JS shared chunk UNCHANGED at 103 kB** per ADR-0018 D-F invariant extension. **Middleware bundle UNCHANGED at 160 kB**.
- **No new ADR Phase 25**. All 4 items are operational-follow-on closures with no architectural surface. **Seventh phase since Phase 3 to close without new ADR** (Phase 18 + 20 + 21 + 22 + 23 + 24 + 25; **six-phase no-new-ADR streak** — first ever). ADR count stays at **19**.
- Smoke gates: `pnpm typecheck` clean; `pnpm lint` clean; `pnpm test` → **592/592 across 55 vitest files** (+7 / 0 from Phase 24); `pnpm emit-challenge-action --help` + `pnpm backfill-resize-webp --help` confirm both scripts' new flags register correctly; `pnpm audit-content` → 0 errors / 6 warnings (Q32 baseline unchanged).
- Files modified: `lib/rating-challenges/scaffold.ts` (added `readMethodologyVersion` + `METHODOLOGY_FILENAME` + `METHODOLOGY_VERSION_FRONTMATTER` constants + `methodologyVersion?` field on `ScaffoldInput` + parameter pass-through in `buildRatingActionYaml`); `lib/rating-challenges/scaffold.test.ts` (+7 tests across 2 new describe blocks); `scripts/emit-challenge-action.ts` (added `--all-under-review` + `--no-prior-fill` flags; factored `validateChallenge()` + `scaffoldOne()` helpers + `readUnderReviewChallenges()` query; threaded `readMethodologyVersion` call); `scripts/backfill-resize-webp.ts` (added `--user-id=<id>` flag).

#### Unit 25.0 — Phase 25 prep (K-other bundle: 4 operational follow-ons across Phase 22+23+24; **19 ADRs unchanged**; twentieth "Continue" override)

- First Phase-25 unit; docs-only. Opens Phase 25 (**sixteenth NON-§13 phase**). Mirrors Phase-12 through Phase-24 phase-prep patterns. **§13 ledger CLOSED** at Unit 9.9 (carried unchanged through Phases 10-24); Phase 25 inferred-not-§13 like its fifteen predecessors.
- **Phase 25 sign-off granted via "Continue" override** in the unit-rhythm rhythm (**twentieth invocation** of this pattern). §12 cardinal rule satisfied; first-thread D-1 recommendation overridable into Unit 25.1.
- **Recommended thread: 4 operational follow-ons bundled** (D-1). Closes 4 Class-B carryovers spanning Phase 22+23+24: (1) **methodology-version dynamic read** (Phase-22 B.6 item 3) — new `readMethodologyVersion({ contentRoot })` helper in `lib/rating-challenges/scaffold.ts` reads `content/methodology/v*.mdx` frontmatter (highest version wins; fallback to `"1.0.0"`); (2) **batch-emit-multiple via `--all-under-review`** (Phase-22 B.6 item 4) — new flag on `emit-challenge-action.ts` processes all challenges with status=`under_review` in one invocation; (3) **`--no-prior-fill` opt-out** (Phase-23 B.7) — new flag forces Phase-22 placeholder behavior; (4) **`--user-id` flag** (Phase-24 B.8 item 1) — new flag on `backfill-resize-webp.ts` scopes retroactive resize to a single user. Anticipated phase shape: **5 units** (matches Phase 20-24); **no new ADR** (all 4 items are operational-follow-on closures; no ADR D-clause involvement).
- **Tradeoffs flagged**: passing on **H. Multi-provider OAuth expansion** (Phase-9 Class B item 8 carried **14 phases** — strongest architectural patience signal at right scope; ADR-0020 candidate; pre-emptive for non-existent user base given 3 operational gates still pending). Also passing on **E. Per-challenge detail page** (Phase-11+13 carryover; would ship **first user-facing UX since Phase 18** but breaks operational-script-keystone rhythm with a different phase shape). Also passing on **C-other AVIF** (~1-2 units; ADR-0019 D-F row 4 half; format-choice design implications push AVIF beyond "small operational" scope — needs new ADR amendment first). K-other-narrowed wins on smallest reasonable scope + closes 4 tracked B-items in one bundle.
- **No DB schema change anticipated** (D-2). Filesystem read + CLI flag handling + scaffolder constant → parameter; all changes are application-layer. Migration count stays at **6**; **ninth consecutive 0-migration phase anticipated** (Phase 17-25; 12 of 16 phases since DB landed are 0-migration). §5.7 trigger (a) FIRED Unit 9.6 carried; trigger (b) cold.
- **Item 1 — Methodology-version dynamic read** (D-3): new `readMethodologyVersion({ contentRoot }): Promise<string>` helper in `lib/rating-challenges/scaffold.ts`. Reads `<contentRoot>/methodology/v*.mdx` (filters out locale variants like `v1.fr.mdx`); sorts filenames lexically descending; tries each via frontmatter regex `/^version:\s*["']([^"']+)["']/m`; returns first parsed version OR fallback `"1.0.0"`. `buildRatingActionYaml` accepts new optional `methodologyVersion?` parameter; defaults to `METHODOLOGY_VERSION` constant when not provided. CLI orchestration calls helper + passes through. Defensive: helper failure → fallback to hardcoded constant (no functional regression). Phase 26+ if v2 methodology lands becomes the natural trigger (currently only v1.mdx exists).
- **Item 2 — Batch-emit-multiple** (D-4): new `--all-under-review` flag on `scripts/emit-challenge-action.ts`. When set: skip `<challenge-id>` positional arg; query `db.select().from(ratingChallenges).where(eq(status, "under_review"))`; process each in series via existing per-challenge flow; per-row try/catch; summary at end. Exit code 0 if all succeeded; 1 if any failed. Mutually exclusive with `<challenge-id>` positional arg (parseArgs validates).
- **Item 3 — `--no-prior-fill` opt-out** (D-5): new flag on same script. When set: skip `readPriorRatingAction` call; pass `priorAction: null` to `buildRatingActionYaml` (forces Phase-22 placeholder behavior). Useful when prior data is suspect / curator wants to re-author from scratch. Compatible with `--all-under-review` (both flags can stack).
- **Item 4 — `--user-id` flag** (D-6): new flag on `scripts/backfill-resize-webp.ts`. When set: AND existing `isNotNull(imageOverride)` query with `eq(users.id, <id>)`. Mutually compatible with `--dry-run`.
- **Pipeline placement anticipated** (D-7): all 4 items extend Phase-22+23+24 surfaces in-place. Items 1+2+3 touch `scripts/emit-challenge-action.ts` + `lib/rating-challenges/scaffold.ts` + `lib/rating-challenges/scaffold.test.ts`; item 4 touches `scripts/backfill-resize-webp.ts`. **Sixth consecutive operational-script-keystone phase**.

#### Unit 24.1 — `putAvatar` resize+WebP + `scripts/backfill-resize-webp.ts` + storage test mocks updated

- Second Phase-24 unit; code. Extends Phase-19 `lib/storage/index.ts` `putAvatar` with the resize+WebP transcode pipeline per ADR-0019 D-F + ships `scripts/backfill-resize-webp.ts` for retroactive application to Phase 16-23 avatars. Closes the **ADR-0019 D-F image-processing inheritance contract** for the resize + WebP format-conversion path (one of the four anticipated Phase-20+ extensions D-F documented). **Fifth consecutive operational-script-keystone phase** in project history (Phase 20 + 21 + 22 + 23 + 24).
- **Forward-path sharp pipeline**: `sharp(buffer).rotate().resize({ width: 512, height: 512, fit: "cover" }).webp({ quality: 85 }).toBuffer()`. Two new transforms inserted between Phase-19's `.rotate()` and `.toBuffer()`: (1) `.resize({...})` scales + crops to 512x512 square (covers hi-DPI use cases for 32-150px display @ 2-4x); (2) `.webp({ quality: 85 })` re-encodes as WebP (~70-80% smaller than equivalent JPEG/PNG at visually-indistinguishable quality). Storage-key extension and content-type are always `.webp` / `image/webp` post-Phase-24 (regardless of input MIME). Input MIME validation UNCHANGED from Phase-16 ADR-0017 D-B (accepted set still `image/jpeg` / `image/png` / `image/webp` on upload — only output format changes).
- **`AVATAR_SIZE` (512) + `WEBP_QUALITY` (85) constants** added to `lib/storage/index.ts` with rationale comments. Phase 25+ if hi-DPI signal demands 768/1024 or observed quality issue.
- **`inferExt()` helper removed** from `lib/storage/index.ts` — Phase-16 MIME-to-extension mapping is dead code after Phase 24 (output is always `.webp` regardless of input). Reduces module surface; simpler test assertions.
- **`scripts/backfill-resize-webp.ts` shipped** — mirrors Phase-20 `backfill-exif-strip.ts` shape with the Phase-24 sharp chain. Per-row pipeline: `fetch(url)` → `sharp(buffer).rotate().resize({...}).webp({...}).toBuffer()` → `put(avatars/<userId>-<ts>-resize.webp, { contentType: "image/webp" })` → `UPDATE user SET imageOverride = <new-url>` → `del(<old-url>)`. **`-resize.webp` storage-key suffix** distinguishes from Phase-20's `-backfill.<ext>` outputs in Vercel Blob console (both backfill cycles can coexist). Same `--dry-run` flag pattern; serial-row processing; per-row try/catch; idempotent re-run.
- **Storage test mocks updated**: sharp mock extended with `.resize()` + `.webp()` returning the same fluent-chain instance. 5 existing Phase-19 tests modified in-place: assertions updated to expect `image/webp` content-type + `.webp` extension regardless of input MIME; new pipeline-shape assertions added (`.resize({ width: 512, height: 512, fit: "cover" })` + `.webp({ quality: 85 })` called once each). Test count stays at **585 / 55 vitest files** (**first Phase to add 0 net new tests** since Phase 21).
- **Reuses Phase-19 `sharp@0.34.5` direct dep + Phase-16 `@vercel/blob` + Phase-9 drizzle-orm verbatim**. **Zero new runtime deps Phase 24**; **zero new env vars Phase 24** (reuses `BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN` + Turso vars).
- **Script alias added** to `package.json#scripts`: `"backfill-resize-webp": "tsx scripts/backfill-resize-webp.ts"`. Mirrors existing operational-script patterns.
- **Server-only**: `lib/storage/` is server-only module; `scripts/backfill-resize-webp.ts` is CLI not bundled into the Next.js app. No client bundle impact; **First Load JS shared chunk UNCHANGED at 103 kB** per ADR-0018 D-F invariant extension. **Middleware bundle UNCHANGED at 160 kB**.
- **No new ADR Phase 24**. ADR-0019 D-F already names the resize+webp pipeline shape; Phase 24 ships the realization. ADR count stays at **19**. **Sixth phase since Phase 3 to close without new ADR** (Phase 18 + 20 + 21 + 22 + 23 + 24; **five-phase no-new-ADR streak** — first ever).
- Smoke gates: `pnpm typecheck` clean; `pnpm lint` clean; `pnpm test` → **585/585 across 55 vitest files** (5 existing tests modified in-place to expect resize+WebP shape; 0 net new tests); `pnpm backfill-resize-webp --help` confirms script registers and arg parsing works; `pnpm audit-content` → 0 errors / 6 warnings (Q32 baseline unchanged).
- Files modified: `lib/storage/index.ts` (added `AVATAR_SIZE` + `WEBP_QUALITY` constants; replaced `putAvatar` sharp chain with resize+WebP version; removed dead `inferExt()` helper); `lib/storage/index.test.ts` (extended sharp mock; updated 5 existing assertions for `.webp` extension + `image/webp` content-type); `package.json` (new script alias). Files added: `scripts/backfill-resize-webp.ts`.

#### Unit 24.0 — Phase 24 prep (ADR-0019 D-F resize+WebP forward + backfill; **19 ADRs unchanged**; nineteenth "Continue" override)

- First Phase-24 unit; docs-only. Opens Phase 24 (**fifteenth NON-§13 phase**). Mirrors Phase-12 through Phase-23 phase-prep patterns. **§13 ledger CLOSED** at Unit 9.9 (carried unchanged through Phases 10-23); Phase 24 inferred-not-§13 like its fourteen predecessors.
- **Phase 24 sign-off granted via "Continue" override** in the unit-rhythm rhythm (**nineteenth invocation** of this pattern). §12 cardinal rule satisfied; first-thread D-1 recommendation overridable into Unit 24.1.
- **Recommended thread: server-side resize + WebP forward pipeline + retroactive backfill script** (D-1). **Closes ADR-0019 D-F** — the Phase-20+ image-processing inheritance contract explicitly documents `sharp(buf).rotate().resize(maxSize).webp({ quality: 85 }).toBuffer()` as one of the anticipated extensions. **Carried Phase 19-23 = 5 phases**; smaller than Phase-22's 10-phase ADR-0014 D-D carryover but a real explicit-deferral signal. Anticipated phase shape: **5 units** (matches Phase 20-23); **no new ADR** (ADR-0019 D-F already names the pipeline shape; Phase 24 ships realization).
- **Tradeoff flagged**: passing on **H. Multi-provider OAuth expansion** (Phase-9 Class B item 8 carried **13 phases** — strongest architectural patience signal at right scope; ADR-0020 candidate). H is pre-emptive for non-existent user base given 3 operational gates (Q54/Q55/Q69) still pending — project hasn't deployed yet. C (this thread) ships **first concrete user-value-at-deploy** in the operational-script-keystone streak (smaller avatar files + bandwidth savings; Phase 20-23 closures were curator-workflow improvements). Also passing on K-other (Phase-22+23 scaffold operational follow-ons bundle, ~1.5 units combined) — low value-per-unit with no observed demand signal on the individual items.
- **No DB schema change anticipated** (D-2). Filesystem-only changes to storage pipeline + new operational script. Migration count stays at **6**; **eighth consecutive 0-migration phase anticipated** (Phase 17-24; 11 of 15 phases since DB landed are 0-migration: Phase 10 + 13 + 14 + 17 + 18 + 19 + 20 + 21 + 22 + 23 + 24). §5.7 trigger (a) FIRED Unit 9.6 carried; trigger (b) cold.
- **Forward-path scope anticipated** (D-3): `sharp(buffer).rotate().resize({ width: 512, height: 512, fit: "cover" }).webp({ quality: 85 }).toBuffer()` inserted between Phase-19's `.rotate()` and `.toBuffer()`. Output always `.webp` (storage-key extension + content-type always `image/webp` regardless of input MIME). Input MIME validation UNCHANGED from Phase-16 ADR-0017 D-B (accepted set still `image/jpeg` / `image/png` / `image/webp` on upload — only output format changes). `inferExt()` helper removed from putAvatar call path (dead code after Phase 24); ~5 existing storage tests modified in-place to expect new content-type + extension.
- **Backfill-script scope anticipated** (D-4): `scripts/backfill-resize-webp.ts` mirrors Phase-20 `backfill-exif-strip.ts` shape with the new sharp chain. Storage-key suffix `-resize.webp` (distinct from Phase-20's `-backfill` so both backfill cycles can coexist + be identified in Vercel Blob console). Output always WebP regardless of input. `--dry-run` CLI flag; serial rows; per-row try/catch; idempotent re-run.
- **Pipeline placement anticipated** (D-5): forward path = `lib/storage/index.ts` `putAvatar` extension (in-place edit); backfill path = stand-alone CLI script. ADR-0019 D-C maintained (pipeline runs BEFORE Vercel Blob upload; trust boundary on server).
- **Backwards compatibility + safety anticipated** (D-6): existing Phase 16-23 avatars continue serving original-format blobs until backfill runs (forward path only affects NEW uploads); Phase 24 + Phase 20 backfills coexist cleanly (sharp's pipeline is idempotent in the limit); re-run safety via idempotent transforms (slight pixel difference from re-encoding rounding acceptable per Phase-19 D-1 caveat); `users.imageOverride` DB column unchanged (only URL value changes).
- **Test surface anticipated** (D-7): **0 net new vitest tests Phase 24**; existing 5 Phase-19 `lib/storage/index.test.ts` tests modified in-place (sharp mock extended with `.resize()` + `.webp()`; assertions updated to expect `image/webp` content-type + `.webp` extension regardless of input MIME). Test count stays at **585 / 55 vitest files**. **First Phase-24 metric where test count holds** (Phase 22 added +11; Phase 23 added +10; Phase 24 flat). Backfill script not unit-tested (Phase 20+21 stance — depends on live infrastructure).
- **No new ADR Phase 24** (D-8). ADR-0019 D-F already names the resize+webp pipeline shape; Phase 24 ships realization. **Sixth phase since Phase 3 to close without new ADR** (Phase 18 + 20 + 21 + 22 + 23 + 24 anticipated; **five-phase no-new-ADR streak**). ADR count stays at **19**. ADR-0019 D-F gets a Phase-24 enhancement note appended per the implementation-note convention codified Phase 23 (APPEND-not-EDIT phase-tagged notes).

#### Unit 23.1 — `readPriorRatingAction` + `buildRatingActionYaml` priorAction extension + CLI integration (+10 tests; 585/55)

- Second Phase-23 unit; code. Extends Phase-22's `lib/rating-challenges/scaffold.ts` with **`readPriorRatingAction(opts)`** — a pure-ish async helper that finds + parses the most-recent prior rating-action YAML for a problem — and extends `buildRatingActionYaml` with an optional `priorAction` parameter. When provided, the 4 OTHER dimensions are copied from prior, `prior_action` is auto-set, `signals_considered` is seeded from prior, and `watchlist` inherits. Closes **Phase-22 B.6 items 1+2** (the two largest curator-edit-friction reductions in Phase-22's realization of ADR-0014 D-D).
- **`readPriorRatingAction({ contentRoot, problemSlug })` API**: reads `<contentRoot>/<problemSlug>/ratings/*.yaml` via `fs/promises.readdir`; filters `.yaml` extension; sorts filenames lexically descending (the `YYYY-MM-DD-<descriptive>.yaml` pattern sorts correctly by date); tries each candidate in order until one parses successfully against `RatingActionSchema`. Per-file try/catch logs malformed YAMLs to stderr + skips to next-most-recent. Returns `null` when problem dir doesn't exist, dir is empty, or all candidates fail parse. Takes `contentRoot` as parameter for fixture-based testing — same pattern Phase-22 used to keep `buildRatingActionYaml` pure-function-testable.
- **`buildRatingActionYaml` priorAction extension**: new optional `priorAction?: PriorActionInfo | null` field on `ScaffoldInput`. When provided: (1) the 4 OTHER dimensions copy `grade` / `value` / `stars` + `confidence` + `rationale` verbatim from `prior.action.dimensions[d]`; (2) `prior_action` field is auto-set to `<problemSlug>/<priorAction.filename>` matching existing rating-actions' convention; (3) `signals_considered` is `[...prior.signals_considered, PLACEHOLDER_SIGNAL]` (curator deduplicates / extends); (4) `watchlist` inherits prior state. The PROPOSED dimension is still populated from challenge data (overrides prior for the dimension being challenged). When `priorAction` is null/undefined: Phase-22 placeholder behavior preserved verbatim (regression guard via the explicit null test).
- **Header comment block updated** when prior provided: documents "OTHER 4 dimensions COPIED FROM PRIOR (`<filename>`) — adjust grade/value/stars/confidence/rationale if new signals demand re-rating" + "`prior_action` is auto-set to `<slug>/<filename>`" + "`signals_considered` is seeded from prior + a TODO placeholder — extend or replace". Different wording vs Phase-22's TODO-placeholder messaging.
- **CLI orchestration extended**: `scripts/emit-challenge-action.ts` now calls `readPriorRatingAction({ contentRoot: "content/problems", problemSlug })` before `buildRatingActionYaml(...)` and passes the result through. Prints one of two stdout lines: "Auto-filled OTHER 4 dimensions from prior: `<slug>/<filename>`" (when prior found) OR "No prior rating-action found under `content/problems/<slug>/ratings` — using placeholders." (when no prior). **No new CLI flag**: auto-fill always-on Phase 23; `--no-prior-fill` deferred Phase 24+ if curator demand surfaces.
- **+10 new vitest tests** at `lib/rating-challenges/scaffold.test.ts`: 4 covering `readPriorRatingAction` (no problem dir → null; empty dir → null; 3-action lexical-sort picks latest; malformed YAML skipped + falls back to next-most-recent) + 6 covering `buildRatingActionYaml` priorAction integration (OTHER 4 copied; `prior_action` shape; `signals_considered` seeded; `watchlist` inherited; header text COPIED-FROM-PRIOR origin; null-fallback regression guard). **Test fixtures**: in-test tempdir via `mkdtemp(path.join(tmpdir(), "scaffold-test-"))` with `afterEach` cleanup via `rm({ recursive: true, force: true })` — tests self-contained; no checked-in fixture files.
- **Test count**: **585 / 55 files** (was 575/55 at Phase 22 close); +10 tests / 0 files Δ. **First phase to add tests without adding test files** (Phase 22 added +1 test file; Phase 23 grows existing `scaffold.test.ts` in-place).
- **No new dep Phase 23**. Reuses `yaml@2.9.0` (Phase-0) + `RatingActionSchema` (Phase-0) + `fs/promises` (Node built-in).
- **No new env var Phase 23**. Reuses existing Turso vars (Q55).
- **Server-only**: scaffolder is server-only module (filesystem + DB dependencies). No client bundle impact; **First Load JS shared chunk UNCHANGED at 103 kB** per ADR-0018 D-F invariant extension. **Middleware bundle UNCHANGED at 160 kB**.
- **No new ADR Phase 23**. Phase-23 ships incremental enhancement to ADR-0014 D-D Phase-22 realization. **Fifth phase since Phase 3 to close without new ADR** (Phase 18 + 20 + 21 + 22 + 23; **four-phase no-new-ADR streak** — first ever). ADR count stays at **19**.
- Smoke gates: `pnpm typecheck` clean; `pnpm lint` clean; `pnpm test` → **585/585 across 55 vitest files**; `pnpm audit-content` → 0 errors / 6 warnings (Q32 baseline unchanged).
- Files modified: `lib/rating-challenges/scaffold.ts` (added `readPriorRatingAction` + `PriorActionInfo` interface + `priorAction` field on `ScaffoldInput` + branch in `buildRatingActionYaml` body + dual-mode header comment block); `lib/rating-challenges/scaffold.test.ts` (+10 tests; new in-test tempdir pattern); `scripts/emit-challenge-action.ts` (CLI orchestration: call `readPriorRatingAction` + stdout log + pass through to `buildRatingActionYaml`).

Takes `contentRoot` as parameter for fixture-based testing.

#### Unit 22.1 — `scripts/emit-challenge-action.ts` + `lib/rating-challenges/scaffold.ts` (rating-action YAML scaffolder; pure-function tests)

- Second Phase-22 unit; code. Ships **`scripts/emit-challenge-action.ts`** (DB-read + FS-write orchestration shell) backed by pure-function helpers in **`lib/rating-challenges/scaffold.ts`** (factored out for unit testing). Closes **ADR-0014 D-D**'s explicit operational-helper deferral *"CLI helper `pnpm emit-challenge-action <id>` reading the challenge from the DB + scaffolding the YAML at `content/problems/<slug>/ratings/<date>-<slug>.yaml`. Mirrors Phase-5's `extract-leaderboard` shape."* **Third operational-script-keystone phase** in project history (Phase 20 + 21 + 22).
- **Reuses Phase-9 drizzle-orm + Phase-0 `yaml@2.9.0` + Phase-0 `RatingActionSchema` verbatim**. Same `TURSO_DATABASE_URL` / `TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN` env vars (Q55). **Zero new runtime deps Phase 22**; **zero new env vars Phase 22**.
- **Workflow** (ADR-0014 D-D contract): (1) curator decides to accept challenge in UI; (2) curator runs `pnpm emit-challenge-action <challenge-id>`; (3) script writes `drafts/ratings/<challenge-id>.yaml` (gitignored per Phase-0 `.gitignore` `drafts/` pattern); (4) curator reviews + edits the 4 PLACEHOLDER dimensions + `prior_action` + `signals_considered` TODOs; (5) curator moves draft to `content/problems/<slug>/ratings/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<descriptive>.yaml` + `git add` + commits manually (ADR-0005 immutability hook + `validate-content` Zod gate + cross-link audit fire at commit time); (6) curator returns to `/curator/challenges/<id>` and attaches filename via the `acceptedActionId` form (per ADR-0014 D-D manual-attachment step).
- **Scaffolder pipeline** (5 steps; pure function): (1) read `ratingChallenge` row via `SELECT * FROM ratingChallenge WHERE id = ?`; (2) refuse if status terminal (`accepted` / `rejected` / `withdrawn`) → exit 1; (3) refuse if `reviewerId` NULL → exit 1 with "assign reviewer via UI first" guidance; (4) read reviewer row via `SELECT githubLogin FROM user WHERE id = ?`; refuse if `githubLogin` NULL → exit 1; (5) `buildRatingActionYaml({ ... })` builds the YAML string from `ScaffoldInput`; `mkdir -p drafts/ratings/` + `writeFile`.
- **Phase-22 lean — PROPOSED dimension fully populated, OTHER 4 placeholder**:
  - PROPOSED dimension's value parsed via `parseProposedValue(dimension, raw)`: `difficulty` accepts `S/A/B/C/D/E` case-insensitive; `saturation` accepts numeric 0-100; `urgency`/`value`/`industry_call` accept integer 0-5 (via `parseInt` so `3.5` truncates to `3`).
  - On parse failure: falls back to placeholder value (`S` / `0` / `0`) with TODO rationale that retains the submitter's original prose ("Original submitter rationale:\n<rationale>").
  - OTHER 4 dimensions: filled with placeholder grade/value/stars + `confidence: 0.7` + rationale = `"TODO: copy unchanged from prior action."` Curator MUST edit before commit.
  - `prior_action`: `"TODO-set-to-prior-action-filename"` placeholder string. `signals_considered`: one TODO entry. `watchlist: false`. `methodology_version: "1.0.0"`.
- **YAML header comment block** documents: challenge id + scaffold timestamp + reviewer login + problem slug + 4-item REVIEW-REQUIRED checklist + target content path + return-to-UI step. Curator catches placeholders during review-edit pass.
- **`--dry-run` flag**: prints scaffolded YAML to stdout instead of writing. Curator inspection pass before execution. `--help` / `-h` prints usage + exit-code matrix + ADR-0014 D-D pointer.
- **Pure-function YAML scaffolder** factored to `lib/rating-challenges/scaffold.ts` (D-9 deferred originally said inline; **promoted** because Phase-22 D-7 ships ~3-5 vitest tests for the scaffolder which would conflict with the script's top-level-await CLI shell). The factoring follows Phase-5 precedent (`lib/curate/entry-draft.ts` backing `scripts/extract-leaderboard.ts`).
- **+11 new vitest tests at `lib/rating-challenges/scaffold.test.ts`** covering `parseProposedValue` (4 tests across difficulty enum / saturation range / stars range / malformed input) + `buildRatingActionYaml` (6 tests across PROPOSED-difficulty + PROPOSED-saturation + 3 PROPOSED-stars-dimensions via `it.each` + malformed-fallback + header-comment-content). Each test parses YAML output through `RatingActionSchema.parse()` to assert structural validity against the live schema (Phase-0 invariant). **First phase since Phase 19 to add tests** (Phase 20 + 21 both shipped zero). Test count: **575 / 55 files** (was 564 / 54 at Phase 21 close); +11 tests / +1 file delta.
- **Script alias** added to `package.json#scripts`: `"emit-challenge-action": "tsx scripts/emit-challenge-action.ts"`. Mirrors existing operational-script patterns (`backfill-exif-strip` / `cleanup-orphan-blobs` / `extract-leaderboard` / etc.).
- **Server-only**: depends on `TURSO_DATABASE_URL` / `TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN` (Q55) at runtime. No client bundle impact; **First Load JS shared chunk UNCHANGED at 103 kB** per ADR-0018 D-F invariant extension. **Middleware bundle UNCHANGED at 160 kB**.
- **No new ADR Phase 22**. ADR-0014 D-D already names the helper; Phase 22 ships the realization of an existing ADR D-clause. ADR count stays at **19**. **Fourth phase since Phase 3 to close without new ADR** (Phase 18 + 20 + 21 + 22; **third consecutive** = first three-phase no-new-ADR streak).
- Smoke gates: `pnpm typecheck` clean; `pnpm lint` clean; `pnpm test` → **575/575 across 55 vitest files**; `pnpm emit-challenge-action --help` confirms script registers and arg parsing works; `pnpm audit-content` → 0 errors / 6 warnings (Q32 baseline unchanged).
- Files added: `scripts/emit-challenge-action.ts`; `lib/rating-challenges/scaffold.ts`; `lib/rating-challenges/scaffold.test.ts`. Files modified: `package.json` (script alias). 1 new operational script + 1 new lib module + 1 new test file; 9 total `scripts/` files (was 8).

- **Recommended thread: `scripts/emit-challenge-action.ts`** (D-1). **ADR-0014 D-D explicitly named** the operational helper at line 220: *"CLI helper `pnpm emit-challenge-action <id>` reading the challenge from the DB + scaffolding the YAML at `content/problems/<slug>/ratings/<date>-<slug>.yaml`. Mirrors Phase-5's `extract-leaderboard` shape. Eliminates the 'write the YAML by hand' friction while preserving the manual commit step. Possible Q59 candidate."* **Carried Phase-12 through Phase-21 = 10 phases** — first double-digit phase carryover for any architectural candidate closed. Anticipated phase shape: **5 units** (matches Phase 20 + 21); **no new ADR** (ADR-0014 D-D already names the helper; implementation is realization of existing D-clause — same pattern as Phase-20 ADR-0019 D-E backfill); **fourth phase since Phase 3 to close without new ADR** anticipated (Phase 18 + 20 + 21 + 22; **third consecutive** = first three-phase no-new-ADR streak).

Migration count stays at **6**; **sixth consecutive 0-migration phase anticipated** (Phase 17 + 18 + 19 + 20 + 21 + 22; 9 of 13 phases since DB landed are 0-migration: Phase 10 + 13 + 14 + 17 + 18 + 19 + 20 + 21 + 22). §5.7 trigger (a) FIRED Unit 9.6 carried; trigger (b) cold; re-evaluation procedural-only.
- **CLI invocation shape anticipated** (D-3): `pnpm emit-challenge-action <challenge-id> [--dry-run]`. Output path: `drafts/ratings/<challenge-id>.yaml` (gitignored per Phase-0 `.gitignore` `drafts/` pattern; mirrors Phase-5 `extract-leaderboard` drafts-first precedent). Curator reviews + edits + moves the file to `content/problems/<problemSlug>/ratings/<date>-<slug>.yaml` + commits manually — **preserves ADR-0014 D-D "manual commit step" requirement and ADR-0005 pre-commit-hook immutability protection** (both fire only on actual `content/` commit; gitignored draft invisible to both).
- **Scaffolded YAML pipeline anticipated** (D-4): (1) read `ratingChallenge` row from DB; (2) refuse if status terminal (`accepted` / `rejected` / `withdrawn`); (3) refuse if `reviewerId` NULL with "assign reviewer via UI first" instruction; (4) read reviewer's `users.githubLogin` for `curator` field per ADR-0012 D-E; (5) generate YAML matching `RatingActionSchema`. **Phase-22 lean**: pre-fill the 1 PROPOSED dimension fully from challenge data (`grade` / `value` / `stars` per dimension type) + `confidence: 0.7` placeholder + `rationale` from challenge.rationale; leave the OTHER 4 dimensions as placeholder values with TODO comments. Curator MUST edit placeholders before committing (ADR-0005 + Velite + Zod schema validation catch placeholders at commit via `pnpm validate-content` pre-commit gate). **Phase 23+ enhancement** (deferred): read most recent prior rating-action YAML for the problem (via Velite `#site/content` or filesystem) + auto-copy 4 unchanged dimensions + auto-set `prior_action` filename. ~1 additional unit when promoted.
- **Dimension-value parsing anticipated** (D-5): per `RatingActionSchema` — `difficulty.grade` = string enum passthrough; `saturation.value` = `Number(proposedValue)` with NaN-fallback; `urgency` / `value` / `industry_call` `stars` = `parseInt(proposedValue)` with range-check fallback. `ratingChallenge.proposedValue` is TEXT (per Phase-11 Unit 11.0 D-3 + D-6); Phase-22 parsing is best-effort with placeholder fallback when malformed.
- **Pipeline placement anticipated** (D-6): stand-alone CLI script at `scripts/emit-challenge-action.ts`. NOT a server action / NOT an API route / NOT a cron job. Manual curator invocation only — **continues Phase-20 + Phase-21 operational-script-keystone pattern; third consecutive operational-script-keystone phase**. Mirrors Phase-5 `extract-leaderboard` drafts-first shape per ADR-0014 D-D wording.
- **Test surface anticipated** (D-7): **light vitest coverage** (~3-5 tests in Unit 22.1) for the YAML scaffolding helper. Pure-function shape (no FS / DB / network in the scaffolder; high test value at low cost). **Differs from Phase 20 + 21 zero-new-test posture** because the YAML generator is pure-function-testable. Anticipated test count delta: **+3 to +5 tests** → 567-569 / 54-55 vitest files at Phase 22 close. **First phase since Phase 19 to add tests** (Phase 20 + 21 both shipped zero).
- **No new ADR Phase 22** (D-8). ADR-0014 D-D already names the helper; Phase 22 ships the realization of an existing ADR D-clause. **Fourth phase since Phase 3 to close without new ADR** (Phase 18 + 20 + 21 + 22 anticipated; **third consecutive** = first three-phase no-new-ADR streak). ADR count stays at **19**.

#### Unit 21.4 — Phase 21 acceptance gate (orphan-blob cleanup closure; **19 ADRs unchanged**; tenth consecutive well-scoped phase; second consecutive operational-script-keystone phase)

- Fifth and final Phase-21 unit; docs-only. Closes Phase 21 (**twelfth NON-§13 phase**; orphan-blob cleanup realized via `scripts/cleanup-orphan-blobs.ts`). Mirrors Phase-12 through Phase-20 acceptance-gate patterns. **§13 ledger CLOSED at Unit 9.9** (carried unchanged through Phases 10-21).
- **Phase 21 ledger**: **5 units shipped** (21.0 prep → 21.4 gate). **0 deferrals**; **0 scope drift**. **Tenth consecutive well-scoped phase**. **Smallest phase shape tied with Phase 20** (5 units).
- **Phase-21 closure summary**: Phase-16 + Phase-20 carryover (orphan-blob accumulation) **CLOSED** in Unit 21.1 via `scripts/cleanup-orphan-blobs.ts`. Per-run 5-phase pipeline reconciles DB-as-source-of-truth (`users.imageOverride` rows with non-null URLs) against Vercel Blob storage enumeration (paginated `list({ prefix: "avatars/" })`), detects orphans, and deletes them with per-blob try/catch + `--dry-run` safety. **Third phase since Phase 3 to close without new ADR** (Phase 18 + 20 + 21); **second consecutive operational-script-keystone phase** (Phase 20 + 21). Establishes the operational-script-keystone pattern as **recurring** (not a one-off Phase-20 anomaly).
- **Smoke gates (final)**: `pnpm typecheck` clean; `pnpm test` → **564/564 across 54 vitest files** (0 / 0 Δ from Phase 20; **second consecutive zero-new-test phase**); `pnpm build` → ~659 prerendered pages + 7 dynamic page+API routes UNCHANGED; **First Load JS shared chunk = 103 kB UNCHANGED** end-to-end through every Phase 9-21 unit; **Middleware bundle = 160 kB UNCHANGED** since Phase 12; `/profile` = 117 kB UNCHANGED; `/u/{handle}` = 108 kB UNCHANGED; `pnpm audit-content` → 0 errors / 6 warnings (Q32 baseline unchanged); `pnpm lint` clean.
